title: Simultaneous dual detection of total chlorine in oil matrices via quantum dot-based LSPR
abstract: Chlorine compounds including inorganic chloride ions and organic chlorine species are major precursors in the formation of 3-monochloropropane-1,2-diol (3-MCPD) during edible oil refining, raising significant food-safety concerns.

title: Optimizing carbon-nanotube-driven polyhedral Cu 2 Mn 3 O 8 structures for hybrid supercapacitors: unveiling strategies for enhanced electrochemical efficiency
abstract: Global energy demands have compelled researchers to put significant efforts on designing advanced nanostructured electrode materials that are capable to deliver both energy and power output simultaneously, mediated by optimized diffusion dynamics.

title: Indole and triazole containing ferrocene-based two-terminal resistive memory devices
abstract: Tuning the electronic structure modulates resistive switching behavior, and high-performance molecular memory in ferrocene-based devices.

title: Magneto-optical Raman effect of WS 2 tuned by a plasmonic gold nanograting
abstract: The magneto-optical properties of monolayer WS 2 were modulated via a plasmonic Au nanograting, and systematically investigated utilizing polarized-Raman spectroscopy.

title: Solution-processed high- κ SrTiO 3 with high breakdown field for gate dielectrics
abstract: With the optimization of the film, strontium titanate films with a maximum breakdown field strength of 11.04 MV cm −1 were obtained.

title: Unravelling solid-state microstructures from oligomers to polymers of an isoindigo-based π-conjugated system
abstract: This study synthesized isoindigo-based oligomers with varying backbone and side-chain lengths to explore their effects on solid-state microstructures and properties by tuning the balance between π–π interactions and side-chain interactions.

title: 1D all-inorganic metal halide Rb 2 AgX 3 (X = Cl, Br): Pb-free scintillator materials for ionising radiation detection
abstract: Rb-based metal halide materials possess large attenuation coefficients and bright luminescence making them suitable as scintillators for X-ray detection.

title: Synergistic diameter control and post-treatment engineering of electrospun PVDF nanofibers toward β-phase enrichment and enhanced piezoelectric response
abstract: Diameter-controlled PVDF nanofibers were prepared via molecular-weight blending, then synergistically annealed and grid-corona poled to promote β-phase enrichment and dipole alignment, delivering higher piezoelectric output than PVDF films.

title: Interface-engineered p-CsCu 2 I 3 /n-Ga 2 O 3 nanowire heterojunctions for self-powered ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: An all-inorganic p-CsCu 2 I 3 /n-Ga 2 O 3 NW self-powered UV photodetector is developed. Using a CuI interlayer for interface engineering, the device achieves UVA–UVC response with high responsivity (120.3 mA W −1 ) and detectivity (1.5 × 10 12 Jones).

title: Substitution-position-dependent optoelectronic properties of methylthiolated anthracenes
abstract: The methylthio-substitution at the 2,6-positions of anthracene facilitates tighter molecular packing and more efficient charge transport compared to the 9,10- and 2,6,9,10-substituted counterparts.

title: Green deep-eutectic synthesis of multinary quantum dots for luminescent polymeric composites and LED applications
abstract: Hydrophobic deep eutectic solvents are used to synthesise a broad range of luminescent multinary QDs based on silver indium sulfide, copper indium sulfide, and quaternary alloys with zinc sulfide, with applications in LED colour conversion layers.

title: Planar inverted cone antenna based on silver nanowire network with enhanced interfacial adhesion after surface functionalization using (3-glycidyloxypropyl)trimethoxysilane
abstract: The design and fabrication of planar inverted cone antennas (PICA) based on transparent AgNW coatings with improved interfacial adhesion is reported, covering both 2.4 GHz and 5.0 GHz Wi-Fi microwave bands.

title: Planar chiral paracyclophanes: emerging scaffolds for circularly polarized OLEDs
abstract: Development in circularly polarized organic light-emitting diodes (CP-OLEDs).

title: Coordination-induced p-type selectivity and enhanced hole mobility in an ambipolar organic semiconductor via electron passivation
abstract: By reacting with the Lewis acid PtCl 4 , an ambipolar organic semiconductor can be transformed into a unipolar p-type semiconductor, resulting in a near-zero hole trap-state density and a two-orders-of-magnitude enhancement in hole mobility.

title: A new Te/Ag planar heterojunction device based on screen printing for boosting photothermoelectric performance and heat-source-free non-contact sensing
abstract: The TS film’s heat-source-free, non-contact sensing capability enables the detection of human behavior and the recognition of Morse code by utilizing temperature fluctuations induced by wind.

title: NiOOH-mediated electron injection into Ti 3 C 2 F x to weaken Ti–H bonds for accelerated photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: NiOOH–Ti 3 C 2 F x with weakened Ti–H ads bonds improves the H 2 -evolution rate due to electron transfer from NiOOH to Ti 3 C 2 F x to increase the antibonding orbital.

title: Synthesis of metal nitride and metal oxide nanoparticles by atmospheric-pressure spark ablation
abstract: Spark ablation in pure N 2 produces material-dependent nanoparticles: Ti and Al form nitride/oxide mixtures, Mg only oxides, and Pd metallic NPs.

title: Conformation-dependent room-temperature phosphorescence in purely organic systems
abstract: This review highlights the significant influence of molecular conformation on RTP effects, including the conformation-dependent RTP phenomenon, precise control of molecular conformation and dynamic regulation of molecular conformation.

title: Enhancing the coercivity of ferrimagnetic NiCo 2 O 4 films via vicinal substrate growth
abstract: Vicinal substrates drive step-flow growth in NiCo 2 O 4 , suppressing surface Ni segregation and yielding a uniform magnetic film with strongly enhanced coercivity and robust AHE response.

title: High-temperature superconductivity in Li 2 YH 6 driven by Li doping and a specific hydrogen-cage structure under high pressures
abstract: A new high- T c hydrogen-based superconductor, Li 2 YH 6 ( T c ∼ 227 K at 150 GPa), was discovered to possess an I 4/ mmm structure within the pressure range of 130–180 GPa.

title: Carbazole-Based Host Materials for Red to Near–Infrared OLEDs: Molecular Design Strategies and Structure–Property Relationships
abstract: Achieving high efficiency and long-term stability in red-to-near-infrared (NIR) organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs) remains challenging due to narrow bandgaps, severe exciton quenching, and charge imbalance. Designing a suitable host is...

title: Disorder stabilisation of high pressure Mn 2− x Co x ScSbO 6 double perovskites with complex magnetic properties
abstract: Surprisingly, Mn 2− x Co x ScSbO 6 ( x = 0.5, 1 and 1.5) form double perovskites (DPv) at 6 GPa pressure whereas x = 0 and 2 end members adopt a different (NTO) structure, evidencing disorder (configurational entropy) stabilisation of the DPv phase.

title: Photo-dielectric and electric field effect on the structural and phonon properties of calcium-substituted BaTiO 3
abstract: The photo-dielectric effect with electric field-driven structural and phonon changes is demonstrated for BCTO. A remarkable correlation between the structural and dielectric properties is established.

title: The interface effect of MOF-derived Pr 6 O 11 /SnO 2 composite materials enhances oxygen vacancies for achieving highly sensitive detection of n -butanol gas
abstract: The Pr 6 O 11 /SnO 2 composite material with a heterogeneous structure and abundant oxygen vacancies enhances the gas sensing performance of n -butanol.

title: Prediction of aqueous stable lead-free hybrid halide perovskites for efficient solar water splitting using machine learning and molecular dynamics
abstract: By combining ML with DFT and AIMD insights, this study uncovers 21 potential lead-free HOIPs with suitable band gaps and band alignment for solar water splitting, with a case study focused on FmSnI 2 Br as a promising candidate.

title: A Te microwire/2D organic–inorganic hybrid perovskite heterojunction self-powered polarization photodetector for imaging and communication encoding
abstract: A 2D (EA) 2 (MA) 2 Pb 3 Cl 10 /Te self-powered photodetector can be used in applications of polarization imaging and dual-wavelength encrypted communications in the 280–1006 nm range, showing the highest peak responsivity in the solar-blind UV region.

title: Mn 2 O 3 –Co 3 O 4 nanocomposites as advanced electrode materials: achieving high specific capacitance and excellent cycling stability
abstract: Mn 2 O 3 particles were synthesized via a hydrothermal process and subsequently coated with Co 3 O 4 to develop Mn 2 O 3 –Co 3 O 4 composite particles as a supercapacitor electrode material.

title: One-step graphene exfoliation and biofunctionalization using phospholipids for sustainable bioelectronics
abstract: One-step bio-fingerprinting of graphene with phospholipids unlocks a new frontier in unraveling interfacial science for high-performance electrical biohybrid 2D materials.

title: Non-adiabatic molecular dynamics study of excited states in thermally stimulated delayed phosphorescent gold( iii ) complexes
abstract: Pyrazine-based gold( iii ) complexes demonstrate an emission mechanism involving higher triplet states that violates Kasha's rule.

title: 3D-printed mesoporous-silica-confined perovskite quantum dot micro-optics for geometry-tailored color conversion in micro-LED displays
abstract: High-performance 3D CCLs engineered via DLP printing achieve 64.6% EQE and 72.4% IQE. This 3D approach replaces uncontrolled thin films with geometry-tailored architectures, optimizing light extraction in micro-LEDs.

title: Defect-tuned conduction in ultrathin MoTe 2 field-effect transistors
abstract: Temperature- and pressure-dependent transport, together with DFT simulations, reveal how Schottky contacts, vacancies, and surface adsorbates jointly govern charge transport in ultrathin MoTe 2 field effect transistors.

title: An all-printed flexible field effect transistor based on tellurene nanosheets with graphene electrodes
abstract: A full printing strategy was employed to prepare a flexible tellurene-based transistor with an Al 2 O 3 gate dielectric, enabling the application of artificial synapses through both electrical and optical stimuli.

title: Ultralow-threshold UV/solar-pumping waveguide amplifier based on lanthanide-rich metal halide perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: Yb 3+ /Er 3+ -rich (12.2%) CsPbCl 3 NCs emitted at 1540 nm efficiently with an optimal PLQY of 21.6%. The fabrication of a polymer-based waveguide amplifier achieved a highly efficient gain of 4.8 dB through pumping by simulated sunlight.

title: Multi-stimuli responsive circularly polarized luminescence in manganese( ii ) halide hybrids
abstract: Mn( ii )-based chiral hybrids exhibit reversible multi-stimuli CPL to methanol, water, and temperature. Solvent/thermal emission switching enables up to fivefold | g lum | modulation, revealing control of STE emission and MnBr 4 geometry.

title: Hydrogen spillover-driven selective hydrogenation of nitrobenzoic acid to aminobenzoic acid over a ZIF-8/Cu(OH) 2 heterostructure
abstract: Heterostructured ZIF-8/Cu(OH) 2 /CF enables >99% conversion, 95% FE and ~98% selectivity in PNBA electrocatalytic hydrogenation to PABA via synergistic hydrogen spillover.

title: Study of strain engineered MoS 2 and its application in lactic acid biosensors
abstract: The lactic acid biosensor enhances its detection response to lactic acid concentrations ranging from 0 to 1 µM under 0.29% tensile strain, with sensitivity nearly five times higher than in the unstressed state.

title: Unveiling Na-substitution effects on charge transport in Pr 0.8 K 0.2− x Na x MnO 3 using experimental analysis and kernel density estimation
abstract: The present work reports on the effects of substituting potassium with monovalent sodium on the electrical properties of Pr 0.8 K 0.2− x Na x MnO 3 ( x = 0.00, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15 and 0.2) manganites.

title: Enhanced piezoelectric performance of PAN nanofibers incorporated with a lead-free high entropy perovskite oxide for energy harvesting, sensing and IoT-based smart alert systems for security monitoring
abstract: Piezoelectric nanogenerators (PENGs) provide a practical and portable alternative to traditional energy harvesting techniques.

title: Highly tough all-solid-state ionic conductors based on supramolecular deep eutectic polymers for stretchable wearable sensors
abstract: A highly tough supramolecular deep eutectic polymer (ARCDEP) integrates high strength, ultra-stretchability, and ionic conductivity, enabling flexible electroluminescent devices and strain sensors for human motion monitoring.

title: Iron based efficient room temperature thermoelectric materials: a DFT study on FeXSn (X = Nb, Ta) half-Heusler alloys
abstract: First-principles calculations show that FeNbSn and FeTaSn are stable half-Heusler alloys with half-metallicity, ferromagnetism, and promising thermoelectric performance, supported by low κ L obtained from Slack and AIMD methods.

title: Defect passivation and phase regulation of quasi-2D perovskites for efficient blue light-emitting diodes
abstract: Efficient blue quasi-2D perovskite light-emitting diodes were developed by introducing disodium pyrophosphate as an additive into the precursor solution to passivate uncoordinated Pb 2+ and regulate the phase distribution.

title: A one-step flash microwave synthesis of complex high dielectric nanoperovskites applied to microwave gas sensing of ammonia
abstract: Microwave synthesis of high dielectric nanoperovskite (BT, ST and BST) used as sensitive material for microwave gas sensing of ammonia.

title: Reversible polymorph-to-amorphous phase transformation based on an aggregation-dependent thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitter
abstract: A novel mechanochromic luminescence (MCL)-active thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF) material DMAC-PYZ, exhibiting reversible polymorph-to-amorphous phase transition, has been successfully demonstrated for information encryption.

title: Leveraging donor–acceptor mutual dilution to finely tune vertical phase separation for high-performance semitransparent organic solar cells
abstract: The mutually diluted opaque device M-PCE10-2F/M-Y6 achieves a PCE of 14.98%. Meanwhile, the mutually diluted semi-transparent device attains the most balanced PCE of 11.32% with an AVT of 43.25%, yielding the highest LUE of 4.90%.

title: Room-temperature aqueous ZnAgSe alloyed quantum dots: Zn-assisted defect passivation for bright NIR emission and biointerface-ready colloids
abstract: Room-temperature aqueous ZnAgSe alloyed quantum dots use Zn-assisted defect passivation to deliver bright, stable NIR emission and tunable surface charge, yielding biointerface-ready colloids for imaging and sensing.

title: Stability of organic field-effect transistors: from degradation mechanisms to synergistic stabilization
abstract: A comprehensive categorization of factors limiting OFET stability is presented, classifying degradation into two internal and five external pathways, with a structured overview of stabilization strategies across six key aspects.

title: Efficient carbon dot-based fluorescence-afterglow dual-mode white-light materials via surface modification
abstract: Efficient carbon dot-based fluorescence–phosphorescence dual-mode white-light materials were fabricated via surface modification, which provides broad prospects for the application of carbon dots in the field of optoelectronic devices.

title: Improving SERS sensitivity via hot spots and control of molecular orientation
abstract: Surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) is a high-throughput, surface-sensitive and label-free analytical technique that provides the molecular fingerprints of bio/chemical analytes.

title: Anti-naphthalene bisbenzimidazole columnar mesogens as interfacial modifiers in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Introducing an interfacial layer is an effective strategy to enhance both the performance and stability of perovskite solar cells by improving charge transport, suppressing recombination, and optimizing energy level alignment.

title: Dynamic phosphorescent gel with dual supramolecular interactions for visualization of moisture monitoring
abstract: Construction of a dynamic soft material with efficient RTP and multiple stimuli responsiveness via dual non-covalent interactions for visualization of moisture monitoring.

title: Multidimensional regulation of interfacial microenvironment by alkali metal ions for selective photothermal CO 2 conversion
abstract: Alkali ions tune interface microenvironments for photothermal CO 2 conversion. A unified 3D framework elucidates cross-scale regulation across C , metal, semiconductor & insulator interfaces, enabling efficient conversion to multi-carbon products.

title: Structural phase transition and cooperative luminescence in K 3 Yb(PO 4 ) 2 :Eu 3+ for multimodal down-shifting and up-converting luminescence thermometry
abstract: In this work phase transition based thermometer on K 3 Yb(PO 4 ) 2 :Eu 3+ is described.

title: Minimizing barriers to efficient mechanoluminescence via polymer matrix integration
abstract: Mechanoluminescent solids are emerging as prime candidates for next-generation industrial and biomedical technologies, enabling effective light-based stress sensing without external power sources.

title: 16.55% power conversion efficiency achieved in PM6:Y6 organic solar cells based on salicylic acid-modified ZnO
abstract: The device achieved improved power conversion efficiency via modified ZnO cathode interfacial layer with salicylic acid.

title: Research progress of single crystal perovskite growth
abstract: Single crystals are attracting increased interest in photovoltaics research due to their superior material properties.

title: Fabrication of three-dimensional flower-ball heterostructures MoS 2 /LDHs based on an interface coupling strategy and their application in high-performance NO x gas sensing
abstract: 3D flower-ball MoS 2 /LDH heterostructures were prepared through the in situ growth of LDHs on MoS 2 by a combined hydrothermal synthesis method. The response of MoS 2 /LDHs to 100 ppm NO x at room temperature is 28.31, and the response time is 2.13 s.

title: Copper sulfide based self-powered photoelectrochemical detectors for optical communication in seawater
abstract: Uniform CuS thin films were fabricated on Cu foil via one-step electrodeposition. The PEC performance was characterized, enabling the construction of a UOC system with demonstrated signal transmission in real seawater.

title: Sequential Li-intercalation exfoliation enables high-purity NbSe 2 with enhanced electrical and thermal performance
abstract: Sequential Li-intercalation exfoliation removes oxide-rich residues and disordered fragments, producing high-purity NbSe 2 nanosheets with improved crystallinity, reduced resistance, and rapid, uniform Joule-heating performance.

title: Picric acid sensing by carbon nanodots: theoretical validation of selectivity
abstract: Oxidative pathway of quenching of the luminescence of carbon nanodots (PD-CNDs) in the presence of picric acid (PA).

title: Rapid-photo-responsive photoluminescence of spiropyran-encapsulated cage-like zeolitic imidazolate frameworks via a dynamic energy transfer process
abstract: By co-encapsulating AF and SP within the cages of ZIFs (ZIF-11 and rho-ZMOF), solid-state photo-responsive dynamic dual-emission materials are successfully constructed.

title: Dry ice carbonation approach for the synthesis of calcium carbonate-based magnetic composites
abstract: Dry ice-mediated solid-state and aqueous routes for the synthesis of multifunctional magnetic Fe 3 O 4 @PSS@CaCO 3 composites that integrate biocompatibility, structural tunability and magnetic responsiveness.

title: Biomass-derived white-light-emitting carbon dots for white LEDs
abstract: We demonstrate a facile and sustainable one-pot solvothermal synthesis of white-emitting carbon dots derived from biomass and their application in the fabrication of a white light-emitting diode.

title: Smart wearable systems for precision wound monitoring and therapy: a categorical review across wound types
abstract: Wearable devices in healthcare have garnered significant attention in recent years due to their flexibility, high biocompatibility, and small size, which enable them to predict and even modulate disease outcomes.

title: Hydrogel infusion additive manufacturing of customized hierarchical porous cobalt/carbon composites for electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Hierarchical porous Co/C composites were fabricated by hydrogel-infusion additive manufacturing and microwave-plasma reduction. The 3D structure and magnetic components enhanced impedance matching and attenuation, delivering excellent EMW absorption.

title: Stretchable, self-healable, and adhesive ionogels formed via dual association of complex copolymers
abstract: We report a material system capable of forming highly versatile ionogels that exhibit freestanding capability, mechanical robustness, stretchability, adhesiveness, and self-healability through the dual association of two types of complex copolymers.

title: Improved performance of organic single-crystal thin-film transistors via contact doping with a high electron-affinity dopant
abstract: NDP-9 contact doping with a high electron affinity reduces the Schottky barrier and trap density, enabling high-mobility organic single-crystal transistors with low subthreshold swing and long-term bias-stress stability.

title: Design and Synergistic Effects of Ionic Liquid-MoS₂ Composite Hydrogels for Enhanced Conductivity and Adhesion
abstract: Hydrogels are still limited by insufficient mechanical strength and poor electrical conductivity. To address these challenges, it is essential to develop versatile hydrogels that integrate strong adhesion, high conductivity, and...

title: Development of a fluorometric gamma dosimeter based on a BODIPY-LMG dye-loaded superporous polymer
abstract: A solid supported fluorometric gamma dosimeter is developed using a BODIPY-LMG dye-loaded superporous polymer. The dosimeter is easy to use, highly sensitive and useful for accurate measurement of absorbed gamma doses in blood irradiation.

title: Theoretical analysis of spin–orbit coupling-weakened superconductivity in Bi-rich Ba 2 Bi 3 compounds
abstract: The electron–phonon coupling constant is found to decrease significantly from 1.32 to 0.88 (∼33%) upon inclusion of SOC for the Ba 2 Bi 3 superconductor.

title: The size effect of precursor particles in W 6+ -doped CaBi 2 Nb 2 O 9 high-temperature piezoelectric ceramics
abstract: Precursor particle-size reduction in dopant modified CBN-based piezoceramics enhances compositional homogeneity, enabling tuning of intrinsic crystal structure and vacancy defects, leading to significant enhancement of electrical properties.

title: Scientific advancements in antimony selenosulfide solar cells
abstract: Antimony selenosulfide [Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 ] is a scientifically interesting, and technologically promising photovoltaic (PV) material for the next generation of solar cells.

title: Regioselective synthesis of multifunctional dibenzo-heterocyclic dihydrophenazine derivatives: tunable excited-state structural relaxation and efficient red OLED host materials
abstract: A selective, efficient intramolecular amination strategy yields dihydrophenazine derivatives with distinct heteroatom doping. These structures modulate excited-state dynamics, making them promising for high-performance OLEDs.

title: Room temperature strategy for high-performance Pr:IZO/Al 2 O 3 thin-film transistors: Enhanced stability of light-induced bias stress and applications in flexible devices
abstract: This study focuses on the development of high-performance Pr:IZO/Al 2 O 3 bilayer metal-oxide thin-film transistors (MOTFTs) prepared at room temperature, addressing critical challenges in achieving high mobility, stability, and adaptability for flexible...

title: Sequential electrodeposition of layered Co 3 O 4 /rGO/PANI ternary nanocomposite electrodes for high performance supercapacitors
abstract: A layered Co 3 O 4 /rGO/PANI ternary nanocomposite was successfully synthesized via sequential hydrothermal and electrodeposition on a steel substrate.

title: Molecular redesign unlocks 99.89% light-driven control of azobiphenyl liquid crystals
abstract: Near-perfect photo-switching (99.89% E → Z ) in ACEt-5 enables precise light control of LC phases. Ultra‑slow thermal back-relaxation (160 min) enables optical memory in E7. Terminal groups tune mesophases: cyano → N*; esters → N*/SmA*/SmC* parity-dependent.

title: Energy Transfer Mediated Rabi Splitting in Ag@CsPbCl3 Nanohybrids
abstract: We report a strong exciton (semiconductor) -plasmon (metal) coupling in colloidal Ag@CsPbCl 3 nanohybrids, leading to the emergence of new hybrid states, known as plexcitons. Here, we systematically tuned the...

title: A selenium-substituted core and a synergistic regioisomer-free monomethylated terminal enable methylated acceptors with high efficiency and low energy loss in binary organic solar cells
abstract: Regioisomer-free monomethylated end groups and a selenium-substituted core are employed, two fused-ring small molecular acceptors (FR-SMA) are synthesized. BIT-M-γ-based binary devices exhibit a higher PCE among methylated A–D–A-type FR-SMAs.

title: Blatter diradicals with planar chirality: investigation of through-space spin–spin coupling in regioisomeric derivatives of [2.2]paracyclophane
abstract: Three regioisomeric benzo[ e ][1,2,4]triazinyl diradicals are investigated to assess topological through-space spin coupling ability of the planarly prochiral [2.2]paracyclophane scaffold.

title: Mechanically programmable and stretchable Janus elastomer with bidirectional visible-infrared radiative modes for thermal regulation and adaptive camouflage
abstract: A Janus film enables bidirectional emissivity switching under single-axis strain: the liquid-metal side enhances reflection, while the Au layer reduces it due to cracking, offering new solutions for adaptive thermal management.

title: Composition-driven optimization of amorphous Ge 1− x Se x for high-performance OTS selectors
abstract: First-principles simulations reveal optimal stability in Ge 1− x Se x ( x = 0.4 and 0.6) via Ge–Se bonding. The band gap increases linearly (0.58–0.81 eV) with Se content, enabling threshold voltage tuning and enhanced endurance for OTS selectors.

title: Interpretable machine learning screening and analysis of pressure sensitivity and high-pressure emission line shifts in lanthanide- and transition-metal-doped phosphors
abstract: An interpretable hierarchical machine learning framework is proposed to efficiently screen and analyse the absolute pressure sensitivity of lanthanide- and transition-metal-doped phosphors.

title: The effect of enhanced π–π interactions and tail branching on azobenzene surfactant self-assembly
abstract: Azobenzene-based surfactants are known for their photo-responsive aggregation behaviour, which is strongly influenced by molecular design.

title: Hybrid Au nanodisk–nanohole photonic crystals fabricated by laser interference lithography for large-scale, reproducible-performance, low-power and fast-sensing SERS platforms
abstract: This study reports an easy procedure to fabricate high and reproducible-performance SERS platforms (on >2 cm 2 scales) from hybrid Au nanodisk–nanohole arrays produced via single-beam laser interference lithography.

title: Prediction of the elastic properties and electrical resistance of halide glass based on interpretable machine learning
abstract: This paper employs regression models based on machine learning to predict the elastic properties and electrical resistance of halide glass, unveiling the composition–property map and enabling swift optimization and rapid iteration.

title: Donor-modified tetradentate platinum( ii ) complexes for red OLEDs
abstract: Donor-modified tetradentate Pt( ii ) complexes exhibit red electroluminescence from 616 to 648 nm with high external quantum efficiencies up to 20.1%, demonstrating great potential for OLED applications.

title: Signal modulation facilitated by a single organic electrochemical transistor
abstract: Single organic electrochemical transistors, characterized by unique “V”-shaped transfer characteristics, enable versatile signal modulation like frequency doubling, and binary modulation, significantly reducing circuit size and power consumption.

title: Investigating the energy level influence on the stability of phototransistor memory using the poly(siloxane-imide)/perovskite floating gate
abstract: Perovskite (PVSK) materials have emerged as promising photo-responsive elements in memory devices due to their strong light absorption, simple structure, and non-contact optical programmability.

title: Dually switchable lasing emission in a photonic crystal cavity with temperature and solvent responsiveness
abstract: A dually switchable laser is demonstrated by tuning the stopband of responsive photonic crystals to overlap with the photoluminescence of RhB doped polymer films. This study is a perspective for constructing switchable organic solid-state lasers.

title: Novel [BO 2 ] superhalogen module-enabled deep-ultraviolet nonlinear optical material: rational design for tailored birefringence and phase matching
abstract: A novel design strategy utilizing covalent superhalogen [BO 2 ] to achieve deep-ultraviolet phase matching is described.

title: Bypassing sulfides: comparing the morphology and performance of solution processed Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 films prepared via two selenide molecular precursor routes
abstract: Solution-processed Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 thin-film photovoltaics are prepared via two selenium-based molecular precursor routes. STEM-EDX and SAED are used to analyze film morphology and identify secondary phases. A champion performance of 11.2% is reported.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance of Bi 2 Te 2.7 Se 0.3 by constructing multiple interfaces through the introduction of heterojunction compounds
abstract: Multilayer MnS/MoS 2 was incorporated into the Bi 2 Te 2.7 Se 0.3 matrix to enhance the interfacial structure, thereby improving the thermoelectric performance.

title: Molecular precursor-directed growth of nanostructured SnS 2 for memristive and neuromorphic electronics
abstract: A dithiophosphate single-source precursor enables one-step solvothermal SnS 2 growth, bridging molecular design to neuromorphic device functionality.

title: A deep blue TADF dendron for efficient energy transfer in green and red iridium light emitting dendrimers
abstract: Phosphorescent dendrimers with thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF) dendrons were synthesised. OLEDs fabricated with the green and red emitters achieved EQEs of 15.8% and 13.9%, respectively.

title: Optimized thermoelectric properties of p-type PbTe 0.88 Ag 0.12 through metallic transition and investigation of its time- and temperature-dependent oxidation process
abstract: Silver doping in PbTe 0.88 Ag 0.12 improves stability, reducing oxidation compared to pure PbTe, while maintaining high thermoelectric performance.

title: Enhancing efficiency and tuning the emission band via energy transfer of Cr 3+ , Ce 3+ co-doped CaLu 2 Mg 2 Si 3 O 12 phosphors
abstract: Efficient Ce 3+ → Cr 3+ energy transfer overcomes the intrinsically weak absorption of Cr 3+ and boosts NIR emission in the CaLu 2 Mg 2 Si 3 O 12 host.

title: Quadruple-cation lead-free perovskite-inspired materials enable high-speed self-powered photodetection
abstract: Antimony-based perovskite photodiodes incorporating four different cations exhibit high photovoltages, rendering them promising candidates for self-powered photodetectors.

title: Exploration of the magnetocaloric effect in SrRuO 3 and BiFeO 3 /SrRuO 3 films via vacancy and interface design
abstract: Ru-deficient SrRuO 3 films and BiFeO 3 /SrRuO 3 heterostructures show tunable low-field magnetocaloric effects via vacancies and interface coupling. Ru vacancies induce dual FM transitions and higher entropy, while BiFeO 3 causes inverse MCE near T N .

title: Dynamically nonlinear NbO x memristors for multifunctional reservoir and neuromorphic computing
abstract: NbO x memristors were rapidly fabricated via an electrochemical process, providing nonlinearity and memory suitable for multifunctional, integrated neuromorphic computing systems.

title: Enhancing the efficiency and stability of perovskite solar cells using ALD-Al 2 O 3 as an electron transport layer buffer
abstract: A universal ALD-Al 2 O 3 interfacial engineering strategy is implemented at the ETL/electrode contact in both n–i–p and p–i–n architectures, promoting charge extraction and simultaneously enhancing efficiency and stability in perovskite solar cells.

title: Stable pure-blue phosphor-sensitized OLEDs utilizing a hypsochromic-shifting non-TADF fluorescent emitter
abstract: An indolocarbazole-based non-TADF emitter enables high-performance pure-blue OLEDs in combination with a phosphorescent sensitizer.

title: Enhancing the stability of perovskite light-emitting diodes based on Cl-MXene
abstract: Cl-MXene provides nucleation site for enhanced crystal quality and stability of perovskite LEDs.

title: Frustration-driven non-collinear magnetism and AC conduction mechanism in YBaCuFeO 5 double perovskite
abstract: SVD-derived exchange ratios reveal frustration-driven helical ordering in YBaCuFeO 5 . Competing interactions stabilize the incommensurate phase, accompanied by a Maxwell–Wagner dielectric response and polaronic transport.

title: Photoswitchable carbosilane-based polycatenars forming nematic, tilted smectic, hexatic, and cubic liquid crystalline phases
abstract: Photoswitchable carbosilane-based polycatenars combine nematic, tilted smectic, hexatic, and double-gyroid cubic phases, revealing how terminal chain topology controls self-assembly.

title: Hierarchical graphene quantum dot–copper nanocluster superstructures: synergistic assembly for optical limiting applications
abstract: Glutathione-stabilized copper nanoclusters form unprecedented 40 nm hexagonal superstructures. Covalent hybridization with N,S-GQDs enhances two-photon absorption and optical limiting, revealing a new class of nonlinear optical materials.

title: A stretchable light electrochemical cell based on CsPbBr 3 and a distributed GaP nanowire array with a single-walled carbon nanotube electrode
abstract: Flexible electronics aim to provide stretchable light-emitting devices for daily life capable of withstanding multiple stretch–relaxation cycles.

title: Exploration of the ferroelectric behavior of turmeric and its integration as a gate dielectric in organic field-effect transistors
abstract: Biodegradable turmeric thin film exhibit room-temperature ferroelectricity and piezoelectric response. intregated as a gate dielectric in OFETs, they enable low-voltage operation with enhanced mobility, offering a sustainable pathway for green and bio-inspired electronics.

title: Mechanochromism of dinuclear aluminum-based triple-stranded helicates through crystalline-to-amorphous phase transition
abstract: This study reveals torsion-angle-controlled reversible mechanochromism in aluminum-based helicates, establishing a clear structure–property relationship for switchable luminescence.

title: Enhancing buried interfacial toughness and performance in n–i–p perovskite solar cells via dipolar molecular interlayers
abstract: Enhancing the performance and suppressing interfacial degradation of perovskite solar cells via dipolar 3,5-diaminobenzotrifluoride (CF3-mDA) molecular modification.

title: Shamrock-shaped non-fullerene acceptors via side-chain and end-group engineering for high-efficiency and high-voltage organic photovoltaics
abstract: Three novel shamrock-shaped NFAs were synthesized by introducing lateral branched chains and adjusting the end groups. This work describes an effective strategy in advancing high- V OC and high-efficiency AQI acceptors for OPVs.

title: Biocompatible and structurally stable near-spherical PLNP@PDA@PW 12 nanoplatforms for precise temperature sensing and photothermal therapy
abstract: A core–shell PLNP@PDA@PW 12 nanoplatform was fabricated, which exhibited superior stability, biocompatibility, NIR-afterglow-based temperature sensing, and tissue-penetrating photothermal antibacterial capacity.

title: Engineering the amorphous-to-crystalline transformation, fluorescence enhancement and reversible switching of a cyclobutane derivative inside a polymer film
abstract: A polymer matrix approach enables reversible amorphous-to-crystalline transformation and fluorescence switching of CB in a polymer thin film. Computational modelling provided insights into the mechanism enhancing solid-state fluorescence emission.

title: Free-standing thermoelectric nanocomposite based on PEDOT:PSS, Cu 12+ x Sb 4 S 13 , and MWCNTs
abstract: Free-standing PEDOT:PSS/Cu 12+ x Sb 4 S 13 /MWCNT nanocomposite films exhibit enhanced thermoelectric performance, achieving a power factor of 49.5 µW m −1 K −2 at room temperature through complementary interfacial effects and optimized polymer conductivity.

title: Sn-Doping Induced Lattice Distortion and Deep-Red Self-Trapped Exciton Emission in 2D Lead Halide Perovskites
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) Ruddlesden-Popper perovskites have emerged as a versatile platform for broad-band light-emitting applications. Although pristine (C6H5C2H4NH3)2PbI4 (PEPI), typically yields narrow-band free exciton (FE) emission, the introduction of ns2-metal ions...

title: Halogen-driven emission switching in zero-dimensional indium halides: from efficient blue to yellow emission
abstract: Halogens govern the competition between the singlet (S 1 ) and self-trapped exciton (STE) states of the organic cations, leading to distinct luminescence.

title: Charge carrier dynamics modulation of Ag x InS 2 thin films for high-performance photodetection
abstract: Charge carrier dynamics of Ag 0.8 InS 2 films were characterized via transient absorption, which facilitated the rational fabrication of associated devices. The optimized devices achieve UV-visible switchable photoresponse with high performance.

title: Strategy for a bilayer structure of a cellulose-based membrane with efficient anti-fouling for solar-driven seawater desalination
abstract: Freshwater scarcity, insufficient heat localization, and membrane fouling remain major challenges for interfacial solar steam generation and seawater desalination.

title: Strong electronic coupling in Mn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S/Nb 2 CT X ohmic junctions enhances photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: This work enhances photocatalytic hydrogen production efficiency by constructing Mn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S/Nb 2 CT X MXene ohmic junctions.

title: Silk-derived carbon nanosheets for transparent conducting electrodes on flexible substrates
abstract: Transforming silk cocoons into carbon nanosheets by first creating regenerated silk fibroin solution, spin-casting it on silicon, and carbonizing the thin film. These transparent conductors are then transferred to flexible substrates.

title: Single- and dual-band quantum dot-based nanoporous solid-state lasers for gas sensing
abstract: A laser-based nitrogen dioxide (NO 2 ) gas sensing system was engineered by combining nanoporous anodic alumina random laser (NAA–RL) platforms and quantum dots (QDs).

title: Unravelling the role of various interactions in mechanochromism-induced polymorphism: a sensor kit for acidic fume detection and double encryption–decryption technologies
abstract: Pyrene-furan based mechanophore transforms to a different polymorph with a red-shifted enhanced emission upon external stimulation. The structure-property relation between molecular arrangements and mechanochromism has been established.

title: A significant impact of nitrogen substitution on the solid-state electronic structure: a case of 1,3,6,8-tetrakis(methylchalcogeno)-2,7-diazapyrene
abstract: MT-azapyrene, a nitrogen analog of the ultrahigh-mobility organic semiconductor, MT-pyrene, was synthesized. Despite their similarity, the packings are markedly differ; as a result, the mobility of MT-azapyrene was far inferior to that of MT-pyrene.

title: Barrier-thickness-engineered giant magnetotransport and multi-level storage in a CrTe 2 /Al 2 S 3 van der Waals multiferroic junction
abstract: A bilayer Al 2 S 3 barrier in a CrTe 2 /Al 2 S 3 /CrTe 2 MFTJ boosts TER to 3912%, TMR to 124.7%, and enables six non-volatile states with spin-filtering and NDR for multi-level memory.

title: Neutron reflectometry for in-depth porosity and figure-of-merit correction of organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: A porous channel structure with optimized pore dimensions and preserved molecular ordering enhances the performance of organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs).

title: Carbon dots–polyurethane composites with anisotropic enhanced fluorescence for strain sensing
abstract: Flexible carbon dots–polyurethane composites were conveniently fabricated and used as fluorescent strain sensors for detecting a wide range of strain. The sensing mechanism is attributed to a novel anisotropic anti-quenching mechanism.

title: Influence of composition and structure on resistive switching properties of hafnium–titanium-oxide thin films grown by atomic layer deposition
abstract: Resistive switching of Hf x Ti 1− x O y (0.07 ≤ x ≤ 0.64) films was studied. Increase in x , enabling formation of orthorhombic Hf x Ti 1− x O y , caused an increase in the breakdown electric field strength, resistive switching memory window, and endurance.

title: Ultrahigh electromechanical output in ferroelectric polymer via a continuous and aligned pore design
abstract: A P(VDF-TrFE) film with continuous and aligned pores was prepared, achieving enhanced electromechanical output and a high equivalent piezoelectric coefficient of 5727 pC N −1 .

title: Light atom influenced room temperature phosphorescence in functionalized benzophenones: experimental and theoretical correlations
abstract: This study depicts the relevance of substituted benzophenones exhibiting room temperature phosphorescence. Crystal packing analysis depicts the role of different intermolecular interactions that impart structural rigidity and govern this behaviour.

title: Physical vapor deposited nanomaterials for gas sensing: design strategies, performance limits and machine learning prospects
abstract: Physical vapor deposited nanomaterials for gas sensing.

title: High-throughput structure searches for ambient-pressure superconductors in boron–carbon clathrates
abstract: A systematic high-throughput structural search identifies 21 stable X 2 B 8 C 4 clathrates at ambient pressure. Among them, Rb 2 B 8 C 4 exhibits the highest superconducting critical temperature, with T c reaching up to 34 K.

title: High-Q chiral metasurface with quasi-BIC for tunable circular dichroism and refractive index sensing
abstract: This work presents an all-dielectric metasurface with strong intrinsic chirality from quasi-BIC and a high Q-factor, demonstrating outstanding sensing performance.

title: PEDOT:PSS conducting eutectogel for enhanced electrical recording and stimulation in implantable neural interfaces
abstract: Deep eutectic solvent–modified PEDOT:PSS enables single-layer thick, soft, and conductive coatings, enhancing charge transfer and neural interfacing, validated in vitro and in vivo in rat sciatic nerves. Original photograph, cartoon style applied with AI.

title: Adhesive, mechanochromic structural color materials for large strain sensing and optical reflectors
abstract: Chameleon-inspired mechanochromic photonic crystals (MPCs) are receiving growing attention owing to their unique ability to alter structural colors under external forces.

title: Unraveling the excitation-dependent pathways: competing thermal mechanisms of Ce 3+ emission in LuAG transparent ceramics
abstract: Under excitations of 325 nm and 450 nm lasers, the characteristic green luminescence of the Ce 3+ ions in LuAG PTCs shows diametrically opposite temperature dependence, i.e. , negative thermal quenching and normal thermal quenching, respectively.

title: Switchable room-temperature valley polarization in a Janus GeRuO 2 SiN 2 native ferrovalley monolayer and its self-assembled sliding multiferroics
abstract: Vertically stacked GeRuO 2 SiN 2 ferrovalley homobilayers with switchable out-of-plane sliding ferroelectricity enable effective tunability of both the valley polarization and layer-locked anomalous valley Hall effect.

title: Synthesis and halochromic behavior of resorufin-based monomer, and redox-responsive optical properties of both monomer and polymer
abstract: Resorufin exhibits reversible structural transformation to its reduced form, dihydroresorufin, during redox reactions.

title: Monolayer and bilayer tin monoxide in edge contact with common metals: a first-principles investigation
abstract: Edge contact using two-dimensional tin monoxide as the channel material is a promising design for next-generation transistors, offering a favourable tunability of the Schottky barrier height and a reduced charge carrier transition time.

title: In situ synthesized copper-incorporated laser-induced graphene enabling high-sensitivity flexible temperature sensing
abstract: Through Cu incorporation to overcome LIG's NTC and integration with PDMS, a highly sensitive, flexible PTC temperature sensor is achieved for applications in health monitoring.

title: Manipulating the piezoelectric response of BCZT-based ceramics through BMS doping-controlled domain size and phase boundary engineering
abstract: The MRB + RMC strategy enables pronounced grain refinement, a hierarchical multi-scale domain structure, and stable multiphase coexistence, thereby markedly enhancing the piezoelectric coefficient and effectively suppressing the hysteresis.

title: Room-temperature electrochemical co-deposition of Cu-MOF/CNT heterostructures on carbon fibers for efficient microwave absorption at low filler loading
abstract: The report describes a Cu-MOF/CNT heterostructure cathodically deposited on carbon fibers, forming an interpenetrating conductive network that enables −60.6 dB reflection loss and a 4.06 GHz bandwidth at 5 wt% loading.

title: A novel multi-component lead-free (1− x )BF– x BT–0.01(BNT–20BKT) piezoelectric ceramic exhibits excellent high-temperature piezoelectric properties and thermal stability
abstract: A novel lead-free (1− x )BF– x BT–0.01(BNT–20BKT)(0.27 ≤ x ≤ 0.35) piezoelectric ceramic was fabricated. At x = 0.31, an in situ d 33 of 687.8 pC N −1 was achieved at a depolarization temperature ( T dr ) of 381.8 °C.

title: A digital-analog memristor based on a Ru–polymer complex for high-performance, noise-resistant AI applications
abstract: A coordination-engineered RuCl 3 -PVPS organic memristor enables filament-free redox switching with coexisting digital and analog behaviors, synaptic plasticity, an I ON / I OFF ratio > 10 2 , and 92.72% recognition accuracy under 40% Gaussian noise.

title: Narrow red-emitting InP quantum dots with 38 nm FWHM: mechanistic insights into the role of In( i )Cl precursors
abstract: High-quality red-emitting InP quantum dots with a record-narrow 38 nm FWHM were synthesized using In( i )Cl, providing mechanistic insight into the reaction.

title: UVC-induced valence switching in BaFBr:Sm 3+ nanoplates
abstract: BaFBr:Sm 3+ nanoplates undergo reversible valence switching upon exposure to UVC radiation. Luminescence studies were conducted to probe this photochemical reaction, including its extent, kinetics, and the stability of photogenerated Sm 2+ ions.

title: Ultrafast response UV-regulated graphene-based NO 2 gas sensor
abstract: UV-regulated CVD graphene NO 2 sensor for ultrafast room-temperature detection. UV-induced Fermi-level modulation accelerates adsorption–desorption dynamics, delivering 1 s response, high selectivity, and long-term stability.

title: A mechanically robust self-healing polysiloxane via Fe 3+ -dopamine metal coordination for flexible sensors
abstract: In this study, a self-healing polydimethylsiloxane material with enhanced mechanical properties for flexible strain sensors in physiological monitoring was prepared.

title: On the photothermal behaviour of naphthalene diimide aggregates in water
abstract: Here we show the photothermal behaviour of self-assembled naphthalene diimides in water. We illustrate how the group in the imide position and pH change alter the photothermal response from the materials.

title: Triple-readout luminescence thermometry in a heterodinuclear Yb–Er complex and performance comparison with the homodinuclear analogues
abstract: A performance competition: the Yb–Er complex enables three thermometric pathways, whereas the Yb 2 analogue delivers a single, highly sensitive readout.

title: Atomic layer deposition of molybdenum carbide and substrate-dependent reduction to metallic molybdenum
abstract: Development of a novel molybdenum carbide ALD process and subsequent formation of high-quality metallic molybdenum films via substrate dependent decarburization.

title: Comment on “Electron-interfered field-effect transistors as a sensing platform for detecting a delicate surface chemical reaction” by G. Choi, K. Lee, S. Oh, J. Seo, E. Park, Y. D. Park, J. Lee and H. S. Lee, J. Mater. Chem. C , 2021, 9 , 8179
abstract: This comment critiques electron-interfered field-effect transistors (EIFET), using organic charge-modulated (OCMFET) ones as the valid standard. EIFETs are flawed; charge creates gate currents, not shifts.

title: Advances in the design of bio-organic resistive switching memory
abstract: A design-oriented review of bio-organic resistive switching memory, which highlights how material selection, interfaces, additives, processing, and electrical testing strategies influence the switching mechanism, stability, and device performance.

title: Localized plasmonic reconstruction via xenon photoactivation for high-sensitivity SERS substrates
abstract: We report an ultra-fast Xe photoactivation strategy for rapid activation of SERS substrates, shortening the treatment time from tens to hundreds of minutes to only 6 s, for real-time sensing and large-area nanophotonic applications.

title: New D–π–A dyes based on indolo[3,2,1- jk ]carbazole and thienopyrrolo[3,2,1- jk ]carbazole for application in dye-sensitized solar cells
abstract: Following optimisation of the DSSC architecture, the newly obtained D–π–A molecule demonstrated high efficiency under low illumination conditions.

title: Exciton dynamics in a room-temperature luminescent quasi 2D perovskite C 10 H 20 (NH 3 ) 2 PbBr 4
abstract: This work explores electronic and optical properties of the quasi 2D hybrid halide perovskite C 10 H 20 (NH 3 ) 2 PbBr 4 . According to DFT calculations, it is a direct bandgap semiconductor. The experimental optical bandgap determined...

title: Selenium-assisted surface enhancement single-crystal PbSe/Ge heterojunctions for a high detectivity uncooled mid infrared wavelength photovoltaic detector
abstract: IV–VI/Ge heteroepitaxy provides a solution for the epitaxial incompatibility between IV–VI semiconductors and silicon-based read-out integrated circuits (Si-ROICs).

title: Alleviating trade-off between dark current and sensitivity of β-Ga 2 O 3 X ray detectors via defect engineering
abstract: In this study, air annealing was employed to achieve simultaneous modulation of both V o and V Ga defects in β-Ga 2 O 3 single crystals. The fabricated detector demonstrates outstanding comprehensive performance and successfully realizes object imaging.

title: Carbon-tetrel bonds: cooperative existence and disappearance in elastically bendable fluorescent organic single crystals by operating within the hierarchy of non-covalent interactions
abstract: The mechanical properties of elastically bendable fluorescent organic single crystals are modified using carbon-tetrel bonds. The cooperativity in the carbon-tetrel bond is studied using DFT calculations.

title: Near-infrared light-emitting electrochemical cells based on a molecular ruby
abstract: The first Cr( iii )-based NIR LECs achieving ∼20-fold EQE enhancement via host–guest design and layer optimization.

title: Atomic-layer precision etching of SiO 2 using sequential molecular adsorption and plasma activation
abstract: A novel cycle-mode etching strategy combining reversible physisorption and a defect-mediated chemisorption achieves 1.4 Å per cycle, contamination-free, directional SiO 2 etching.

title: Antipolar and short- and long-range magnetic ordering in quasi-two-dimensional AgCrP 2 S 6
abstract: Temperature- and polarization-dependent Raman spectroscopy of AgCrP 2 S 6 reveals distinct phonon anomalies and signatures of an antiferromagnetic phase transition, highlighting strong spin–lattice interactions in this quasi-2D system.

title: Local structure of hydrated and dehydrated Prussian white cathode materials
abstract: The local structure of hydrated and dehydrated Prussian white is investigated using neutron total scattering and Reverse Monte Carlo refinements.

title: Preferentially oriented SrTiO 3 thin films grown on lanthanide-assisted Si(001) via pulsed laser deposition
abstract: The integration of high-quality SrTiO 3 thin films on silicon substrates is crucial for various applications.

title: An optimal route towards green optoelectronics: enhancing electrical conductivity of conducting polymers using microbubble lithography
abstract: We explore the physics behind the conductivity enhancement of PEDOT:PSS patterns fabricated using Microbubble Lithography, describing the complex interplay between phase separation, densification of PEDOT domains, and improved percolation pathways.

title: A bidirectional knitted strain sensor via AgNWs/MXene hybridization for broad-range multi-stage sensing
abstract: Bidirectional textile sensor with AgNWs/MXene-functionalized double-knitted fabric exhibits exceptional tensile/compressive responsiveness via geometric reconfiguration and interfacial slippage for comprehensive motion monitoring.

title: Coexistence of the quantum anomalous Hall and anomalous Nernst effects in Cr-doped NaMgX (X = P, As, Sb) monolayers
abstract: The integration of both the quantum anomalous Hall effect (QAHE) and the anomalous Nernst effect (ANE) into a single material system is critical yet remains a significant challenge for the advancement of multifunctional quantum devices.

title: Strain-durable and stretchable electrodes with low interfacial impedance using liquid metal/PEDOT:PSS fiber mats for multifunctional epidermal bioelectronics
abstract: Schematic illustration of the fabrication of the ETLP electrode and its application in wearable electronics.

title: Graphene has a finite band gap originating from the exchange term of self-energy
abstract: Graphene exhibits a band gap of 0.249 eV in the double group of a finite spin–orbit coupling term in a GW approximation.

title: Highly sensitive wearable strain sensor with enhanced working range based on heterogeneous mechanical cracks
abstract: Despite their extraordinary sensitivity, crack-based strain sensors suffer from narrow strain-sensing ranges. Here, we present a novel heterogeneous crack (HC) structure comprising channel- and network-shaped metal cracks to aid in...

title: A theoretical framework for benchmarking amphiphilic 2D covalent organic frameworks for atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Atmospheric water harvesting (AWH) relies on sorbent materials that combine high water uptake at low relative humidity with low regeneration energy and long-term cycling stability.

title: Solid-state supramolecular assembly, luminescence thermometry and solution-state photoisomerization studies in lanthanide polyoxazamacrocycle
abstract: Pendant-arm modification of polyoxaaza macrocycles directs supramolecular architectures of 1+1 and 2+2 lanthanide complexes and efficient luminescent thermometric behaviour of Dy 3+ and Nd 3+ assemblies.

title: Contact engineering of MoS 2 synaptic transistors via reactive ion etching
abstract: A localized contact-region CF 4 /Ar RIE strategy modulates MoS 2 /metal contact injection and defect-assisted charge trapping, leading to improved synaptic performance in back-gated MoS 2 transistors.

title: An energy-efficient NbO x /ZrO 2 bilayer memristor enabling low-voltage multilevel switching for neuromorphic computing
abstract: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) and edge computing has intensified an urgent demand for energy-efficient hardware capable of mimicking the parallel processing and adaptive learning functions of the human brain.

title: Order–disorder phase transition and orientational defect formation in a hybrid organic–inorganic piezoelectric material
abstract: Ferroelectric-to-paraelectric phase transitions in hybrid organic–inorganic piezoelectric materials can be modelled accurately using machine learning force fields and show unexpected molecular orientations contributing to entropic stabilisation.

title: Controlling platinum loading in PEMFC membrane electrodes by sputtering time: a systematic study from ultra-low to high loading for optimal performance
abstract: Magnetron sputtering technology holds significant application value in preparing low-platinum catalysts for proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs) due to its atomic-level deposition precision and controllable thin-film structure.

title: Metal oxide semiconductor-based hydrogen sensors: material engineering strategies for performance enhancement
abstract: Hydrogen sensors are crucial for energy, industry, battery safety, healthcare, and aerospace. This review links material properties, sensing mechanisms, and design strategies for high-performance MOS hydrogen sensors.

title: Relaxor behavior and ferroelectric polarization modified by Sm doping of Ba 0.9 Sr 0.1 Ti 0.93 Sn 0.07 O 3 ceramics for electrocaloric cooling applications
abstract: The (Ba 0.9 Sr 0.1 ) 0.995 Sm 0.005 Ti 0.93 Sn 0.07 O 3 ceramic exhibits a Δ T of 3.8 K at an electric field of 200 kV cm −1 near room temperature, accompanied by a wide T span of 78 °C.

title: Exploring pressure-induced phase transitions in imidazolium lead bromide hybrids
abstract: Pressure-driven structural and phonon responses of IM i PbBr i +2 ( i = 1–3) were studied by high-pressure XRD/Raman and temperature-dependent IR/Raman.

title: Volatile nanocomposite memristor with a phase stratification dielectric layer: a threshold switching with rich neuromorphic dynamics
abstract: Volatile threshold memristive switches are attractive for the fabrication of artificial compact neurons, third–order neuromorphic elements, as well as reservoir computing (RC) systems. The latter have emerged as a powerful...

title: Tuning structural instability in Cu 12− x Ni x Sb 4 S 13 ( x = 0, 0.05, 0.2, 0.5) tetrahedrites
abstract: The structural phase transition in the Cu 12− x Ni x Sb 4 S 13 ( x = 0, 0.05, 0.2, 0.5) tetrahedrites takes place for 0.05 ≤ x ≤ 0.2.

title: Phenothiazine and anthracene derivatives as emitters for vacuum-deposited and solution-processed optoelectronic devices
abstract: Organic twisted compounds exhibit unique electronic structures and intermolecular interactions, which contribute significantly to the development of solid-state fluorescent materials that have potential applications in optoelectronic devices.

title: Achieving intrinsically stretchable high-performance n-type polymer semiconductors via flexible linker engineering
abstract: Using flexible linker engineering, we developed two intrinsically flexible n-type conjugated polymers, P1TVT and P2TVT, which retain high electron mobilities of 0.32 cm 2 V −1 s −1 under 25% parallel strain.

title: An unbalanced load resistor for ideal logic levels and maximum gain in organic electrochemical transistor-based unipolar inverters
abstract: This paper proposes straightforward unipolar inverter circuit design guidelines and identifies a trade-off between maximum voltage gain and logic level fidelity as a function of load resistance.

title: High-sensitivity NO 2 detection using Se-doped In 2 O 3 thin films synthesized via RF magnetron sputtering
abstract: NO 2 is a key atmospheric pollutant, and its efficient detection is crucial for environmental protection and human health.

title: Mechanistic insights into the dry oxidation of amorphous silicon nitride: a DFT study
abstract: O defects in amorphous Si 3 N 4 behave very differently compared to the crystalline phase. Local excess of O defects facilitates the initial substitution of N, rationalising the exceptional oxidation resistance and measured NO : N 2 formation ratios.

title: Impact of pseudohalide-based cesium formate salt as an additive or substituent on perovskite solar cell performance
abstract: This study investigates the dual roles of cesium formate (CsHCOO) as an additive and substituent in perovskite solar cells. Devices with the CsHCOO additive yield superior performance by enhancing grain size and passivating surface defects.

title: The role of a [2.2]paracyclophane moiety in piezofluorochromism of crystalline organoboron complexes
abstract: The fluorescence properties of crystals of organoboron complexes were investigated under high pressure using a diamond anvil cell to evaluate the effects of intramolecular π–π interactions in the [2.2]paracyclophane moiety on piezofluorochromism.

title: Data-driven exploration of NaXTe 2 (X = Al, Ga, and In): from high-throughput screening to tailored optoelectronic functionalities
abstract: The rational design of semiconductors with tunable properties for optoelectronic applications remains challenging, especially beyond traditional paradigms limited by d-electron localization and instability.

title: Real-time monitoring of turbidity changes of solutions induced by polymer nanoparticles using fluorescent probes
abstract: A real-time turbidity monitoring system based on PTF2 enables in situ tracking of turbidity changes in heterogeneous systems via fluorescence variations, allowing monitoring of polymer assembly, nucleation, and particle growth.

title: Optoelectronic inhibitory synapses in the visible range from PbS nanocrystal arrays
abstract: Optoelectronic inhibitory synapses play critical roles in modulating neural activity and maintaining the balance between excitation and inhibition within artificial neural circuits.

title: Coupled long- and short-period dynamics in Bi 3+ -doped BCZT/cellulose nanogenerators for optimized output and high-temperature stability
abstract: A Bi 3+ -doped BCZT/cellulose film PENG shows dual-period output and, at 0.025 mol doping, generates 15.3 V, 16.85 µA, and 36.56 µW cm −2 with excellent thermal stability and flexibility for wearable energy harvesting.

title: Swelling-assisted embedding of metal nanowires into polymer substrates via roll-to-roll processing for enhanced mechanical durability
abstract: Flexible electrode conductors are essential components for the next generation of flexible electronics.

title: Interfacial oxygen-scavenging-driven formation of atomic-layer-deposited MoO 2 on MoN x electrodes for improved TiO 2 capacitor performance
abstract: Oxygen-scavenging MoN x converts ALD MoO x into metastable monoclinic MoO 2 , enabling high-k rutile TiO 2 -based capacitors with low leakage and ultralow EOT.

title: Self-filtering red narrowband photodetector based on a perovskite/organic multi-stack photoactive layer
abstract: A multi-stacked narrowband photodetector composed of perovskite/D18/PC 71 BM was prepared, achieving an external quantum efficiency of 30.36% with FWHM 39.40 nm, dark current of 1.64 × 10 −8 mA cm −2 , and the specific detectivity of 3.97 × 10 10 Jones.

title: Stoichiometry-controlled synthesis and optoelectronic performance of 2D InSe
abstract: Stoichiometry-controlled growth of InSe reveals that Se deficiency induces defect states that strongly modulate carrier transport and photodetector performance, highlighting stoichiometric tuning as an effective strategy for defect engineering.

title: Cost-efficient quinoxaline-based semiconducting polymers: systematic structure–property–morphology correlation toward reliable organic field-effect transistors
abstract: Exploration within a low-synthetic-complexity quinoxaline framework enables rational π-linker and side-chain design, establishing clear structure–morphology–transport correlations and delivering competitive OFET performance.

title: Synergistic Side-Chain and End-Group Engineering of C-Shaped A–D–A Acceptors for Enhanced Performance of Organic Photovoltaics
abstract: In this work, two ortho-benzodipyrrole-based non-fullerene acceptors, CBBO-Cl and CBHD-Cl, were designed and synthesized by introducing branched side chains at the β-position of the thieno[3,2-b]thiophene units in the outer convex...

title: Spectroscopic investigation of bandwidth control effects on the Mott–Hubbard state in epitaxial RVO 3 (R = La, Pr, Y) thin films
abstract: Electronic structure analysis via VBS and XAS spectra reveals the Mott–Hubbard type insulating nature of RVO 3 thin films.

title: Biomimetics-inspired honeycomb architecture-based flexible sensors: from design to applications
abstract: This review outlines recent advances in honeycomb-inspired flexible sensors, and their applications in health monitoring, smart wearables, and human–machine interaction.

title: Direct ink writing of silver nanowire-based flexible temperature sensors on fabric for wearable thermal monitoring
abstract: A silver nanowire-based flexible temperature with detection range was constructed on fabric through direct ink writing, which has great potential in the wearable field.

title: Protein-rich marine Spirulina for synthesizing biocompatible carbon dots with high quantum yield for fluorescent yarns
abstract: Spirulina phycocyanin was used to prepare high-performance CDs. These CDs overcome the poor reproducibility of biomass CDs. Purification and co-doping enhance the quantum yield to 20.4%. Biocompatible PU-CDs are applicable for safe wearable textiles.

title: An agarose-epoxy resin composite system for enhancing green body strength in microwave dielectric ceramic filters
abstract: The 14.46% enhanced bending strength of MgTiO 3 –CaTiO 3 ceramic green bodies achieved by the composite system, with no adverse effect on sintered ceramic performance.

title: Disorder-induced topological properties in CoFeCrZ (Z = Al and Ga) Heusler alloys: from spin gapless semiconductors to magnetic Weyl semimetals
abstract: B2-type disorder destabilizes the spin gapless semiconducting phase, drives the system into a magnetic Weyl semimetallic state, enhances the intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity and induces topologically protected surface states.

title: Elucidating thermally activated luminescence quenching in Eu III β-diketonate complexes as crystals or polymeric films for application in thermometry
abstract: Temperature-dependent luminescence dynamics of the Eu III β-diketonate complex [Eu 2 (bpm)(nta) 6 ] was investigated in the crystalline state and PMMA films, revealing how polymer dispersion affects thermal quenching mechanisms and luminescence thermometry performance.

title: A bifunctional porous organic polymer for NIR-selective electrochromism and bright-to-quenched electrofluorochromic smart windows
abstract: A donor–acceptor low-bandgap covalent porous polymeric framework (TPA–TZTZ) exhibits NIR-selective electrochromism and bright-to-quenched electrofluorochromism via redox-induced polaron/bipolaron formation for smart optoelectronic applications.

title: A transparent self-powered ZnVO 2 /CdGa 2 O 3 heterostructure for integrating UV photodetection and luminescence down-conversion
abstract: Transmission, detection, luminescence down-conversion, and self-powered operation are integrated in a single optoelectronic device.

title: Recent advances in synthesis of ZnCr 2 O 4 nanomaterials and their composites for catalytic, energy, sensing, and biomedical applications: a review
abstract: ZnCr 2 O 4 NPs have emerged as a multifunctional class of spinel oxides exhibiting remarkable physicochemical, magnetic, optoelectronic, and catalytic properties, making them promising candidates for a variety of technological and biomedical uses.

title: A Janus PEDOT/PAM conductive hydrogel enabling dual-function: uric acid detection and mechanical monitoring
abstract: The Janus PEDOT/PAM conductive hydrogel developed enables ultrastretchable strain sensing and uric acid detection. MXene–PEDOT:PSS interactions improve adhesion and conductivity, supporting wearable motion monitoring and biochemical diagnostics.

title: Dual-Mode Devices Integrating Photodetection and Optoelectronic Synaptic Plasticity Based on Pb(Zr,Ti)O3 Films
abstract: The separation of photodetectors and optoelectronic synapses within artificial visual systems not only increases the design complexity, but also results in high energy consumption. In this work, we demonstrate a...

title: Controlling ink's spreadability to reduce its disorderly flow for promoting the uniformity of silver nanowire transparent electrode
abstract: Appropriately controlling the spreadability of the ink to reduce its disorderly flow can greatly promote the uniformity of silver nanowire transparent electrode.

title: Highly aligned polymer nanowire-based fin architecture for enhanced functionality of organic electrochemical transistors in neuromorphic computing
abstract: Aligned polymer nanowires in organic transistors achieve highly linear synaptic plasticity by enhancing interactions between conjugated polymer and ion–gel electrolytes, enabling efficient neuromorphic computing.

title: Recent advances in the performance modulation of polyurethane-based triboelectric materials for wearable devices
abstract: Application of polyurethane-based triboelectric electrical materials in medical health and tactile sensing.

title: Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 @TiO 2 nanocomposites with enhanced nonlinear absorption and electrochemical properties
abstract: Synthesis of well-defined Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 @TiO 2 nanocomposites. The material exhibits outstanding reverse saturable absorption ( β = 294 cm GW −1 ) and superior optical limiting ( F th = 3.87 J cm −2 ), proving its promise for photonic limiting devices.

title: Electrochemically modulated photodoping in polymeric semiconductors for efficient photo-thermoelectric conversion
abstract: Photodoping in a PDPP4T : Pg2T-T polymer blend is improved by electrochemical coupling, underscoring its potential for improving the photo-thermoelectric properties.

title: Interfacial thermal conductance between a spin-ladder cuprate (La 5 Ca 9 Cu 24 O 41 ) and a metal film: the role of surface defects in cuprates
abstract: Thermal switching is an invaluable technique for advanced thermal management and enables highly efficient reuse of thermal energy and improves the performance of various devices that are impacted by waste heat.

title: Boosting methane selectivity in CO 2 electroreduction via rational modulation of Cu 0 –Cu + sites in Cu/Cu 2 O composites
abstract: Tailoring Cu 0 –Cu + sites in Cu/Cu 2 O catalysts significantly improves CO 2 -to-methane conversion efficiency by optimizing intermediate adsorption and suppressing competing reactions.

title: Efficiently doped P3HT and polystyrene blend with porous 3D structure for thermoelectric applications
abstract: This study describes a reproducible process for forming highly mesoporous, mechanically robust, and handleable aerogels based on entangled poly(3-hexylthiophene) (P3HT) and syndiotactic polystyrene (sPS) nanofibers for thermoelectric applications.

title: Correction: Fluorination in core-only calamitic liquid crystals: how many and where should they go?
abstract: Correction for ‘Fluorination in core-only calamitic liquid crystals: how many and where should they go?’ by Jacob G. Rothera et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5tc02621k.

title: Wavelength-sensitive CMOS-like optoelectronic inverter circuits based on solution-processable perovskite nanocrystals/organic semiconductor blends
abstract: The integration of solution-processed perovskites and organic semiconductors (OSCs) offers a promising approach for low-cost and flexible optoelectronic devices owing to the conductivity of OSCs and remarkable photoelectric features of perovskites.

title: Dual-polarization and dual-band quasi-BIC metasurface for tunable absorption and high-sensitivity sensing
abstract: An all-dielectric metasurface with high sensing performance is designed to achieve dual-polarization and dual-band Q-BIC resonance. The physical mechanism is analyzed by calculating eigenmodes and multipole decomposition.

title: Topological spin dynamics in Eu-doped SnTe using spin resonance and magnetic measurements
abstract: Eu doping in SnTe raises vacancy formation energy, lowers Sn vacancies, and induces strain and impurity-driven scattering, yielding anisotropic spin–flop transitions and enhanced WAL for quantum device applications.

title: Metal-assisted chemical etching of silicon pore structures to investigate the electrochemical performance of silicon-based anodes
abstract: A porous Si/C composite derived from Mg 2 Si and pitch, with optimized pore structure, enables fast Li + transport, mitigates volume expansion, and achieves high-capacity and durable silicon anodes.

title: Nonlinear optical properties of Cs 3 BiBr 6 nanocrystals doped with SbBr 3 : from experimental observation to theoretical calculations
abstract: Enhanced RSA and optical limiting in Sb-doped Cs 3 BiBr 6 /SbBr 3 /PMMA OGs arising from Sb-induced electronic states, enabling superior nonlinear optical performance for advanced photonic protection.

title: Competition between helical and heliconical twist in the development of complex soft matter structures
abstract: Molecular chirality and chain branching in chiral bent-core liquid crystals control the helical vs. heliconical twist, leading to the discovery of new low-birefringent and isotropic mesophases.

title: Realizing the efficient utilization of Tb resources and high coercivity in grain boundary diffusion Nd–Fe–B magnets: the synergistic effect of Cu and Tb
abstract: The utilization of Cu micropowder as an auxiliary material presents a promising strategy to enhance the efficiency of heavy rare earth elements in grain boundary diffusion (GBD) processes.

title: Multifunctional interfacial molecule for high-performance blue quantum dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: We propose a multifunctional interfacial modification strategy using FPPB at the HTL/QD interface, in which the –CHO groups passivate the surface of QDs, while the deep HOMO level improves carrier balance, thereby enabling a peak EQE of 22.9%.

title: A multifunctional anti-swelling hydrogel with high stretchability and adhesion for wearable flexible sensors
abstract: Hydrogels possess properties such as stretchability, anti-swelling resistance and adhesiveness, and thus have been widely applied in intelligent wearable electronic products.

title: Highly luminescent Tb( iii ) cluster for the sensitization of Eu( iii ) through f → f energy transfer for LED applications
abstract: A new class of Na–Ln nanoclusters was synthesized with the general formula [Ln 2 L′ 2 (hfac) 4 (tfa) 2 ]·Na 2 L 2 , where Ln = Tb (TbC), Eu (EuC) or Gd (GdC).

title: A transparent and ionic conductive hydrogel integrating photochromism and electromagnetic interference shielding for smart window applications
abstract: Smart windows capable of simultaneously regulating light and providing electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding represent an emerging frontier in functional building materials.

title: Tailoring electrical properties of BiFeO 3 –BaTiO 3 ceramics via A-site La 3+ substitution: suppressed leakage and enhanced strain
abstract: Through A-site La 3+ doping, this work achieves selective optimization of the electrical properties of 0.7BiFeO 3 –0.3BaTiO 3 ceramics. Doping suppressed defects and established a morphotropic phase boundary.

title: Fiber laser-assisted room-temperature crystallization of solution-processed indium-rich IGO thin films for high-performance, high-reliability thin-film transistors
abstract: Solution-processed nanocrystalline oxide semiconductors with fiber laser-assisted post-treatment enable next-generation displays and sensors, combining large-area uniformity, low-cost fabrication, and high electrical performance.

title: First-principles study of the phase competition, and mechanical and piezoelectric properties of pseudo-binary (SiC) 1− x (AlN) x alloy
abstract: The pseudo-binary alloy (SiC)(AlN) is studied through first-principles random structure sampling and special quasi-random structures. Mechanical and piezoelectric properties of interest for MEMS applications are obtained for the wurtzite phase.

title: Modulation of oxygen vacancy filaments and crystal structure by thermal annealing for high-performance solution-processed HfZrO x resistive memory
abstract: Thermal annealing optimizes oxygen vacancy content and structure in HZO WORM memory. Balancing leakage suppression with robust filament formation regulates resistive switching, achieving an ON/OFF current ratio of 10 7 .

title: Defect density modulation in graphene quantum dots towards high magnetic resonance relaxivity
abstract: Regulating the defect density of magnetic GQDs by selecting precursor topologies during the “bottom-up” preparation process is effective for enhancing the magnetic resonance relaxivity of Gd 3+ @GQDs.

title: Modulating electrochromic properties via thiophene donors in thieno[3,4- c ]pyrrole-4,6-dione-based conjugated polymers
abstract: Addressing the lack of detailed structure–property studies, this work demonstrates the effective tuning of colours and electrochromic properties of thieno[3,4 -c ]pyrrole-4,6-dione (TPD)-based conjugated polymers through systematic donor engineering.

title: Polymorph-triggered multiple responses in dynamic molecular crystals of barbituric acid derivatives
abstract: Rigid molecules devoid of flexible chains exhibit polymorphism arising from varying degrees of conformational distortion in the crystal lattice and display distinct responsive behaviors to external stimuli, including water vapor, heating, and light.

title: Recent advances in CuSCN doping for electronic and photonic applications
abstract: Various doping strategies have been explored to improve the properties of CuSCN and continue to enhance the performance of CuSCN-based electronic and photonic devices. The underlying mechanisms and technological advancements are summarized herein.

title: Modulating the glassy and crystalline ordering of carbazole small molecules in bulk
abstract: We show tunable (glassy vs . crystalline) mesoscopic ordering of N-functionalized carbazoles and their impact on chiroptical properties (differential scattering of circularly polarized light).
