title: Antibonding and electronic instabilities in GdRu 2 X 2 (X = Si, Ge, and Sn): a new pathway toward developing centrosymmetric skyrmion materials
abstract: This work demonstrates how chemical bonding and electronic instabilities enable a new framework for understanding and developing skyrmions under desired conditions that would otherwise be impossible.

title: Direct preparation of PEA 2 PbBr 4 nanoplates with electron-irradiation-induced optical evolution
abstract: Direct preparation of PEA 2 PbBr 4 nanoplates exhibiting an electron-irradiation-induced narrow-to-broadband emission transition originated from self-trapped excitons.

title: Tunable intrinsic magnetism in 2D Mo 2 B 2 via double-transition-metal engineering under an electric field and hole doping
abstract: We propose a family of double-transition-metal (DTM) boride monolayers, MMoB 2 (M = V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni), and systematically investigate their magnetic behaviors using first-principles calculations combined with Monte Carlo simulations.

title: Facilitating rapid ion transport and boosting redox kinetics in Yb/NiCo-LDH for enhanced supercapacitor performance
abstract: A dual atom regulation strategy coupled with anionic defect engineering is shown to be highly effective for tuning the electronic structure and improving the electrochemical behaviour of layered double hydroxides (LDHs).

title: Optimizing Tb diffusion behavior in Tb-Cu-Al grain boundary diffused Nd-Fe-B magnets by Y substitution
abstract: Grain boundary diffusion (GBD) is a well-established technique for improving the coercivity of Nd-Fe-B permanent magnets while saving heavy rare earths (HRE) resource. However, a common challenge for employing HRE-based...

title: The lateral photovoltaic effect of WSeS/Si heterojunctions
abstract: The time response of LPV can effectively prevent the failure of VPV under high-intensity light.

title: Stereoisomeric Mixture of a Multigram-Synthesized Helicene Assembles Swiftly into Hierarchical Ribbons via Supramolecular Sheets
abstract: Hierarchical supramolecular assembly is a hallmark of biological systems but remains difficult to emulate with synthetic tectons. The challenge increases with chiral tectons, particularly when they are not optically enriched....

title: A comprehensive review of blue-emissive perovskites: mechanisms for regulating structural, electronic, and luminescent properties
abstract: In recent years, metal halide perovskites have gained research interest owing to the direct and tunable bandgaps, narrow bandwidth, and high carrier mobility, which are mainly driven by their potential application in the optoelectronics field.

title: Light-induced color changing of redox dyes assisted by a photoinitiator for information encryption on hydrogel paper
abstract: A simple “ink-pumping” strategy uses free radicals from a commercial photoinitiator to trigger UV-induced color change in methylene blue, enabling secure, rewritable, and multicolor information storage on hydrogel paper.

title: Resonant level and band splitting boost the thermoelectric performance of p-type polycrystalline SnSe
abstract: Ge and Ga co-doping enhances electrical conductivity and reduces thermal conductivity through band engineering and defect-induced phonon scattering, achieving a ZT value of 1 at 773 K in polycrystalline SnSe.

title: In situ observation of ferroelectric–antiferroelectric structural evolution in Sr-modulated sodium niobate and unraveling the polarization origin
abstract: Sr 2+ doping provides a novel lattice-level strategy to regulate the electrical-ordering stability in NaNbO 3 , which breaks the long-standing prevailing consensus of the tolerance factor as the sole criterion for related modulation.

title: Multicolor luminescence and low-temperature phosphorescence from cucurbituril supramolecular assemblies: tuning clusteroluminescence via macrocycle size and substituents
abstract: Outer-wall interaction-induced clustered luminescence in cucurbiturils.

title: Hydrated lithium phosphate as a low-temperature processable solid electrolyte for high-performance printed electronics
abstract: The proposed low- T (≤120 °C) solid electrolyte, hydrated Li 3 PO 4 , enables inkjet-printed a -IGO-based TFTs with excellent performance parameters; subthreshold slope of 61 mV dec − ¹, linear mobility of 48.2 cm² V − ¹ s − ¹, and an on/off ratio of 3.8 × 10 8 .

title: Dielectric microbead-decorated conductive polymer composites towards ultrathin high-performance terahertz absorbing coatings
abstract: Embedding dielectric microbeads into conductive polymers endows ultrathin THz absorbers with >99% absorption. The approach provides a scalable design for next-generation EMI shielding coatings.

title: Theoretical giant circular dichroism and broadband asymmetric absorption enabled by multilayered chiral metastructure-photonic crystals in the near-infrared regime
abstract: The specific spatial stacking configurations of layers in MPC1, MPC2, and MPC3 are shown.

title: Achieving atomic-level surfaces for aluminum alloys induced by a novel chemical mechanical polishing strategy using a developed polisher with yttria hybrid nano-abrasives
abstract: Schematic diagram of an atomic model for mechanism of chemical mechanical polishing at (a) original surface, (b) dissolution, (c) complexation and (d) removal stages.

title: A Fibonacci quasicrystal-based photonic spin Hall effect sensor for high-sensitivity pressure and refractive index sensing
abstract: This work theoretically presents a highly sensitive PSHE sensor based on the Fibonacci-sequence photonic quasicrystal structure, which can achieve external pressure detection (0–3 GPa) and wide-range refractive index measurement (1–1.42).

title: Optoelectronic synaptic devices based on solution-processed microcrystal indium gallium zinc oxide for neuromorphic computing and visual perception
abstract: Photoelectric synaptic function is modulated by adjusting In, Zn, Ga proportions in m-IGZO. Indium-rich devices serve visual perception and memory; gallium-rich devices (short-term memory, fast response) are applied in ultraviolet communication. Created by Figdraw.com.

title: Insights into the microscopic mechanism of persistent luminescence in Cr 3+ -doped ZnGa 2 O 4 phosphors
abstract: The origin of strong Cr 3+ PersL in Ga-based phosphors were elucidated. The results show that Cr 3+ substitution tunes the position of defect energy levels and enhances the anisotropy of its physical parameters, thereby enhancing PersL performance.

title: Enhancing remnant polarization in ferroelectric Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 thin films by oxygen-diffusive interlayers
abstract: An oxygen-diffusive V 2 O 5 interlayer promotes oxygen redistribution in Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 , leading to higher remnant polarization, lower coercive field, and excellent reliability.

title: Correction: Electronic structures and magnetic properties of the rare-earth-free permanent magnet α″-Fe 16 N 2 : first-principles calculations
abstract: Correction for ‘Electronic structures and magnetic properties of the rare-earth-free permanent magnet α″-Fe 16 N 2 : first-principles calculations’ by Peirun Duan et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 6728–6735, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC04934A.

title: Observation of delayed fluorescence/room-temperature phosphorescence emissions in organic small-molecule emitters, their properties, and electroluminescent performance
abstract: D–π–A type fused polyaromatic hydrocarbon-based blue emitters with multi-channel triplet exciton harvesting property are synthesized, and their solid-state emissions and electroluminescent performance are studied.

title: A multi-responsive soft actuator with integrated sensing based on an MXene trilayer composite
abstract: A soft actuator based on an MXene/Paper/PI trilayer film is fabricated. It can generate reversible deformation under multi-stimuli and provide integrated sensing, showing potential in wearable devices and human–machine interaction.

title: Deformable cholesteric liquid crystal microcapsules for high-reflectivity smart coatings
abstract: An HTPDMS-modified MF shell deforms cholesteric liquid crystal microcapsules from spheres into oblate spheroids. This shape change expands their reflective area from points into circular regions, significantly increasing overall reflectivity.

title: Local lattice distortion-driven highly efficient luminescence and thermal quenching resistance in Sb 3+ -doped hybrid indium chlorides
abstract: The excellent thermal quenching resistance is driven by the distortion of the [SbCl 5 ] 2− polyhedron.

title: Reply to the ‘Comment on “Electron-interfered field-effect transistors as a sensing platform for detecting a delicate surface chemical reaction”’ by M. Micjan and M. Weis, J. Mater. Chem. C , 2026, 14 , DOI: 10.1039/D5TC02689J
abstract: Electron-interfered field-effect transistors convert Au–thiol surface chemistry into reproducible threshold-voltage transients, separating persistent self-assembled monolayer formation from transient interfacial charge relaxation.

title: 350 ps Ultrafast room-temperature scintillation realized on CsPbBr 3 -based single crystals via Br 2 over-doping
abstract: By introducing excess bromine atoms as luminescent centers, we grew CsPbBr 3.03 single crystal ingots exhibiting a sub-nanosecond scintillation decay time of 350 ps. It is anticipated to offer novel insights for the research on fast timing materials.

title: Enhanced exciton luminescence and fast scintillation potential in Sr 2+ -activated CsPbCl 3 single crystals
abstract: Fast scintillator potential of Sr 2+ -activated CsPbCl 3 single crystals: tenfold excitonic luminescence enhancement and ultrashort ∼0.2 ns decay times.

title: Machine learning potential-assisted design for thermoelectric performance in anharmonic CsPbBr 3 and CsPbI 3
abstract: Machine learning potential-assisted design for thermoelectric performance in perovskites.

title: Enhanced deep-blue emission and quantum yield of Ca 0.995 YGaO 4 :0.5%Bi 3+ via lithium-based flux additives
abstract: Bi 3+ -doped CaYGaO 4 phosphors were synthesized via solid-state reaction. Compared to Li 2 O, which suppressed PLQY to 55%, Li 2 CO 3 flux significantly optimizes the synthesis, enhancing blue PLQY from a pristine 58% to a superior 70%.

title: Dual-parameter sensing-visualization integration in a single electrochromic device for visual display
abstract: A dual-cathode Zn 2+ electrochromic device with NbWO x /PANI and water-in-salt electrolyte realizes voltage-modulated 2D color control, achieving high optical modulation for intuitive dual-parameter environmental monitoring.

title: Ultra Responsive Heterostructure of Gold Nanoparticles Functionalized MoS 2 /GaSe/Ga 2 O 3 for Self-Powered Broadband Photodetection
abstract: Conventional photodetectors have limitations in spectrum range and efficiency; incorporating diverse materials could help overcome these constraints by enabling broadband, self-powered detection across the UVC to NIR spectrum. The MoS...

title: sp 3 -Linked dimeric carbazole-based p-type hosts for exciplex-driven green TADF OLEDs
abstract: sp 3 -linked dimeric carbazole hosts suppress crystallization, enhance thermal stability, and enable robust solution-processed exciplex TADF OLEDs with >20% EQE and stable high-temperature processing performance devices.

title: Research progress in pyrargyrite: synthesis, properties, applications, and future directions
abstract: This article reviews the detailed synthesis, characterization, and applications of Ag 3 SbS 3 . It provides critical benchmarks, structure–property insights, and design strategies for improved photocatalytic, photovoltaic, and energy storage performance.

title: Controlling self-assembly and charge transport in photo-responsive nanostructured materials
abstract: Aromatic core fluorination directs the self-assembly of hockey-stick polycatenars into a novel mesophase, unlocking tuneable charge transport for application as photo-responsive organic semiconductors of hole mobility up to 10 −2 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: Soft matter-enabled electrochemiluminescence: from interfacial kinetics to scenario-driven applications
abstract: Deploying electrochemiluminescence (ECL) sensors beyond controlled laboratory settings remains a formidable challenge.

title: Crystallographically altered (Mg,Sr) 3 (Al,Ga) 2 GeO 8 :Cr 3+ phosphors with tuned emission width from the ultra-sharp red to profound NIR band
abstract: An original concept converts sharp deep-red Cr 3+ emission into extraordinarily intense broadband near-IR emission with tunable peak and width in a single phosphor for smart lighting and imaging.

title: Construction of a P-rGO/CNTs@PANI ternary composite for supercapacitors with balanced energy–power performance
abstract: A ternary P-rGO/CNTs@PANI composite was prepared via a phosphoric acid-mediated one-step hydrothermal method. The symmetric supercapacitor exhibits excellent cycling stability and can stably power an electronic timer.

title: Mn–Sn co-doped NiO nanostructures with boosted activity for integrated energy conversion and storage
abstract: Mn and Sn co-doped nickel oxide (NiO) nanostructures were synthesized with controlled compositions using the formula Ni 0.9 (Mn 1− x Sn x ) 0.1 O ( x = 0.00, 0.25, 0.50, and 0.75), corresponding to pristine NiO, NMS 2, NMS 5, and NMS 7.

title: Near-infrared fluorescent Ag 2 S quantum dots stabilized by penicillamine enantiomers: influence of ligand chirality on linear and nonlinear optical properties and toxicity
abstract: Optimized synthesis of chiral NIR-emitting Ag 2 S QDs with nonlinear optical characterization and toxicity evaluation.

title: Modulating charge transport and optoelectronic properties through N -substitution in annulated thiophene–phenylene oligomers
abstract: We address the impact of the substitution of S atoms by N–CH 3 groups in a thiophene–phenylene co-oligomer DPBTBT, on the optical and electronic properties of isolated molecules and the charge transport and optoelectronic properties in thin films.

title: Hollow ZnO/C nanospheres with defect engineering for enhanced dielectric loss and broadband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Hollow ZnO/C nanospheres with hierarchical porosity and defects achieve strong EMW adsorption through interfacial polarization, dipole relaxation, multiple reflections, and conduction loss, enhancing impedance matching and broadband performance.

title: Evolution of dielectric and ferroelectric properties in LiTaO 3 under high pressure
abstract: A pressure-induced transition from coexisting electrostriction and ionic conduction to electronic conduction was observed in LiTaO 3 . Ferroelectricity strengthened in R 3 c but vanished in Pnma , driven by TaO 6 to TaO 8 polyhedral transformation.

title: Construction of ferroelectric topological domains in freestanding epitaxial BiFeO 3 nanostructures
abstract: Topological center domains in ferroelectric nanostructures have garnered considerable attention owing to their novel functionalities and potential applications in next-generation electronic devices.

title: Oligosaccharide toughened polyacrylamide hydrogels for high sensitivity strain sensors
abstract: Oligosaccharide toughened polyacrylamide hydrogels are promising for wearable electronics and human–machine interfaces due to their stretchability, conductivity, and stimulus responsiveness.

title: Kinetically regulated growth of CsPbBr 3 /CdSe core/shell quantum dots for stable light-emitting diodes
abstract: One-step core shell growth strategy is proposed, utilizing the activity difference between perovskite and shell precursors to achieve CdSe epitaxial growth. The optimal CsPbBr 3 /CdSe core/shell QD LED show a 13-fold extension in operational lifetime.

title: Detailed investigation of quantum confinement effects in MAPbI 3 perovskite quantum dots through cyclic voltammetry and ultraviolet photoelectron spectroscopy
abstract: A detailed investigation of quantum confinement effects in MAPbI 3 perovskite quantum dots (PQDs) is essential for understanding size-dependent modifications in electronic structure, optical transitions, and carrier dynamics.

title: Reversible hydration-induced ionic conductivity switching accompanied by large desorption enthalpy in sodium salts of 1,2,3-triazole-fused p -benzoquinone
abstract: Stepwise H + /Na + substitution in a rigid π-system realizes a flexible crystal lattice, leading to hydration-induced ionic conductivity and high-performance thermal energy storage.

title: Predicting the bandgap of ABX 2 materials: supervised machine learning on small dataset
abstract: Predicting the electronic bandgap in ABX 2 -type materials (where A = {Al, Ga, Zn, Cd}, B = {Ga, In, Ge, Sn, Si}, and X = {N, P, As, Sb}) is essential for photovoltaic applications.

title: Alignment-dependent polarization properties of self-assembled carbon nanotube films
abstract: In this work, through precise regulation of catalyst particle density via a tunable oxidation process, we successfully establish a direct and quantitative correlation between the orientation degree of CNT films and their polarization performance.

title: Preparation, performance optimization and application progress of flexible lead zirconate titanate films
abstract: This paper reviews the preparation and piezo-optimization of flexible PZT films, notes their uses in wearables, and points to future research directions.

title: Correction: Work function modulated water-soluble anode interlayer with copper-ion doping for precise signal detection in organic photodiodes
abstract: Correction for ‘Work function modulated water-soluble anode interlayer with copper-ion doping for precise signal detection in organic photodiodes’ by Min Soo Kim et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2025, 13 , 15603–15614, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TC01630D.

title: A molecularly engineered polymer dielectric with pyrene functionalization for organic transistors and circuits with reduced hysteresis
abstract: A pyrene-functionalized polymer gate dielectric exhibits ehnahced compatibility with organic semiconductors, and has been successfully applied to the fabrication of organic electronic devices with reduced hysteresis.

title: Switching and detection of the Néel vector multistate in non-collinear antiferromagnetic NiF 2 for logic applications
abstract: Four-state switching and detection of the Néel vector in antiferromagnetic NiF 2 are achieved. Based on this, we have designed AND and OR quaternary logic gates.

title: Graded surface-engineered lignin-derived carbon dots for tunable multicolor anti-counterfeiting films
abstract: Stepwise surface engineering endows lignin-derived carbon dots with blue-to-yellow tunable luminescence for multicolor anti-counterfeiting and UV-shielding PVA films.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance of SnTe thin films by magnetron co-sputtering with optimized carrier concentration and microstructure
abstract: Te co-sputtering optimizes SnTe films' defect chemistry and microstructure, yielding fine grains with enhanced carrier concentration via Te Sn antisites, achieving power factor of 141 µW cm -1 K -2 and device output of 44.75 nW at Δ T = 60 K.

title: Flexible RRAM utilizing CdSe quantum dot–P4VP composites and graphene layers
abstract: We developed a flexible RRAM device for neuromorphic systems using a CdSe quantum dot–P4VP (poly-4-vinylpyridine) composite with a graphene interfacial layer for superior reliability and flexibility.

title: Revisiting the mechanism of long-lived luminescence in host/guest organic crystals
abstract: Room-temperature phosphorescence in carbazole-benzoindole host/guest molecular crystals is identified to rely on energy-transfer, rather than charge-transfer, which is enabled by herringbone packing.

title: 2-Aminobenzazolium chlorocuprate( ii ) complexes for memristive devices: structural studies, synaptic plasticity, and random number generation
abstract: Heteroatom engineering in hybrid chlorocuprate memristors tunes interfacial hybridisation and trap-state density, enabling stable Schottky-barrier-driven resistive switching, neuromorphic behaviour, and stochastic hardware random number generation.

title: Floquet engineering of topological phase transitions in intrinsic ferrovalley semiconductor via circularly and elliptically polarized light
abstract: Floquet engineering provides a non-contact approach to dynamically tailor electronic band structures and induce topological phase transitions via time-periodic optical fields.

title: Realization of a high Chern number quantum anomalous Hall effect in ferromagnetic monolayer NiAl 2 Se 4 from the 120° antiferromagnetic state via doping and strain regulations
abstract: The QAHE, characterized by Chern number C = 2, can be attained in tensile-strained monolayer NiAl 2 Se 4 when the hole-doping concentration is 1.0 holes per unit cell. This is promising for the experimental exploration of topological spintronics.

title: Dynamic characterization of the pulse discharge behavior of Sr 1− x Ca x TiO 3 ceramics through RLC circuits
abstract: Moving beyond conventional polarization–breakdown models, this study establishes a direct correlation between Ca 2+ -induced microstructural evolution and key discharge performance metrics in Sr 1− x Ca x TiO 3 ceramics.

title: Unveiling the crystallization behavior of amorphous Te driven by pressure
abstract: Amorphous Te crystallizes into a body-centered cubic (BCC) phase at a compression strain of 18%.

title: Tailoring the longitudinal surface plasmon resonance wavelength of gold nanorods by selective etching
abstract: This article reports a method for selectively etching both ends or sides of AuNRs based on adjusting the concentration of sodium hypochlorite, which can achieve wide range tuning of longitudinal surface plasmon resonance wavelength of AuNRs.

title: Enhanced photoluminescence quantum efficiency of Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 by modulating the superposition of exciton wavefunctions
abstract: The introduction of tetrabutylammonium (TBA + ) into zero-dimensional Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 perovskite thin films increases the overlap of the exciton wavefunctions, resulting in superior photoluminescence properties and enhanced air stability.

title: Solution-processed metal oxide synaptic transistor with bilayer Li-ion-conducting gate dielectric
abstract: Solution-processed neuromorphic oxide TFT with Li 5 AlO 4 /Li 4 Ti 5 O 12 bilayer dielectric with excellent TFT properties showing high endurance, STP–LTP transition, PPF, and robust potentiation-depression, enabling 92% digit-recognition accuracy.

title: Rational design of multiple sections in ferroelectric photovoltaics for enhanced current density
abstract: The laser-ablated BFO films were constructed multi-circuits ferroelectric photovoltaic device. The current density was increased to 1.069 mA cm −2 while the open circuit voltage was 2.74 V.

title: Remarkable photostability enhancement in FAPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystal polymer films via lecithin passivation
abstract: Soy lecithin ligand engineering stabilizes FAPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals and extends PL intensity retention by over 300-fold, accelerating practical use of high performance perovskite nanocrystals.

title: Lattice vibration characteristics and dielectric response mechanisms in low-temperature sintered SrSn(BO 3 ) 2 − x wt% LiF microwave ceramics for 5G microstrip antenna applications
abstract: In this study, SrSn(BO 3 ) 2 – x wt% LiF microwave ceramics were successfully prepared at 950 °C via conventional solid-state sintering.

title: Interlayer Sliding for Magnetic and Topological Phase Control in Pt₂HgSe₃/Nb₃I₈ van der Waals Heterostructure
abstract: The integration of two-dimensional van der Waals materials provides a powerful platform for engineering quantum states with tailored properties. In this work, we systematically investigate the Pt₂HgSe₃/Nb₃I₈ magnetic heterostructure using...

title: Low-doping clustering of Ti 4+ in monoclinic ZrO 2 : photoluminescence and formation mechanism
abstract: Oxygen vacancies induce Ti 4+ clustering in m -ZrO 2 , enabling UV-driven electron delocalization within clusters and subsequent energy transfer to isolated Ti 4+ ions.

title: Broad-spectrum (UV-vis-Nir) high-performance photodetector based on perovskite/quantum dot tandem heterostructure
abstract: A photodetector with a wide spectral response range from the ultraviolet to the near infrared region (250–1700 nm) was achieved with a FA 0.9 Cs 0.1 PbI 3 /PbS heterojunction structure, which demonstrated substantial performance.

title: A loofah-like carbon nanotube–Ag structure (LOOCAS) enabling piezoresistive transient sensing of dynamic strain and impulse load
abstract: A loofah-like carbon nanotube–Ag structure (termed LOOCAS) that realizes piezoresistive transient sensing in a polymer-encapsulated, stretchable, and flexible form factor.

title: Asymmetric phonon-drag effect and thermoelectric performance in PbTe under strain modulation
abstract: The intricate interplay between electrical and thermal transport in thermoelectric materials has long garnered significant theoretical interest. Despite long-standing theoretical interest in thermoelectric materials, conventional approaches neglect phonon-drag effect and...

title: High-Performance Ultra-Flexible Organic Solar Cells Enabled by Transfer-Free Polyimide/ITO Integrated Electrodes
abstract: Ultra-flexible organic solar cells (OSCs) have recently garnered growing interest with remarkable advancements in power conversion efficiency (PCE), yet they still suffer from complicated transfer process and delicate device configuration...

title: Light-induced deformation of side-chain azo-polyacrylate: insights from atomistic modeling
abstract: A fully atomistic model of a widely used azo-polyacrylate exposed to linearly polarized light reveals a non-monotonic deformation: rapid contraction from azobenzene reorientation followed by slower elongation due to polymer backbone alignment.

title: Tunable multicolor photoluminescence in metal–organic halides via pressure-treatment π–π interactions
abstract: A tunable multicolor PL emission is achieved in 1D MOH (4HP) 2 ZnCl 2 via pressure-treatment.

title: A portable salivary 17β-estradiol sensor with a label-free organic field-effect transistor
abstract: An OFET-based biosensor enables stable, non-invasive detection of 17β-estradiol in saliva with an ultralow detection limit of 0.27 fg mL −1 . The portable device shows high selectivity in complex samples, supporting personalized hormone monitoring.

title: Orbital model unravelling slipped-stacking induced quantum interference in tetrahedral 2D conjugated polymers
abstract: A double-orbital model effectively elucidates the origins of the enhanced quantum interference induced by interlayer interactions in 2D conjugated homopolymers.

title: First-principles study on magnetic MXenes for free radical trapping and sensing
abstract: Driven by first-principles design, magnetic MXenes are identified as highly efficient capture agents for radicals. Their pronounced magnetic and SHC variations pave the way for designing portable, signal-responsive radical scavengers.

title: A flexible pressure sensor based on a PVA/TA/CNT organogel for deep learning powered human gesture recognition
abstract: Gel-based pressure sensors are widely used in wearable electronics to provide features of human activity detection like gesture, gait, and sitting posture recognition.

title: Green, solvent-free synthesis of divinylbenzo[ c ][1,2,5]thiadiazole emitters via the hand-grinding Horner–Wadsworth–Emmons reaction for high-performance OLEDs
abstract: Divinylbenzo[ c ][1,2,5]thiadiazole (DBTDz)-based compounds were investigated as electroluminescent emitters for organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs), covering emission wavelengths from yellow to near-infrared (NIR).

title: Photoaligned nematic liquid crystals doped with palladium-immobilised carbon nanospheres for advanced low-voltage display and energy storage devices
abstract: Important properties of photoaligned nematic liquid crystal doped with palladium immobilised carbon nanoparticles.

title: Nanopatterning optimization of zinc phosphide: hole mobility up to 560 cm 2 /V s with selective area epitaxy
abstract: This study shows how selective area epitaxy can be used to improve carrier transport in Zn 3 P 2 and how structural defects, such as rotated domains (RD) interfaces or misfit dislocations (MDs), can affect the optoelectronic properties.

title: MXenes for personal thermal management: advances in mechanisms, applications, and materials design
abstract: A structure–function coupling in MXene-based PTM is systematically revealed.

title: An inclined-aligned ZnO nanorod array for self-powered all-scenario underwater weak light detection and optical communication
abstract: ZnO INRA-based PEC photodetectors for detecting weak UV light in harsh underwater environments with different pH levels and temperatures are demonstrated, featuring ultrahigh responsivity, excellent stability, and outstanding spectral selectivity.

title: Research progress in double perovskite oxide half-metals for magnetic storage technology
abstract: This review summarizes recent progress of double perovskite oxide half-metals (DPOHMs) for magnetic storage devices from experimental and theoretical aspects as well as their applications in MTJs, spin valves, and spin field-effect transistors.

title: Enhancing the strength of organo-hybrid SiO 2 aerogels with ultra-low dielectric properties
abstract: To overcome a key bottleneck in the development of high-performance low-dielectric materials, this work presents a novel functionalized hybrid strategy based on benzocyclobutene (BCB)-driven molecular scaffold-synergistic crosslinking.

title: Electrically active hydrogels based on PEDOT:PSS for neural cultures
abstract: Electrically active hydrogels are attracting significant interest as biohybrid materials for electrical interfacing with biological tissues.

title: Ionic–electronic transport interplay sustaining composition-tolerant terahertz absorption in organic mixed conductors
abstract: Organic mixed conductor thin films display a robust composition-tolerant terahertz absorption plateau of ∼50% due to the compensated ionic-electronic transport interplay.

title: Zwitterion-assisted growth and strain optimisation of perovskite single crystals for high-performance photon counting radiation detection
abstract: Zwitterion-assisted growth yields low-defect FAPbBr 3 single crystals, while optimised temperature ramping reduces strain. The devices show high charge transport, ultra-low dark current, and excellent room-temperature α-particle and X-ray detection.

title: Through-space cation–π quadrupole–monopole design enables selective suppression of third-order nonlinear optical Kerr effects
abstract: Intramolecular cation–π interaction inverts the nonlinear optical hierarchy, suppressing third-order behaviour to favour a pure fifth-order response.

title: Tuning the phase transition of TaS 2 polymorphs under high pressure and high temperature conditions
abstract: The polymorphic transition in TaS 2 has demonstrated rich tunability in physical properties, including modulated charge density wave (CDW) orders and superconductivity (SC), which is crucial for the development of new-concept and functional devices.

title: Controlled synthesis processes of cobalt containing borates in a multi-component flux system based on Bi 2 O 3 –MoO 3 –Na 2 O–B 2 O 3
abstract: Targeted Ni 2+ doping shifts the Co 3− x Ni x B 2 O 6 –Co 3− x Ni x BO 5 phase boundary and enables reversible switching at Co 3 B 2 O 6 –Co 3− x Ni x BO 5 –Ni 3 B 2 O 6 triple point at x = 1. The precise synthesis control and redox management in cobalt materials are demonstrated.

title: Effect of an ultrasonic field on the nucleation and growth mechanism of electrodeposited Cu thin films on a magnetron sputtered Cu layer
abstract: Composite copper foil and ultra-thin copper foil are prepared through the synergistic application of magnetron sputtering and electrodeposition techniques.

title: Copolyfluorene containing 4,4′-bis[4-(di- p -tolylamino)styryl]biphenyl as a chromophore for efficient blue polymer light-emitting diodes
abstract: DPAVBi is incorporated as a building block into polyfluorene-based copolymers for the first time. The optimized single-layer device thereby achieves a maximum luminance efficiency of > 6 cd A −1 with blue CIE coordinates of (0.15, 0.17–0.19).

title: Spin-polarized carriers and ferromagnetism induced by Pt incorporation in ZnO: an experimental and DFT assessment
abstract: In the present work, we report the synthesis and detailed investigations on the structural, microstructural, and magnetic properties of ZnO– x Pt ( x = 0.0, 0.02, 0.06, 0.10 wt%) nanocomposites, using experimental and theoretical approaches.

title: CeO 6 Te 2 nanosheets for multifunctional broadband visible light photodetection and NLO applications: an experimental and computational approach
abstract: The hydrothermal synthesis creates COT nanosheets with tuneable band gaps, strong photoresponse, and excellent third-order NLO properties, making them potential candidates in broadband photodetection and photonic applications.

title: A ZnS/H-NiS homojunction–heterojunction hybrid composite for high-performance triethylamine gas sensing
abstract: A ZnS/H-NiS homojunction–heterojunction composite is constructed to enhance interfacial charge modulation, leading to improved triethylamine gas-sensing performance.

title: Ionic liquid bridging buried interface for high-performance flexible self-powered perovskite nanowire photodetectors with excellent mechanical stability
abstract: This work uses BMIMHSO 4 to bridge and optimize the buried SnO 2 /MAPbI 3 nanowire interface, enabling flexible carbon-based self-powered perovskite photodetectors with high responsivity, wide LDR, and strong humidity/bending stability.

title: Metal-free quinoxaline-based donor–acceptor small molecule for efficient water splitting across universal pH conditions
abstract: TTQx, a metal-free D–A small molecule, functions as an efficient bifunctional HER/OER electrocatalyst, delivering low HER overpotentials of −108, −152, and −163 mV and OER overpotentials of 233, 251, and 271 mV at 10 mA cm −2 .

title: A light-emitting and detecting bifunctional device based on the bias-selective redox activity of a polymer
abstract: A light-emitting electrochemical cell and a photodetector in a unified platform are developed, using bias-selective ion migration for a selective p- or n-doped photoactive layer.

title: Adaptive self-powered photodetection and neuromorphic computing in a CuCrP 2 S 6 ionotronic device
abstract: In-sensor computing devices integrating sensing and processing functions are emerging as key enablers for next-generation artificial vision systems.

title: Hydrated Zirconium Coordination Enhanced the Photoluminescence Performance in 0D Cadmium Manganese Based Perovskites
abstract: Mn2+ activated hybrid perovskites containing Cd are appealing single matrix emitters for tunable color and dual mode luminescence, yet in most Cd/Mn systems, absorption and sensitization are still regulated mainly...

title: Site-Selective Deuteration Modulates Vibronic Coupling and Excited-State Dynamics in Deep-Blue Thermally Activated Delayed Fluorescence Emitters
abstract: The development of efficient and stable deep-blue emitters has long remained a critical challenge for organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs), with deuteration emerging as a promising strategy. Here, site-selective deuteration of...

title: Co-substitution of Zn 2+ /Ge 4+ in Er 3+ -activated garnet phosphors to enhance luminescence properties and optical thermometry
abstract: A heterovalent co-substitution strategy (Zn 2+ + Ge 4+ = 2 Ga 3+ ) was adopted in Gd 3 Zn x Ga 5−2 x Ge x O 12 :Er 3+ (GZG 5−2 x G) solid solutions, inducing an over three-fold enhancement of green emission and optical thermometry, with S r > 1.20% K −1 and δ T ≈ 0.4 K.

title: Tailoring microwave dielectric properties through high-entropy strategy in spinel-type titanium ceramics
abstract: Enhanced microwave dielectric properties achieved via configurational entropy engineering.

title: Theoretical screening of Co- and Mo-based binary alloys as interconnect metals
abstract: This work presents the candidate screening procedure of binary intermetallic compounds for future metal interconnects using cohesive energy, and directionally averaged figure of merit.

title: Spin exchange, electronic correlation, and thermoelectric transport in Rb 2 GeMBr 6 (M = V, Mn, Ni) halide double perovskites from first principles calculations
abstract: At 300 K, Rb 2 GeVBr 6 , Rb 2 GeMnBr 6 , and Rb 2 GeNiBr 6 exhibit high thermoelectric figure of merit ( zT ) values of 1.00, 0.99, and 0.98, respectively, underscoring their significant potential for renewable energy applications.

title: Reversible switching through irradiation of capacitors and organic transistors employing dielectrics blended with non-ionic molecular photoswitches
abstract: Optical control of polymer transistors and capacitors is achieved by incorporating molecular switches into polymer dielectrics. The photochromic molecules induce additional functionality enabling reversible programming of the devices with light.

title: Fine-tuning the backbone coplanarity and energy level of diketopyrrolopyrrole-based conjugated polymers for single-walled carbon nanotube sorting and field-effect transistor applications
abstract: Three DPP polymers are applied to wrap and sort the semiconducting carbon nanotubes. The chemical structures, energy level alignments, and the field-effect transistor device comprising the semiconducting carbon nanotube channel are presented.

title: Atomistic insights into hydrogen migration in IGZO from machine-learning interatomic potential: linking atomic diffusion to device performance
abstract: Understanding hydrogen diffusion is critical for improving the reliability and performance of oxide thin-film transistors (TFTs), where hydrogen plays a key role in carrier modulation and bias instability.

title: Magnetostrictronics: an example case of bulk L1 0 -MnPt from first-principles calculations
abstract: A first-principles approach to Magnetostrictronics in the case of bulk L1 0 -MnPt. Mn and Pt atoms are marked with deep purple and grey solid balls.

title: Instantaneous aqueous spray synthesis of halide-encapsulated CsPb(Cl/Br) 3 quantum dots for stable green to pure-blue mini/micro-LEDs
abstract: CsPb(Cl/Br) 3 quantum dots were synthesized in under 10 seconds in water without organic solvents. Emission was tuned from green to blue. The powder was used in luminescent materials, labeling, anti-counterfeiting, and mini-LEDs.

title: An integrated high-throughput screening framework for perovskite light-emitting diode passivators based on machine learning
abstract: An integrated ML-based high-throughput framework enables efficient passivator screening.

title: Learning the limits: how data, diversity, and representation control machine-learning predictions of reorganisation energy
abstract: Accurate and scalable prediction of hole and electron reorganisation energies remains a bottleneck in data-driven design of organic semiconductors, as routine ab initio calculations are impractical for large molecular libraries.

title: Aggregation-induced emission-active thermally activated delayed fluorescent materials for solution-processed organic light-emitting diodes: a review
abstract: This review tries to give insight into rational molecular design strategies for the construction of next-generation AIE-active TADF and MR-TADF emitters for high-performance OLED applications.

title: Structure–property relationships in organic framework materials: the role of linkage modes and building units
abstract: Common types of organic framework materials and their applications in conductivity, catalysis, adsorption, separation, and biomedicine.

title: Development of high-efficiency near-infrared organic light-emitting diodes using TPA-DCPP via interface exciplex formation
abstract: Near-infrared organic light-emitting diodes (NIR OLEDs) hold great promise for applications in biomedical imaging, optical communication, and night-vision technologies.

title: K/Na Co-doped carbon nitride: a robust luminescent material with high thermal endurance and UV durability
abstract: K/Na co-doping synergistically enhances carbon nitride's photoluminescence and exceptional thermal-UV durability.

title: Gold-coated graphene-embedded micron-sized polystyrene core–shell structures with an unconventional Hall anomaly and Berry curvature
abstract: Unusual quantum phenomena in graphene are driven by non-zero Berry curvature and orbital magnetization.

title: Triarylamine-BODIPYs exhibiting record hole mobility: synthesis, photophysical, electrochemical, spectroelectrochemical, and charge carrier mobility studies
abstract: Triarylamine-BODIPYs BTA1 – BTA4 were synthesized, and their photophysical, electrochemical, spectroelectrochemical, and charge carrier mobility were studied. BTA2 – BTA4 exhibit high hole mobilities of ∼10 −2 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: Silicon–PEDOT:PSS hybrid heterojunction solar cells: potential, recent developments and challenges
abstract: The roadmap for >20% efficient Si-PEDOT:PSS HSCs is presented, highlighting the role of interface engineering, light trapping mechanism, and surface passivation in overcoming PCE limits and stability challenges for flexible PVs and niche BIPVs.

title: Investigation of high-energy proton irradiation effects on carbon nanotube field-effect transistors
abstract: CNTFETs under 200 MeV proton irradiation show non-monotonic responses: negative V th shift, while SS , G m , and R c degrade at low fluence but recover at high fluence, transitioning from trap accumulation to adsorbate desorption and barrier modulation.

title: High-performance self-healing epoxy resin film based on the synergistic effect of photothermal conversion and dynamic bonds
abstract: Through the synergistic effect of dynamic diselenide bonds and MXene, the film-forming property and mechanical property of epoxy resin were improved, while endowing it with photothermal conversion capability and outstanding self-healing performance.

title: Enhancing the proton conduction in α-zirconium phosphate nanoplates by amine intercalation
abstract: This work demonstrates that intercalating an optimal amount of amine into α-ZrP nanoplates is an efficient strategy for modulating their proton-conduction characteristics, consequently extending their range of potential applications.

title: Detection of chloride ions in hydrochloric acidic wastewater using DBSA-stabilised CsPbBr 3 perovskite quantum dots
abstract: Perovskite quantum dots (PQDs) show great promise for chloride ion (Cl − ) detection in acidic laboratory wastewater owing to their excellent optical properties.

title: A composition-dependent structural and gradually tunable bandgap of GeS 1− x Se x alloys synthesized via chemical vapor transport
abstract: Composition-dependent strain evolution in CVT-grown GeS 1− x Se x alloys generates strain-disordered crystalline structures with abundant interfacial regions.

title: Unlocking the full potential of spiro-OMeTAD in perovskite solar cells: towards synthetic routes, doping mechanism, degradation, and stability
abstract: A summary of recent advances in spiro-OMeTAD-based HTMs for PSCs, covering four main aspects: (1) synthetic routes, (2) doping mechanisms, (3) degradation processes, and (4) strategies for enhancing stability.

title: Disclosing the molecular structure and dynamics of naphthalene diimide based organic semiconductors in the solid state
abstract: Naphthalene diimide (NDI) based molecules are soft organic functional materials investigated for their n-type semiconducting properties and, recently, for their coupled electronic and ionic transport functionalities.

title: Hydrogen-Bonding Assisted Nano-Aggregation and Electropolymerization of C3-D-A-s-Triazine-2,4,6-Triamide for High-Area-Capacitance Supercapacitors.
abstract: Fabricating strictly organic, highly porous electrodes without sacrificial templates or additives remains a formidable challenge for energy storage devices. Herein, we report a novel additive-free strategy for constructing hierarchically porous...

title: NIR-II electroluminescence from lanthanide-doped nanoparticles was achieved through triplet excitons
abstract: In this Highlight, we present a recent advance in near-infrared LEDs based on electroluminescence from lanthanide-doped fluoride nanoparticles. We review the mechanisms and propose future directions for materials, devices, and applications.

title: Intermolecular charge-transfer phosphorescence in organometallic–organic doped crystals dominated by the iridium complex lattice
abstract: Charge-transfer transitions constitute a pivotal mechanism for modulating the photophysical characteristics in organic luminescent materials.

title: Ultrasensitive SERS detection using UV-ozone treated Nb 2 O 5− x nanosheets coupled with plasmonic nanoparticles: an integrated experimental and theoretical study
abstract: An integrated experimental and theoretical study of ultrasensitive SERS detection via synergistic electromagnetic and chemical enhancement mechanisms in defect-engineered Nb 2 O 5 nanosheets coupled with plasmonic metal nanoparticles.

title: Full-range luminescence color tuning from blue to red in generic single-boron multi-resonance delayed fluorescence emitters
abstract: Full-range luminescence color tuning from blue to red was achieved via simple design strategies in generic single-boron, multi-resonance delayed fluorescence emitters.

title: Introducing vacancy defects to induce ferroelectric-like switching in antiferroelectric oxides
abstract: Ferroelectric-like polarization switching is realised in antiferroelectric oxides through vacancy defect strategy.

title: Ultrafast carrier and coherent phonon dynamics in van der Waals metallic ferromagnet Fe 4 GeTe 2
abstract: We investigate the carrier and phonon dynamics of Fe 4 GeTe 2 via pump-probe reflectivity measurements. We reveal anomalous dynamic optical responses around T SR and T c . The frequency and dephasing rate are well described using the anharmonicity model.

title: Model of luminescence and delayed luminescence correlated blinking in single CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals
abstract: We propose a single CsPbBr 3 nanocrystal luminescence blinking model. The model successfully reproduces the two-dimensional distributions of the PL emission components, the photon distribution function and the power spectral density.

title: Encrypted mirror prepared based on the linearization of circularly polarized light
abstract: A patterned cholesteric liquid crystal polymer network mirror was prepared, which was suitably applied for encryption.

title: Efficient visible-light photocatalytic degradation of rhodamine B dye based on water-stable (4,4′-VDP)Pb 2 Br 6 perovskites
abstract: In this work, a water-stable organic–inorganic hybrid perovskite material [(4,4′-VDP)Pb 2 Br 6 ] is successfully synthesized and applied for the first time for the photocatalytic degradation of RhB in aqueous solutions.

title: Silica-reinforced dual-network ionogel fibers with excellent mechanical properties for wearable alternating current electroluminescent devices
abstract: Wearable displays, considered the ultimate platform for human-body information acquisition and processing, demand conformable and versatile form factors that conventional planar and rigid technologies cannot satisfy.

title: Hybrid magneto-excitonic polariton metastructures: room-temperature strong coupling in all-oxide platforms
abstract: Breaking optical reciprocity at room temperature in a fully dielectric platform remains a demanding yet impactful goal for integrated photonics.

title: Composite thermal interface materials of gallium-based liquid metals and CuGa 2 with high thermal conductivity and long-term stability
abstract: A gallium-based liquid metal/CuGa 2 thermal interface material was prepared, exhibiting a thermal conductivity as high as 74.92 W (m K) −1 .

title: A multi-state spectroscopic approach to carrier dynamics in thin-film topological insulators
abstract: Understanding how to differentiate the dynamics of surface and bulk states is essential for advancing carrier physics in topological insulators.

title: Enhanced H 2 S gas detection using low-cost carbon-loaded CuCrO 2 –CuO nanomaterials
abstract: Effective monitoring of H 2 S gas is crucial, as inhaling H 2 S poses a significant risk to human health, potentially leading to grave harm or even death.

title: Tailoring non-fullerene acceptors to enhance molecular ordering in inverted bilayer organic solar cells
abstract: Transfer-printed inverted bilayer OSCs employing BTP-eC9 achieve enhanced performance and stability through a smooth film that extends carrier lifetime, improves charge extraction, establishing NFA morphology optimization as key to efficient devices.

title: Excitation-selective coexistence of upconversion and persistent luminescence in Pr 3+ -doped eulytite-type phosphates towards advanced anti-counterfeiting
abstract: The characteristic spectra of a 0.1% Pr 3+ -doped Ba 3 Lu(PO 4 ) 3 sample, with X-ray or 444 nm excitation on and off, along with digital photos showing XRL and PersL of the sample after X-ray irradiation.

title: A regioisomeric phenanthroquinoxaline-based small molecular defect passivation layer for enhanced efficiency in stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: D–A–D surface passivators were employed to heal perovskite surface defects to enhance PCE and stability.

title: The effect of torsion-inducing methyl substituents on the delayed luminescence of star-shaped emitters
abstract: We probed the impact of meta - against para -conjugation in donor–acceptor emitters containing phenylene-linked carbazole and 1,3,5-triazine moieties and their effect on thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF).

title: Polymorphism-controlled charge transport in T-shaped π-conjugated crystals: high mobility via a double-column pathway
abstract: Polymorphism-controlled charge transport in T-shaped π-conjugated crystals featuring high mobility via double-column pathways.

title: Optoelectronic control of ferroelectric field coupling for enhanced ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: Wide and ultrawide bandgap semiconductors have emerged as a class of ultraviolet (UV) photosensitive semiconductors, displaying huge potential for internet of things, optical communications and imaging.

title: Deciphering the role of anionic group in zwitterionic electron transport layers for organic solar cells
abstract: The intrinsic advantages of zwitterions have driven their widespread use as electron transport layers (ETLs) in organic solar cells (OSCs). However, the role of the anionic group in governing interfacial...

title: Carbon doping in GeTe drives differences in local structure and properties
abstract: Advances in low-power, energy-efficient information storage and computing require understanding and controlling the atomic and nanoscale structures of functional materials, such as phase-change materials. Phase-change memory technology enables nonvolatile, low-power...

title: Quantitative evaluation of mechanochromic luminescent materials under controlled grinding stimuli
abstract: Custom apparatus enables quantitative analysis of mechanochromic luminescence (MCL) by controlled grinding and kinetic evaluation of emission-spectral conversion.

title: Biomass-derived fibrous materials for flexible electronics
abstract: Biomass-based fibrous materials provide sustainable platforms for flexible electronics. This review highlights their structural design, fabrication strategies, and emerging applications in sensing, therapeutic electronics, and energy devices.

title: Efficient long-wavelength-excited triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion with phenyl-diketopyrrolopyrroles as the annihilators
abstract: Dihedral-angle-tuned DPP annihilators achieve small Δ E g with the sensitizer, giving 8.6% long-wavelength-excited TTA-UC and 0.55 eV anti-Stokes shift, enabling 721 nm light-driven spatially confined photopolymerization.

title: Tunable negative and low thermal expansion in magnetic calcite-type Fe 1− x Cr x BO 3 ( x = 0.0, 0.14, 1.0) borates: a comprehensive in situ study
abstract: Fe 1− x Cr x BO 3 borates demonstrate composition-tuned magnetic and thermal properties. All samples exhibit LTE, while NTE emerges exclusively at x = 0.14 near RT, promising for optical and magneto-optical applications.

title: Low-temperature crystallization of the K 2 TiF 6 :Mn 4+ phosphor with near-unity quantum efficiency in dilute HF solution
abstract: Dilute-HF (≤30 wt%) + low- T cooling (20 → −40 °C) leads to the growth of mm-scale K 2 TiF 6 :Mn 4+ platelets with near-unity IQE and 10× better water resistance than powders, enabling 124 lm W −1 warm WLEDs.

title: Flexible, self-powered UVC photodetectors enabled by low-temperature solution-processed SnO 2 : PEI nanocomposites
abstract: Balancing the compact film-forming ability of the PEI binder with its insulating nature, a SnO 2 : PEI film enables efficient charge transport and can be fabricated by low-temperature solution processing for flexible UVC photodetectors.

title: Correlation between domain structure dynamics and electromechanical behavior in Sn-doped BaTiO 3
abstract: The domain structure of Sn-doped BaTiO 3 was examined with piezoresponse force microscopy and spatially resolved Raman spectroscopy, and derived reversible and irreversible contributions from Rayleigh analysis of the dielectric response.

title: External field control and characteristic quantities of Rashba spin orbit coupling in MA 2 Z 4 derived monolayer materials
abstract: MA 2 Z 4 -based 2D materials enable tunable Rashba spin–orbit coupling. Intrinsic RSOC can be achieved via structural engineering and elemental substitution, while its strength is further adjustable through strain and electric fields.

title: Stoichiometric engineering for large-size CsPbBr 3 crystal growth and gamma-ray detection optimization
abstract: By tailoring the stoichiometric ratio to suppress the formation of secondary phases, planar detectors fabricated from CsPbBr 3 single crystal exhibit energy resolutions of 9.16% and 12.69% for 137 Cs@662 keV and 241 Am@59.5 keV γ-ray, respectively.

title: Dendritic thermally activated delayed fluorescence sensitizers for efficient deep-blue narrowband organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Dendritic TADF sensitizers are developed by strategically introducing a methyl group into a benzonitrile/carbazole dendron framework, giving maximum EQE of 19.0% and CIE coordinates of (0.142, 0.056) for solution-processed deep-blue narrowband OLEDs.

title: Multiphase conduction and piezoelectric properties of 2D (GA) 2 PbI 4 Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites for energy harvesting applications
abstract: Multiphase piezoelectric (GA) 2 PbI 4 perovskites exhibiting high dc conductivity, as observed from temperature dependence of conductivity spectra, enable nanogenerator-driven LED illumination.

title: Spacer-directed comparable and contrasting optical properties in D–A molecules enabling the fabrication of efficient novel thin-film phototransistors
abstract: D–σ–A and D–π–A type donor–acceptor molecules have been developed and effect of spacer and donor strength on photophysical properties and application has been studied.

title: Room-temperature liquid-phase exfoliation of MoO 3 nanosheets for photochromic smart windows
abstract: Novel photochromic MoO 3 nanosheets were prepared from commercial MoO 3 via a facile liquid-phase exfoliation method in this work. The fabricated MoO 3 film exhibits excellent photochromic properties, demonstrating great potential in smart window.

title: RRAM-enabled reservoir computing: from interfacial switching dynamics to scalable and hybrid architectures
abstract: Summarizing RRAM-enabled reservoir computing, linking device nonlinearity and volatile relaxation to fading-memory dynamics, covering wide and hybrid optical–RRAM architectures, discussing readout learning for hardware implementation.

title: Hierarchical chiral amplification via grafting and co-assembly in flexible polymers for full-color circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: This work describes a novel polymer enabling a helical co-assembly strategy that achieves both full-color and white circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) with a unique combination of simplicity, tunability, and scalability.

title: Beyond single-ion doping: K + –Bi 3+ synergy enables crop-tailored blue luminescence through crystal field engineering
abstract: The K + ion-modulated Cs 2 MgSi 5 O 12 :Bi 3+ blue phosphor exhibits remarkable performance enhancement after modulation, demonstrating enormous application potential in the field of agriculture lighting.

title: A review of elastic conductive textiles: classification, fabrication, and emerging applications
abstract: This review categorizes elastic conductive textiles into three fabrication strategies: structural design, coating/deposition, and composite fiber approaches, analyzes mechanisms, compares trade-offs, and highlights emerging wearable applications.

title: Luminescence properties of Ce 3+ - and Bi 3+ -doped Cs 2 LiInCl 6 single crystals: a phase-transition material growth
abstract: Through an analysis of the cubic-to-hexagonal phase transition in Cs 2 LiInCl 6 , successfully grew high-quality transparent single crystals. The properties of Ce 3+ - and Bi 3+ -doped samples were characterized under both ultraviolet irradiation.

title: Energy dissipation and electromechanical response in dielectric actuation of liquid crystal elastomers
abstract: Dielectric liquid crystal elastomer actuators (DLCEAs) offer a promising route to low-density actuators with directional control and high power density.

title: Ionically conductive hydrogels constructed with the participation of aliphatic polycarbonates for multi-scenario flexible electronics and bioelectrical signal sensing
abstract: A dual physically crosslinked hydrogel exhibits good comprehensive performance, achieves multi-scenario applications including bioelectrical sensing, writing recognition, and robotic control.

title: Role of positional isomerism in A-site organic cation: structural variation driven photophysical and ferroelectric responses in centrosymmetric layered perovskites
abstract: Positional isomerism in the A-site cation modulates the crystal packing and photophysical properties of layered perovskites and subsequently, drives defect-mediated ferroelectric responses by reducing crystallographic symmetry.

title: Optimizing fibril morphology of polymer donors by increasing dipole–dipole interactions using additives for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: The fibrilization of polymer donors is achieved via additive induced dipole–dipole interactions, which effectively improves the power conversion efficiency of organic solar cells.

title: Design of a ppb-level gas sensor based on hollow nanosphere structures for H 2 S detection in aerospace environments
abstract: Hollow CuO–SnO 2 nanospheres deliver stable ppb-level H 2 S sensing in an aerospace environment.

title: Light- and electrically-programmable logic devices, hybrid communication, and image recognition technology based on WSe 2 /h-BN/Gr heterostructures
abstract: A WSe 2 /h-BN/Gr heterostructure local gate photodetector: synergistic modulation of local graphene gate voltages and optical signals enables reconfigurable logic operations, optical communication, and image recognition in one architecture.

title: Transformation from room temperature phosphorescence to TADF enhanced dual emission by aggregation-induced quantum interference
abstract: Two crystals based one compound with similar packing exhibit distinct luminescence: monomer-like green RTP and yellow TADF dual emission, tuned by aggregation-induced quantum interference.

title: Highly sensitive indirect X-ray detector using an ultrasonically exfoliated MoS 2 monolayer in the active layer
abstract: Exfoliated 25 nm MoS 2 monolayers uniformly dispersed in a PBDB-T:ITIC X-ray detector increased sensitivity by enhancing carrier transport (mobility, carrier lifetime) characteristics and reducing defect density.

title: Metal–p(C 3 O 2 ) X assembly enables construction of 2D metal/C nanocomposites for broadband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Preparation of OC–M and EMW absorption mechanisms.

title: Energy-level modification of perovskite nanocrystals by dipole ligand treatment for green light-emitting diodes
abstract: Dipole ligands are used to modify the perovskite NCs surface, thus tailoring their energy level. LEDs exhibit significantly improved performance, with maximum luminance increasing from 31 145 to 64 383 cd m −2 and EQE increasing from 5.72 to 8.77%.

title: A simplified machine learning workflow for identifying potential singlet fission candidates: benzannulated biphenylenes as a case study
abstract: Singlet fission (SF) can improve solar-cell efficiency. We present a machine-learning workflow combining AQME, ROBERT, and DFT calculations to screen 3835 benzannulated biphenylenes, identifying 505 promising candidates for SF.

title: On-demand optical printing of Ag-based neuromorphic devices for brain-inspired learning
abstract: A neuromorphic device fabricated using a white-light-based plasmonic printing technique demonstrates brain-inspired learning.

title: Crystal orientation-dependent degradation of MAPbBr 2.5 Cl 0.5 single crystals under X-ray irradiation
abstract: The irradiation damage caused by high dose X-ray in CH 3 NH 3 PbBr 2.5 Cl 0.5 perovskite single crystals is observed. The irradiation could caused defects which is confirmed by transmission electron microscopy and other measurements.

title: Tuning the optoelectronic properties of iminodibenzyl–triphenylamine based semiconductors: hybrid self-powered photodetector applications
abstract: IDB-TPA-based organic semiconductors enable efficient interfacial charge transfer and high-performance self-powered photodetection in Si heterojunction devices.

title: MXene–TMD heterostructure photodetectors: engineering the Ti 3 C 2 /SnS 2 interface for high-speed visible light detection
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) materials with engineered heterojunctions offer exciting opportunities for next-generation optoelectronic devices.

title: Role of spin disorder and crystallinity in the exchange bias effect of the Fe 3 O 4 @Mn x O y nanocomposites
abstract: In this study, we report a correlation between structural crystallinity and the exchange bias effect in a bimagnetic nanocomposite system.

title: Unlocking high absorption efficiency of Cr 3+ ions in magnesium titanates with intrinsically high octahedron distortion
abstract: The high intrinsic octahedral distortion of the MgTiO 3 host enables ultrahigh absorption efficiency even at low Cr 3+ doping concentrations. In 3+ substitution improves internal quantum efficiency and thermal stability in MgTiO 3 :Cr 3+ .

title: Intrinsic piezoresistive mechanism of conductive porous nano-composites with linearity assumption: theoretical and experimental analyses
abstract: A model coupling Timoshenko beam with quantum tunneling for PPNs establishes ligament bending over pore closure as the dominant mechanism at small-to-moderate strains. Experimentally validated, it allows predictive design of high-sensitivity sensors.

title: Horizontally oriented pyrido[2,3- b ]pyrazine-based thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitters exhibiting bipolar charge transport for efficient organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: The strategic use of carbazole or phenothiazine donors in pyrido[2,3- b ] pyrazine emitters governs thermal stability and horizontal molecular orientation, enabling high-performance TADF-based OLEDs with external quantum efficiencies up to 21.3%.

title: Energy level alignment and defect passivation realized by a dipole molecular bridge for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: AIDCN was utilized for the first time as a dipole molecular bridge at the perovskite/hole transport layer (HTL) interface to simultaneously achieve energy level alignment and defect passivation.

title: Deuterium-enabled stabilization of metal/oxide interfaces via suppressed oxygen diffusion in BEOL-compatible InGaZnO thin-film transistors
abstract: This study systematically investigates the influence of post-metallization annealing (PMA) ambient on the electrical and interfacial properties of a-IGZO thin-film transistors (TFTs) incorporating BEOL-compatible tungsten (W) contacts.

title: Organic thermoelectric films: achieving high conductivity and power factor through sulfonated-poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) and single-walled carbon nanotube composites
abstract: The quest for sustainable energy solutions is critical amidst environmental challenges.

title: Tuned X-ray-to-visible light conversion via a chromophore doping strategy for enhanced scintillation performance
abstract: This work presents a doping strategy for organic host–guest crystals. Using tailored phenyl chromophores, it achieves widely tunable emission, ultrafast decay, and significantly enhanced scintillation performance.

title: High-performance electrode-controlled MoTe 2 memristor for dual-mode synaptic plasticity in neuromorphic systems
abstract: A bottom-embedded electrode MoTe 2 memtransistor suppresses Fermi-level pinning, emulates homosynaptic/heterosynaptic plasticity via H 2 O/O 2 adsorption, and enables 90.32% MNIST recognition accuracy for neuromorphic systems.

title: Zwitterion-derived ligand-coated ZnO quantum dots engineered via an organometallic approach
abstract: We report an organometallic synthesis of ZnO QDs with zwitterionic ligands, showing that the ligand type and synthetic conditions control size, surface passivation, and PLQY (up to 41.8%), highlighting their potential as luminescent nanomaterials.

title: Comparative optical characterization of CsPbBr 3 quantum dots and CVD-grown CsPbBr 3 microplates
abstract: We systematically investigate the influence of morphology on exciton dynamics and bandgap behavior in all-inorganic CsPbBr 3 nanostructures by comparing colloidal quantum dots (QDs) and chemical vapor deposition (CVD)-grown microplates.

title: Super high ionic conductivity and an all-weather adaptable LiCl-based hydrogel electrolyte for electrochromic devices
abstract: Structure, photographs and performance of all-weather adaptable ECDs.

title: Employing Eu,Tb-doped HfO 2 nanocrystals as built-in cryogenic luminescent nanothermometers for commercial coated conductors
abstract: This work presents a novel approach for detecting quench or hot spot regimes in coated conductors by depositing luminescent nanocrystals operating as nanothermometers onto the silver protective layer, allowing simultaneous temperature measurements.

title: A sandwich structure FPI-PEO/Celgard/FPI-PEO separator with polysulfide shuttle suppression and dendrite puncture resistance for room-temperature sodium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A sandwich structure separator with polysulfide shuttle suppression and dendrite puncture resistance for room-temperature sodium–sulfur batteries.

title: Multi-objective Bayesian optimization with human-in-the-loop for flexible neuromorphic electronics fabrication
abstract: A fusion of researcher observation and machine learning is used to fabricate tunable dielectric on PET by photonic curing for neuromorphic devices.

title: Metal–monolayer MoSi 2 N 4 junctions: metal dimension dependent Fermi-level pinning
abstract: High-performance semiconductor devices demand ohmic contacts with low contact resistances, while such low-resistance ohmic contacts are closely linked to the weak Fermi level pinning (FLP) effect.

title: Subzero temperature-induced lattice compression enables high carrier mobility in MAPbBr 3 single crystals
abstract: Freezing-temperature crystallization induces lattice compression in MAPbBr 3 single crystals, leading to bandgap tunability, extended carrier lifetime, and high mobility. These properties enable low-voltage and high-performance optoelectronic devices.

title: High-throughput in situ fabrication of fibrous membranes enables scalable passive radiative cooling
abstract: Solution blow spinning produces recyclable and amphiphobic SEBS-Y 2 O 3 fibrous membranes with 8–12× faster fabrication than electrospinning, conformability to nonplanar surfaces, delayed ice melting, and sub-ambient cooling up to 7.0 °C.

title: Revealing the excited-state emission pathways in indolizine-derived TADF molecules by photoluminescence and electroluminescence
abstract: Differences between photoluminescence (PL) and electroluminescence (EL) spectra might be observed for some TADF compounds because photoexcitation preferentially populates locally excited states, whereas electroexcitation favors charge-transfer states.

title: Prediction of high-temperature superconductivity in lithium-doped thorium superhydrides under high pressures
abstract: Li 2 ThH 17 , featuring H 28 and H 20 cages, is predicted to exhibit a T c of 162 K at 300 GPa and increases to 187 K at 350 GPa.

title: Advanced engineering of an S-scheme BiCoO 3 /Co-g-C 3 N 4 composite material supported on reduced graphene oxide for efficient photocatalytic activity
abstract: Photo-assisted catalysis, distinguished by its mild reaction conditions, robust efficacy, and sustainable nature, is envisioned as a transformative solution for future environmental protection.

title: Interfacial charge-transfer in CoAl-LDH/CoO x for photocatalytic dye degradation and UV/H 2 O 2 assisted real-wastewater treatment
abstract: Photocatalysis can decolorize dye-laden wastewaters using light, but practical catalysts often suffer from narrow absorption, charge recombination, and stability limits.

title: Direct-ink-writing of multi-functional dynamic polymer-carbon nanotube hydrogel composites
abstract: Additive manufacturing of conductive hydrogel composites has huge potential in developing advanced materials.

title: Visual detection of the topology freezing transition temperature of vitrimers based on fluorescein-soaked coatings from photonic crystals and vitrimers
abstract: A visual detection method without the use of expensive equipment is proposed to find T v of vitrimers based on fluorescein-soaked photonic coatings from lower PCs and upper vitrimers.

title: Room-temperature surface engineering of CuInS 2 nanocrystals: synergistic effects of ionic salts and dodecanethiol for enhanced optical properties
abstract: Copper indium sulfide (CIS) quantum dots are promising non-toxic NIR emitters, but low photoluminescence quantum yield from surface defects and trap-assisted recombination limit their use, while core/shell approaches increase size and heterogeneity.

title: Charge-transfer doping in C 3 N 2 vdWHs endows sub-5 nm FETs with near-Boltzmann switching and ultrahigh on-state current
abstract: This work addresses the critical challenge of scaling transistors beyond Moore's law by proposing a novel two-dimensional channel material composed of monolayer C 3 N 2 and its graphene heterostructure.

title: Nonlinear optical properties of a Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5 film saturable absorber for short-pulse solid-state lasers
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate passively Q-switched and Q-switched mode-locked Nd:YVO 4 lasers based on Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5 (GST) films as a saturable absorber.

title: White light-emitting electrochemical cells: progress, challenges, and outlook
abstract: White light-emitting electrochemical cells (LECs) are advanced by rational emissive material design and device engineering that combine electrical and optical optimization.

title: Functional organic room-temperature phosphorescent materials
abstract: Functionalizing organic RTP materials widens their scope and endows them with immense applicability across diverse fields. These functionalities can either be intrinsic or transductive, with multiple design strategies preferring polymeric systems.

title: Hybrid capture layer synaptic transistors with visible spectrum perception for artificial vision
abstract: The hybrid capture layer synaptic transistor simulates an artificial visual system and enables the sensing, memory, and processing of light signals across the visible spectrum, from red to blue light.

title: Engineering the interface of cellulose nanocrystals for transient and bioactive iontronics based on protein eutectogels
abstract: Dual physical–chemical modification of cellulose nanocrystals enables high-performance gelatin eutectogels. Dynamic CNC–COOH/TA assemblies impart strain-sensing capability and contact-mediated antibacterial functionality for greener iontronics.

title: Suppression of pyrene excimers through sulfur-bridge oxidation and steric shielding
abstract: Oxidation-state tuning of a sulfur bridge controls packing and excited states in sterically shielded pyrenes. Sulfide-to-sulfone conversion shifts HLCT to localized emission, enabling narrow-band, efficient, excimer-free solid-state luminescence.

title: Synergistic integration of disordered Tm:CaGdAlO 4 single-crystal fibers with oxidation-resistant MXene for high-performance 2 µm lasers
abstract: Disordered Tm:CaGdAlO 4 single-crystal fibers combined with oxidation-resistant MXene enable high-efficiency CW and high-performance pulsed 2 µm lasers, providing a scalable strategy for advanced mid-infrared photonic devices.

title: Combinatorial Co-sputtering of Mg–Bi–O thin films: structural evolution and tunable optoelectronic properties
abstract: We report the first high-throughput Mg–Bi–O thin-film synthesis via combinatorial co-sputtering, revealing composition-dependent structural evolution and tunable bandgaps.

title: Recent advancements and future challenges of perovskite-based supercapacitors
abstract: Perovskite materials have attracted increasing interests as advanced electrode materials for next generation of supercapacitors with high energy density, good electrical conductivity and cyclic stability. Graphical abstract partly generated by AI.

title: Strain-tunable spin filtering and valley splitting coexisting with the anomalous Hall effect in the 2D half-metallic VSe 2 /VN heterostructure: toward a unified spintronic–valleytronic platform
abstract: A 2D VSe 2 /VN platform for integrated spintronic and valleytronic functionality.

title: Comparative study of high- k metal oxide–PVP/PMMA hybrid gate dielectrics for low-temperature, all-solution-processed flexible In 2 O 3 thin film transistors
abstract: Hybrid high- k MO–PVP/PMMA dielectrics enable low-temperature, solution-processed In 2 O 3 TFTs with improved electrical performance and mechanical flexibility.

title: Progress and perspectives on Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene-based composite materials for electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: In this perspective, we summarize recent advances in MXene-based composites for EMW absorption and reveal critical issues and highlight possible future research directions.

title: Multimodal Luminescence in Ca2Ga2GeO7:Er3+,Yb3+ Phosphors for Optical Thermometry and Anti-Counterfeiting Applications
abstract: Lanthanide-activated multifunctional luminescent materials capable of simultaneous spectral conversion and optical sensing are highly desirable for next-generation photonic security and sensing technologies. Herein, we report dual-mode up- and down-conversion luminescence...

title: Sustainable upcycling of waste low-density polyethylene droppers into active materials for lithium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: This work tackles the growing plastic waste in landfills and the rising demand for sustainable energy storage by upcycling LDPE waste into affordable, high-performance electrode materials for Li-ion capacitors.

title: Synergistic design of a deep eutectic solvent-based conductive hydrogel enabled by Al 3+ coordination and MXene integration for flexible sensing
abstract: Synergistic Al 3+ coordination and MXene integration enable a DES-based conductive hydrogel with enhanced mechanical strength, conductivity and sensing performance for flexible strain sensing.

title: Strain and interface effects on magnetocrystalline anisotropy of MnN
abstract: First explicit calculations and explanation of the effects of strain and Fermi level tuning on the magnetocrystalline anisotropy of MnN.

title: Sequential quenching to predict semiconductor defect concentrations from formation & migration energies: the case of CdTe:As doping
abstract: Defects freeze at different temperatures during cooldown, producing non-equilibrium semiconductor states beyond equilibrium and full-quench predictions.

title: 1532 nm-driven multimode optical thermometry within biological window enabled by Er 3+ self-sensitized upconversion luminescence
abstract: 1532 nm-driven multimode optical thermometry within biological window is enabled by Er 3+ self-sensitized upconversion luminescence.

title: ZnO-free organic solar cells with a self-assembled monolayer as an ETL for improved stability
abstract: A novel graftable molecule (2PAP-SAM) was developed to replace zinc oxide as an electron transport layer in organic solar cells. 2PAP-SAM shows great ability to efficiently select electrons and minimizes degradation at the ETL/active layer interface.

title: Exploring magnetodielectric and negative differential resistance effects in NiMn 2 O 4 nanocomposites
abstract: Nanoscale spin-charge interactions driving magnetocapacitive response in NiMn 2 O 4 nanocomposites.

title: Supercapacitive performance of a PEDOT⊃metal–porphyrin framework composite in a concentrated aqueous LiClO 4 electrolyte
abstract: This study presents a robust neodymium-porphyrin metal–organic framework with in situ polymerized poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene), achieving high capacitance, excellent cycling stability, and a wide electrochemical window.

title: Aluminum/waterborne polyurethane composite aerogels with combined low infrared emissivity and thermal conductivity
abstract: Due to their low infrared emissivity, anisotropic thermal conductivity and flexibility, Al/WPU composite aerogels have the potential to be used in infrared stealth.

title: Influence of particle orientation, concentration and matrix stiffness on the elastocaloric performance of spin crossover composite materials
abstract: The impact of matrix stiffness, particle orientation and concentration on the elastocaloric performance of spin-crossover@polymer composites was investigated, highlighting key issues for their implementation in solid-state refrigeration technologies.

title: Role of Ce 3+ doping in the photochromic and storage properties of Ca 3 MgSi 2 O 8
abstract: Ca 3 MgSi 2 O 8 :Ce 3+ is a novel photochromic material and a promising candidate for dosimetry with exceptional color contrast, an efficient optically stimulated luminescence, and a linear thermostimulated luminescence dose response up to 40 Gy.

title: F − substitution-induced high-efficiency broadband near-infrared MgAl 2 O 4 :Fe 3+ phosphors for night-vision and biomedical imaging
abstract: Anion F − substitution in MgAl 2 O 4 :Fe 3+ boosting IQE from 66.3% to 96.5% and tuning broadband NIR emission (740–770 nm). A 365 nm-excited pc-LED delivers 5.74 mW for night vision and bio-imaging.
