title: Dual benefits from “wiring” charge-transfer moieties to perovskite surfaces
abstract: Attaching electron donors to the surface of CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals via aliphatic ammonium not only facilitates interfacial charge transfer but also eliminates some of the traps responsible for shortening the exciton lifetimes.

title: Recent advances and future perspectives on metal doping strategies for regulating the electrocatalytic performance of MoS 2
abstract: This review summarizes metal-doped MoS 2 electrocatalysts, highlighting doping strategies, structure modulation, kinetic optimization, and electrocatalytic applications.

title: Photo-modulation of the work function and electronic properties of MXenes via a self-assembled cationic azobenzene molecular layer
abstract: A new design strategy for functionalization of an ultrathin 2D Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene film with photochromic cationic azobenzenes is presented.

title: Photononic crystal-plasmonic synergy-driven ultrabright upconversion for high-efficiency near-infrared photocatalysis
abstract: By constructing a SiO 2 sphere array@Au film@UCNPs composite structure, the upconversion fluorescence was amplified by 10.4-fold through the plasmonic effect, enabling efficient dye degradation under 980 nm near-infrared light excitation.

title: Singlet and triplet excitations in chemically functionalized single-walled carbon nanotubes
abstract: Ab initio study of the lowest singlet–triplet energy splitting (Δ E ST ) in (11,0) single walled carbon nanotubes with aryl adduct defects, indicating an efficient TADF emitters.

title: Controllable modulation of through-bond and through-space coupling channels in indene-derived photoresponsive molecules for single-molecule photoswitches
abstract: Molecular engineering enables light-controlled switching between through-bond and through-space charge transport in single-molecule photoswitches.

title: Integrated bidirectional and polarization-sensitive photodetection in a ReSe 2 /graphene/WSe 2 heterostructure
abstract: This work developed a photodetector based on a ReSe 2 /graphene/WSe 2 heterostructure that integrates both bidirectional and polarization-sensitive characteristics, achiving a high polarization ratio (PR) value of 9 at 635 nm and 6 at 808 nm.

title: Delocalization and bandgap engineering in defective MoS 2 by metal ion doping for enhanced electrical performance and efficient near-infrared detection
abstract: Delocalized and narrowed bandgap electronic structures of defective MoS 2 for enhanced electrical performance and efficient near-infrared detection by doping.

title: Highly sensitive dual-sensing of blood glucose and UV light enabled by complexing biazopyridiniums with quantum dots
abstract: A dual sensor for glucose and UV light is enabled by a bisazopyridine-QD complex. Its PET-governed fluorescence turn-on response allows for highly sensitive detection in biosensing and environmental UV monitoring.

title: Ferroelectric memcapacitive dynamics from nanoseconds to milliseconds for bio-inspired neuromorphic computing and control
abstract: Achieving sub-volt, nanosecond-to-millisecond synaptic plasticity in a scalable ferroelectric platform remains a key challenge for neuromorphic hardware.

title: Nano carbon sphere modified CoAl 2 O 4 –CdS Z-scheme heterojunctions: broad-spectrum absorption enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The 20% CAO–CdS–CS showed that the average hydrogen evolution rate over 5 hours for 20% CAO–CdS–CS was 43.1 mmol g −1 h −1 . Based on XPS and DFT characterization analyses, it is inferred that a Z-scheme heterojunction forms between CoAl 2 O 4 and CdS.

title: All sites’ systematic non-stoichiometric regulation of ordering-related structure and microstructure for enabling ultralow dielectric loss and temperature stability in Ba(Mg 1/3 Nb 2/3 )O 3 perovskite ceramics
abstract: Non-stoichiometric design at distinct sites in Ba(Mg 1/3 Nb 1/3 )O 3 to regulate cation ordering, ordered domains, and grain size, revealing a critical grain-size effect that governs the coupled impact of ordering degree and grain boundaries to the Q × f .

title: A Cu-based metal–organic framework with high specificity and efficient peroxidase-like catalytic activity for colorimetric biosensing
abstract: A novel Cu-MOF (ZJU-132), was synthesized and shows good specificity as well as high catalytic efficiency as a novel OPD peroxidase nanozyme with linear ranges and a low detection limit.

title: Radiation-hard organic electronics via perylenediimide core engineering
abstract: This first systematic study of gamma ray exposure on perylenediimide (PDI) semiconductors reveals their outstanding radiation hardness, unlocking potential for extreme-environment electronics.

title: Pressure-engineered trap-controlled multimodal luminescence for versatile emission responses in Pr-doped NaNbO 3
abstract: NaNbO 3 :Pr 3+ exhibits multimodal luminescence (photoluminescence, persistent luminescence, and mechanoluminescence) via pressure-tuned shallow and deep traps under varying applied pressures.

title: Trap states and charge transport mechanisms in small molecule TADF materials with different donor–acceptor structures
abstract: Charge transport and recombination have been regulated in TADF-OLEDs via synergistic molecular engineering and trap-dilution. The optimized device based on doping concentration of 20 wt% BPAPTC shows an EQE max of 13.22%.

title: Solution processable luminescent fluorinated perylene derivatives showing high n-type mobility and columnar self-assembly
abstract: Number, nature and pattern of substitution of flexible peripheral chains play a major role in the self-assembly and charge carrier mobility of perylene derivatives.

title: Cholesteric liquid crystal microcapsules exhibiting a triple response and dual-mode dynamic regulation for information encryption
abstract: A core–shell CLCM (SP/BODIPY@CLC, PMMA) offers triple response (temperature, UV, blue light): reversible thermochromism, fast photoswitchable fluorescence, stability. Enables dynamic encryption by mapping optical outputs to ASCII codes for security.

title: A controlled growth strategy for CAU-17 MOF thin films via two-step synthesis
abstract: Sputtering-assisted two-step synthesis converts amorphous Bi 2 O 3 into crystalline CAU-17 MOF thin films through an oriented attachment mechanism, enabling controlled growth of nanorods directly on various substrates.

title: Low off-state current density in vertical organic field-effect transistors via AgNWs/TiO 2 interface modification for neuromorphic devices
abstract: Low off-state current density, high on/off ratio and output saturation performance were achieved in VOFET, and low-power optoelectronic neuromorphic devices with distinct conductance states and high recognition accuracy were further demonstrated.

title: The Emergence of Quantum Dot Complex
abstract: While dimension-dependent properties of quantum dots (Qdots) - in addition to the dependence on the constituents and structure of the core - have received great attention, the importance of their...

title: Ge-doping-induced phase engineering of β/ε-Ga 2 O 3 for high-performance ultraviolet detectors
abstract: Solar-blind deep-ultraviolet (DUV) photodetectors based on intrinsic Ga 2 O 3 typically suffer from a trade-off between low responsivity and high dark current due to poor carrier transport and high defect densities.

title: Flexible transparent conductive films based on slot-die- coating-induced oriented silver nanowire networks
abstract: Bilayer orthogonal silver nanowire transparent conductive films fabricated by slot-die coating and mechanical pressing achieve a sheet resistance of 19 Ω sq −1 , a transmittance of 95.4%, and a haze value of 2.05%, outperforming random networks.

title: Air-sinterable copper pastes for next-generation electronics: a review
abstract: Growing demand for high-power, smart electronics drives air-sinterable copper paste development, addressing their oxidation and strict sintering issues. This work reviews its advances, applications and industrialization challenges.

title: Enhancing photovoltaic performance by modulating fractal dimensions of non-fullerene acceptors
abstract: A series of non-fullerene acceptors with different side-chain lengths are designed. The side-chain length has remarkable impacts on fractal dimensions of the acceptors, leading to a big difference in efficiencies of organic solar cells.

title: Interfacial Regulation and Modeling of Electromagnetic Shielding in Liquid Gallium-Modified Copper Fabric/Polyurea Composites
abstract: Flexible electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding materials with controllable electromagnetic responses are highly desired for high-frequency and deformable electronics. Here, a flexible sandwich-structured composite membrane (PU-Ga@CuF) is fabricated via liquid-gallium-enabled interfacial...

title: Synergistic NH 4 F and ZnCl 2 -assisted surface engineering enables efficient red ZnSeTe quantum dots for QLEDs
abstract: Red ZnSeTe-based QLEDs, enabled by synergistic NH 4 F and ZnCl 2 -assisted QDs, achieve a maximum luminance of 117 cd m −2 and an external quantum efficiency of 0.3%.

title: Orthogonal steric modulation to suppress concentration quenching and spectral broadening in multi-resonance TADF emitters
abstract: Three MR-TADF emitters with bulky 4- tert -butylphenoxy substituents were developed. The ortho -disubstituted o-2O-DtCzBN exhibits a near-orthogonal geometry, delivering narrowband emission and excellent ACQ suppression from 2 to 20 wt% doping.

title: Unveiling the capacitance overestimation of a HfO 2 –ZrO 2 solid solution at the morphotropic phase boundary
abstract: A comprehensive electrical analysis reveals that the dielectric constant of HfO 2 -based thin film capacitors at the morphotropic phase boundary has been overestimated.

title: Dual-function conductive silver paste: application of an epoxy system based on curing resistance evolution in packaging and flexible electronics
abstract: Epoxy resin-based silver paste (SP) has garnered widespread attention in chip packaging owing to its superior mechanical properties and interfacial adhesion.

title: Theoretical investigation of altermagnetic materials with tunable valley polarization in monolayer Nb 2 Cl 2
abstract: Realize a strain-driven valley-polarization effect in the altermagnetic material Nb 2 Cl 2 .

title: A fully biocompatible and biodegradable triboelectric nanogenerator based on PVA/chitosan fibers and PLA fibers with enhanced output performance
abstract: A fully biodegradable triboelectric nanogenerator using electrospun PVA/chitosan and PLA fibers achieves enhanced triboelectric performance and biocompatibility, offering a sustainable power source for wearable electronics.

title: Microfluidic fabrication of dual-functional hydrogel optical fibers with controlled swelling for simultaneous light transmission and ionic conductivity
abstract: Conductive hydrogel optical fibers with integrated dual functionality are fabricated via microfluidic coaxial extrusion, transmitting optical and electrical signals during deformation for advanced wearable biomedical sensors.

title: Tunable color switch and sensor using a cross-shaped metasurface
abstract: An innovative tunable colour switch and sensor based on a cross-shaped metasurface (CSM) on a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) substrate is created.

title: Regioselective –NO 2 substitution enables tunable photophysics and molecular packing in a polycyclic 1,2-BN heteroarene framework
abstract: We report four biocompatible NO 2 -substituted pyrrolidinone-fused 1,2-BN heteroarenes that exhibit tunable photophysical properties and precise molecular-packing control enabled by regioselective nitration.

title: Controlling the triplet dynamics for efficient room temperature phosphorescence by molecular engineering
abstract: Fine-tuning triplet dynamics in functionalized naphthalene–boron conjugates for efficient organic room temperature phosphorescence.

title: Upconversion luminescence and temperature sensing characteristics of Na 0.5 Mg 0.5 Sc 1.5 (WO 4 ) 3 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ phosphors
abstract: The crystal structure, upconversion luminescence properties and temperature sensing characteristics of novel Na 0.5 Mg 0.5 Sc 1.5 (WO 4 ) 3 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ phosphors have been studied.

title: Performance enhancement of carbon dot-based luminescent solar concentrators via surface modification and TiO 2 -enhanced scattering
abstract: Modification of carbon dots (CDs) with tyramine (Tyr-CDs) enhanced photoluminescence quantum yield, and embedding Tyr-CDs and TiO 2 nanoparticles in ethylene–vinyl acetate copolymer films increased luminescent solar concentrator (LSC) efficiency.

title: The impact of quantum dot purification methods on sensing performance for heavy metal detection in environmental pollution monitoring
abstract: A nanopaper made of carbon quantum dots (CQDs) and TEMPO-oxidised cellulose nanofibers (TOCN) from bacterial nanocellulose was assembled into a biocomposite for heavy metal detection, exploring the impact of purification on the sensing process.

title: Multilayer interfacial engineering of Al–Ce metallic glass for brittleness mitigation and reliable motion sensing
abstract: Integrating an auxiliary crystalline interlayer into metallic glass (MG) thin films significantly enhances electrode ductility, enabling high-performance, flexible strain sensors for precise motion detection.

title: Unveiling asymmetric thermo–mechanical coupling for enhanced energy harvesting: a lead-free BCT–BST based pyro–piezoelectric hybrid nanogenerator
abstract: A high-performance BCT–BST pyro–piezoelectric HNG elucidates thermo–mechanical coupling. The cooling-enhanced and heating-suppressed asymmetric current is explained by band bending modulation, enabling efficient energy harvesting.

title: Tunable 3D/2D MoS 2 /Ni 3 S 2 nanocomposites as an efficient electrocatalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A synergistic 3D/2D MoS 2 /Ni 3 S 2 heterostructure was fabricated with a hydrothermal process for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), exhibiting an overpotential of 135 mV at 10 mA cm −2 , and a Tafel slope of 51 mV dec −1 in acidic media.

title: Organic electrochemical transistor-based biosensors using doped polyaniline
abstract: High-performance OECT-based biosensors are developed using polyaniline films doped with 4-sulfophthalic acid and phenol 4-sulfonic acid, demonstrating a versatile strategy for tunable, robust bioelectronic sensors.

title: Significant roles of lateral alkoxy and alkylthio groups on the ferroelectric nematic phase and phase transition behavior
abstract: We report new room-temperature ferroelectric nematogens and the significant effects of the lateral ortho -alkoxy and alkylthio groups on the formation of the ferroelectric nematic phase and phase transition behavior.

title: Indoleacetic acid-mediated suppression of oxidation and crystallization in tin perovskite LEDs
abstract: By incorporating MIAA into the PEA 2 SnI 4 , the easy oxidation and rapid crystallization of tin-based perovskites were effectively suppressed. Consequently, a high-brightness pure-red PeLED with the maximum luminance of 1412 cd m −2 was achieved.

title: Theoretical investigation of a vanadium dioxide-based terahertz metasurface with maximum overlap bandwidth for polarization conversion and absorption
abstract: A VO 2 -based terahertz metasurface enables thermally switchable dual functionality, acting as a wideband cross-polarization converter in the insulating state and a high-efficiency absorber in the metallic state.

title: A field-deployable multimodal device integrated with paper strips and LIG platforms for rapid optical and electrochemical detection of organophosphate nerve-agent simulants
abstract: Selective detection and detoxification of DCP via hydrogen-bond-driven aggregation using a probe-functionalized laser-induced graphene platform for portable sensing.

title: An ultrahigh responsivity UV photodetector enabled by a spatially confined 2D β-Ga 2 O 3 /PdSe 2 van der Waals heterojunction
abstract: A 2D/2D β-Ga 2 O 3 /PdSe 2 heterojunction enables solar-blind UV detection with an ultralow dark current (∼1 pA) and a record-high responsivity of 6.17 × 10 4 A W −1 .

title: Flexible MXene/holey graphene films as multifunctional electrodes for high-performance energy storage and pressure sensing
abstract: A strategy using holey graphene as spacer in MXene creates ion channels, and expands spacing. This enables multifunctional electrodes for energy storage and pressure sensing.

title: A thermally robust Dy 3+ -activated Sc 2 Mo 3 O 12 phosphor for sustainable WLED lighting and phase transition-enabled visual cryogenic thermometry
abstract: This study investigates the negative-thermal expansion of the Sc 2 Mo 3 O 12 :Dy 3+ phosphor for thermally stable luminescence in sustainable WLED lighting and explores cryogenic phase transition and charge transfer band shift-based optical thermometry.

title: Highly reflective cholesteric liquid crystal films based on nematic isolation layers: broadband infrared regulation and polarization anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: A three-layer broadband highly reflective PSCLC film is fabricated using PSNLC as an isolation layer. The film achieves >80% IR reflectivity, thermal insulation, and visible-region polarization-responsive anti-counterfeiting via chiral dopant tuning.

title: High-performance stretchable thermoelectric multilayers enabled by a synergistic elastomer-conjugated network
abstract: This study presents a stretchable thermoelectric quadlayer combining PPyNPs/DWNT-PEDOT:PSS conductive layers with PEO/PAA elastomers. It achieves 183.3 µW m −1 K −2 , sustains 30% strain, and remains stable after 1000 bending cycles.

title: A self-powered WO 3 -based photoelectrochemical synapse for object distance judgment
abstract: WO 3 films were prepared via radio frequency magnetron sputtering to construct self-powered photoelectrochemical synapses, it successfully simulated object distance judgment, providing a low-cost, low-energy solution for intelligent recognition.

title: Strain regulation strategies of halide perovskite solar cells and optimization of flexible devices
abstract: This paper summarizes strategies for low-modulus substrate selection, stress-relieving structure design, and flexible interfacial layer development, providing references for strain regulation in perovskite films.

title: PMA-interlayered hole transport layers enabling sub-bandgap emission and improved stability in perovskite LEDs
abstract: Phosphomolybdic acid (PMA) acts as a p-type dopant-like interlayer in the HTL, enabling efficient charge injection and sub-bandgap electroluminescence in PeLEDs.

title: Floating-gate synaptic transistors with bending resistance and strong charge storage capability via interfacial redispersion and reconfiguration
abstract: The development of artificial neural networks based on neuromorphic devices is expected to break through the von Neumann bottleneck and promote the further development of artificial intelligence technology.

title: Understanding the charge state of the Eu ion in anti-perovskites: the role of intrinsic defects
abstract: Intrinsic defects determine the charge state of europium ions in solids.

title: Charge transfer-induced enhancement of superconductivity and suppression of CDW in Cu-intercalated TaSe 2 single crystals
abstract: In layered transition metal dichalcogenides such as TaSe 2 , Cu intercalation enhances superconductivity and suppresses charge density wave order by electron doping, demonstrating an effective strategy for tuning competing quantum phases.

title: Lattice-strain effects on the electronic structure and magnetism of epitaxial SmCrO 3 thin films
abstract: Epitaxial SmCrO 3 thin films exhibit a spin-reorientation transition driven by the 3d–4f magnetic exchange and reveal how the lattice-strain influences magnetism and optical band gaps – paving the way for advanced electronic and magnetic devices.

title: A rigid xanthene–anthracene-based scaffold exhibiting ultra deep blue emission: multifunctional material as emitter and host for blue OLEDs
abstract: Ultra-deep blue fluorescent emitters with Commission Internationale de L’Eclairage (CIE y ) < 0.08 are still in demand for achieving vibrant full-color displays.

title: Promising magnetocaloric performance of nanostructured GdFeSi intermetallic ribbons for efficient natural gas liquefaction purposes
abstract: Low temperature magnetocaloric performance of GdFeSi is enhanced for melt-spun ribbons, showing an almost 40% increase in the refrigerant capacity compared with the bulk alloy (RC > 500 J kg −1 @ μ 0 Δ H = 5 T).

title: Ultrathin sub-5 nm gate-all-around SiGe nanowire transistors with near-ideal subthreshold swing
abstract: This paper demonstrates the International Technology Roadmap for Semiconductors (ITRS) high-performance (HP) achieved at 3–5 nm gate lengths.

title: In situ preparation of high-performance flexible copper halide scintillation films for X-ray imaging
abstract: In situ grown flexible (C 19 H 18 P) 2 CuI 3 @PMMA scintillator films achieve high PLQY (82%), excellent light yield (26 800 photons per MeV), and superior X-ray imaging resolution (11 lp mm −1 ).

title: Broadband photoluminescence of epitaxial bismuth nanowires and planar nanostructures
abstract: Epitaxial bismuth planar nanostructures and nanowires grown on CaF 2 /Si(111) exhibit broadband photoluminescence.

title: High-performance UV-crosslinked PMMA-based solid polymer electrolytes for flexible electrochromic devices
abstract: A UV-crosslinked PMMA solid-state electrolyte enables rigid ECDs with ECP-Magenta, Yellow, and Cyan for multicolor and FECDs (≥ 2000 bends at 15 mm). It is compatible with inkjet printing for patterned ECDs.

title: Spontaneous structural deformation of MoS 2 nanoribbons in Au(111)/MoS 2 /Au(111) heterojunctions
abstract: Spontaneous structural deformation of a MoS 2 ribbon on two Au electrodes.

title: Ab initio insights into radiation-induced defects and band-gap evolution in α-, β-, and κ-Ga 2 O 3
abstract: Ab initio molecular dynamics reveals anisotropic threshold displacement energies and radiation-induced defect formation in α-, β-, and κ-Ga 2 O 3 , showing oxygen-dominated damage and defect-driven band-gap modulation under irradiation.

title: Adaptive radiative cooling and thermal insulation enabled by thermoresponsive cholesteric liquid crystal aerogels
abstract: The as-prepared cholesteric liquid crystal-filled aerogel exhibits temperature-dependent modulation of reflectivity, together with self-adaptive cooling performance during daytime and heat-retention capability at nighttime.

title: Molten salt-mediated cyano group augmentation and morphology modulation in carbon nitride for boosting photocatalytic activity
abstract: Clarification of the mechanism underlying the dramatic enhancement of photocatalytic performance resulting from the novel one-step synthesis of crystalline carbon nitrides with cyano-group augmentation and morphology modulation.

title: Trap-mediated photogating in hybrid organic–inorganic heterojunction phototransistors for photo-memory and photodetection
abstract: Trap-mediated photocarrier dynamics govern transient responses in hybrid phototransistors. TES-ADT/IGZO heterojunction shows fast photodetection, while TIPS-Tc/IGZO exhibits gate-controlled trap redistribution for programmable memory and forgetting.

title: Synergistic Na-site Ca and TM-site Cu doping in an O3-type NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathode towards fast kinetics and long-life sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Ca/Cu dual-site doping widens Na + pathways and lowers charge-transfer resistance in O3-type NFM111, resulting in enhanced long cycle performance and air stability.

title: Morphological regulation on barium titanate perovskite particles for broadening microwave absorption in GHz and THz range
abstract: A two-step hydrothermal synthesis was used to prepare barium titanate crystals with spherical, rod-like, platelet-like, and blocky morphologies for microwave absorption.

title: Efficient and robust manganese-based hybrid ceramics enabled by dual-ligand regulation for high-performance 3D X-ray imaging
abstract: A bidentate manganese-based hybrid ceramic (MHC) has been developed, and large-area (8 × 8 cm) MHC samples have been fabricated using a melt-quenching hot-pressing process, achieving an imaging resolution of up to 15 lp mm −1 .

title: Ni@Sn core–shell structured liquid metal magnetic suspensions for high-sensitivity stretchable electronics and tunable magnetic response
abstract: Ni@Sn core-shell nanoparticles are prepared by in situ plating and mixed into liquid metal for magnetic suspensions. The composite shows high sensitivity and stretchability, promising for supercooled sensing, patterning, and magnetic manipulation.

title: A long-wavelength red-light-responsive hydrogel with tunable optical transparency for high-resolution information displays
abstract: A hydrogel embedded with azo polymer and photosensitizer regulates transparency by altering aggregation state of azo moiety via TTET upon 625 nm light exposure. Red-light excitation avoids self-absorption, broadening transmittance regulation range.

title: PVA-based composite hydrogels for biomedical applications
abstract: Summary of the properties and biomedical applications of PVA-based composite hydrogels.

title: Bifunctional [Fe 6 Co 7 ] cyanide bridged assemblies: electron transfer coupled spin transition and liquid crystal properties
abstract: We present the first alkyl-chain functionalized [FeCo] PBAs exhibiting synchronized LC and ETCST at melting temperatures, with reversible thermochromism in solution phase. Alkylation facilitates thin-film processing while retaining magnetic behavior.

title: Photo-induced micro-phase separation structural changes for property regulation of circularly polarized luminescent liquid crystal physical gels
abstract: A photo-responsive LCPG material with enhanced CPL was developed; it exhibits reversible cis – trans isomerization of azobenzene under UV light, leading to improved CPL efficiency and high fatigue resistance.

title: Achieving efficient blue room-temperature phosphorescence in cyclized aromatic amides via a hybridized local and charge-transfer state
abstract: Cyclized aromatic amides enable 2016.9 ms blue phosphorescence with 15.6% yield via a HLCT state, exhibiting a 68-fold lifetime enhancement over its non-cyclized couterpart.

title: Local structural disorder in FeCl 3 –SbCl 5 -intercalated graphite: revealing the disconnect between long-range periodicity and nanoscale intercalant arrangement
abstract: Graphite intercalation compounds (GICs) containing metal chlorides exhibit well-defined layer-by-layer periodicity that underlies their electronic and functional properties.

title: Structure and electrical properties of Ga 2 O 3 thin films grown by atomic layer deposition on Ru and TiN electrodes
abstract: The composition, structure, and electrical properties of Ga 2 O 3 thin films, grown by atomic layer deposition (ALD) from GaI 3 and O 3 precursors, were characterized.

title: Non-parametric statistical characterization of electrical characteristics in nanoscale conductive filament devices
abstract: Nonparametric statistical analysis of 22 parameters for 174 Ag, Cu, Ni, Co, and VCM nanofilamentary devices reveals the current performance and future development trends of RRAM devices.

title: Multilayer heterostructure engineering for high-purity and uniform area emission in perovskite quantum-dot light-emitting transistors
abstract: High-performance ZTO/perovskite QD heterojunction based LETs are realized via interface engineering using PVK and Al 2 O 3 , enabling uniform area emission with Rec.2020-level color purity while maintaining a high-performance transistor characteristic.

title: Spatially confined surface-terminated MXene nanosheets as a multifunctional platform for triboelectric sensing and logic gates
abstract: A spatially confined polar-group-terminated MXene nanosheet/PDMS and EC nanofiber-based vertical TENG, with charge transfer validated by DFT calculations and experiments, enables multifaceted energy harvesting and self-powered sensing applications.

title: Ce-doping-induced Burstein–Moss shift in biogenically synthesized ZnO for multifunctional gas sensing, photocatalytic, and antimicrobial applications
abstract: Multifunctional materials capable of addressing air-quality monitoring, clean energy generation, and antimicrobial control are of significant interest for environmental and public health applications.

title: Ferroelectric devices as physical reservoirs: enabling nonlinear dynamics and memory in neuromorphic systems
abstract: Multiple states and dynamics of polarization in ferroelectric materials provide nonlinearity and short-term memory, enabling state richness required for physical reservoir computing.

title: Deep eutectic solvent-driven synthesis of La 2 NiO 4 @rGO Ruddlesden–Popper hybrids for ethyl parathion sensing supported with theoretical studies
abstract: Ruddlesden–Popper phase perovskite La 2 NiO 4 @rGO nanocomposite for sensor applications in the detection of the organophosphorus pesticide ethyl parathion.

title: Dual-strategy optimization of Ni 2+ -activated NIR emission in CaLu 2 Mg 2 Ge 3 O 12 via Zn 2+ -driven internal quantum efficiency boost and Cr 3+ -enhanced absorption
abstract: We enhanced the luminescence and thermal stability of Ni 2+ -activated CaLu 2 Mg 2 Ge 3 O 12 phosphors via Zn 2+ substitution to boost internal quantum efficiency and Cr 3+ doping to improve absorption, achieving a high-performance near-infrared light source.

title: Efficient broadband light sensing with anisotropic Sb 2 S 3 based thin film heterojunction photodetectors under self-driven conditions
abstract: Self-driven n-Si/Sb 2 S 3 heterojunction photodetectors with broadband and weak light detection characteristics.

title: Design and theoretical investigation of a switchable and tunable terahertz absorber based on graphene and vanadium dioxide
abstract: A graphene–VO 2 -based asymmetric metamaterial absorber achieves switchable and tunable terahertz absorption, governed by impedance matching and transmission line theory, with stable performance under varying polarization and incident angles.

title: Free-standing Blatter radical polymer thin films: precise nanometer thickness tuning and redox-optical properties
abstract: Electropolymerization-assisted generation of carbazolyl Blatter radical polymer thin films with nanometer-precision thickness control, as well as free-standing thin films, was demonstrated.

title: Tailoring heterojunction energetics for a high-performance self-powered halide perovskite photodetector without charge transport layers
abstract: A conventional charge transport layer-free strategy using all-inorganic perovskite heterojunctions enables simplified architecture, improved stability, and efficient self-powered photodetection.

title: Schottky-like contact-induced d-band center shift in Cu(H 2 PO 3 ) 2 /boron for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Boron-induced Schottky-like contact shifts the d-band center of Cu(H 2 PO 3 ) 2 for noble-metal-free, high-performance photocatalytic hydrogen evolution. Background generated with AI.

title: Mechanistic understanding of room temperature phosphorescence in pure organic host–guest systems: efficient emission of molecules lacking n→π* states
abstract: Host–guest doping leads to mixed host (n, π) and guest (π, π*) excitons, enabling phosphorescence of otherwise non-phosphorescent guest molecules.

title: The effect of defects on the formation of inversion domain boundaries in AlScN and their structural/electronic property improvement
abstract: The performance of wurtzite AlScN is hindered by defects such as inversion domain boundaries (IDBs) and interfacial contaminations. Both piezoelectricity and structural stability can be enhanced by applying tensile strain and reducing oxygen concentration.

title: Pentafluorophenyl end-capped bithiophene imide (BTI) based n-type semiconductors for organic thin-film transistors
abstract: Two fluorinated bithiophene imide-based semiconductors, DFP-BTI-b8 (1) and DFP-BTI-b16 (2), were synthesized and exhibit excellent n-type OTFT performance with mobilities up to 0.10 cm 2 V −1 s −1 and current on/off ratios over 10 6 .

title: Achieving superior high-temperature energy storage performance in polycarbonate via small molecule traps and hydrogen bonding
abstract: In this study, PF was introduced into PC to construct molecular traps through hydrogen bonding, thereby successfully preparing a PF/PC composite dielectric with excellent high-temperature energy storage performance.

title: Bifunctional polymer assisted growth of crack-free thick perovskite films for flexible X-ray detection
abstract: Pluronic P123 regulates crystallization of lead-free Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 , yielding crack-free, flexible thick films. PEG–Ag + coordination and PPG steric effects enable uniform growth. Devices show high sensitivity, low detection limit, strong bending durability, and long-term stability.

title: Low-voltage, long-term stable organic heterojunction transistors with a broadband bidirectional photoresponse for in-sensor vision computing
abstract: An organic heterojunction transistor with a HfO 2 /SiO 2 dielectric enables <5 V operation with a broadband bidirectional photoresponse (530–1050 nm). It retains >80% responsivity after 70 days and supports linear, gate-tunable in-sensor computing.

title: Facile assembly of high-solid, responsive liquid photonic crystals via RAFT-synthesized colloidal nanospheres using rotary evaporation
abstract: RAFT-synthesized PAA macro-CTAs enable monodisperse PAA- b -PS nanospheres with high surface charge. Concentration via rotary evaporation produces high-solid (55 wt%) liquid photonic crystals with bright structural colors and rapid dynamic recovery.

title: Chemically and thermally ultra-stable Ba( ii ) coordination polymer with dual fluorescence sensing and high proton conductivity
abstract: An ultra-stable Ba( ii ) coordination polymer with coupled π–π and H-bond networks integrates selective Fe 3+ /nitrobenzene fluorescence sensing and water-assisted proton conduction, showcasing robust interaction-coupled crystal engineering.

title: A soft-glass matrix composite with preservation of blue persistent luminescence
abstract: Direct incorporation of persistent phosphors into a soft-glass matrix preserves their integrity, enabling efficient daylight excitation and sustained afterglow emission.

title: Design rules for nonlinear optical materials: dimensionality, interfaces, topological band geometry and data-driven discovery
abstract: Nonlinear optical (NLO) materials underpin frequency conversion, ultrafast modulation, and light–matter control, yet conventional bulk crystals face practical constraints in tunability and integration.

title: Deciphering multiple energy-transfer photoevents in a 2D-MOF composite: relevance to multicolor lighting
abstract: C153 loading on 2D-MOF Al-ITQ-HB controls its photodynamics. In binary C153/Nile Red composites, concentration-dependent dye interactions and energy transfer processes determine dual-emission performance.

title: Photo-responsive covalent polymeric materials via azobenzene–molecular container host–guest interactions
abstract: This review summarizes photo-responsive polymeric materials over the past three years based on host–guest interactions between azobenzene and molecular containers, categorized by polymerized component: azobenzene, molecular container, or both.

title: Constructing an enzyme-free highly sensitive blood sugar detection platform based on the Maillard reaction using amino-functionalized black phosphorus quantum dot hydrogels
abstract: An enzyme-free, highly sensitive blood glucose platform based on Maillard reaction using amino-functionalized black phosphorus quantum dot hydrogels is built. Blood glucose values measured by the device match hospital enzymatic test results.

title: Scalable mechanochemical synthesis of bifunctional MnZn ferrite/expanded graphite composites for efficient microwave absorption and corrosion resistance
abstract: Short-process and high-volume preparation of bifunctional manganese-zinc ferrite/expanded graphite composite absorbers for efficient microwave absorption and corrosion resistance via a high-temperature mechanochemical method.

title: Non-monotonic plasmonic alignment governed by liquid-crystalline DNA hydrogel networks
abstract: Liquid-crystalline ordering in biopolymer networks provides a powerful yet unexplored route for controlling anisotropy in soft materials.

title: Enhancing high-temperature energy density of dielectric composites by tailoring molecular semiconductors
abstract: By regulating the structure of electron-donating groups in DPP-X (X = Cl, Ph, O), the carrier trapping capability and interfacial interactions of PEI-DPP-X composites are optimized, thereby improving energy storage performance.

title: Atomic force microscopy for ferroelectric materials research
abstract: This review explores the role of AFM in bridging nanoscale domain physics and intrinsic properties. We highlight how multimodal characterization and active manipulation serve as essential strategies for studying ferroelectric materials.

title: Improved on/off ratio in interface-type memristors with an ion irradiation-induced large Schottky barrier for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Characterization and switch ratio comparison diagram of D 0 and D 4 devices.

title: Integration of a symmetric D–A–D chromophore with biocompatible silk fibroin for ultrafast measurement and flexible solid-state lasing
abstract: A donor–acceptor–donor (D–A–D) chromophore exhibits strong two-photon absorption and efficient lasing, enabling ultrafast nonlinear optical measurements and flexible distributed-feedback (DFB) lasers when combined with silk fibroin.

title: An electropolymerized poly(1,10-phenanthroline)–iodine complex as a high-capacity and stable cathode for zinc–iodine batteries
abstract: Rechargeable zinc–iodine batteries (ZIBs) have emerged as promising energy storage systems due to their high energy density, low cost, and inherent safety.

title: Multiplet structure of chromium( iii ) dopants in wide band gap materials
abstract: The performance of electronic structure methods in describing the multiplet structure of strongly correlated Cr( iii ) dopants in α-Al 2 O 3 (a), AlB 4 O 6 N (b), and BeAl 2 O 4 at (c) C s - and (d) C i -symmetrized sites is assessed.

title: Multi-scale enhanced flexible wearable strain sensors: integration of core–shell PVDF/MWCNTs@PVDF/PTFE nanofibers for superior sensitivity, stability, and thermal performance
abstract: Schematic representation of the piezoresistive wearable strain sensor with a core–shell nanofiber structure.

title: Inkjet printing of silver nanowire electrodes for fully stretchable organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Fully stretchable AgNW OLEDs were fabricated using an inkjet printing method.

title: A binary solvent study with n -butanol and dimethyl sulfoxide on PEAI passivation of wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: A binary-solvent strategy combining n -butanol with trace DMSO enables more effective PEAI passivation. This solvent-engineered approach markedly suppresses surface defects and elevates the efficiency of wide-bandgap PSCs to 21.52%.

title: Lasing from ordered colloidal micro-resonator arrays to random distributed systems
abstract: While colloidal microstructures are widely utilized to engineer ordered micro-resonator arrays with precise optical responses, intentionally harnessing their inherent structural randomness opens new ways for tailoring light–matter interactions.

title: Realization of high-temperature metallic altermagnetism in transition metal graphite intercalation compounds
abstract: We report the emergence of altermagnetism, a magnetic phase characterized by the coexistence of compensated spin ordering and momentum-dependent spin splitting, in graphite intercalation compounds.

title: A microneedle-assisted CuNWs–MXene heterostructure enables visualized sub-femtomolar detection of cervical cancer miRNA
abstract: A microneedle-array visual-interface CuNWs–MXene FET enables sub-femtomolar tissue miRNA-21 detection, achieving 0.991 AUC clinically distinguishing CIN lesions from cervicitis.

title: The impact of core-substitution on the sequential reduction-induced open-shell structures in napthalenediimides
abstract: Core-substitution dependent electron doping characteristics of napthalenediimides.

title: Unveiling mechanical, optical and conductivity properties of naphthalenediimide bridged polysilsesquioxane gels
abstract: Organo-bridged polysilsesquioxanes (PSQ) incorporating photo- and electro-active motifs are promising candidates for optoelectronic and ion-conductive materials.

title: The odd–even alkyl chain effect on the structure and optoelectronic properties of alkyl-substituted perylene diimide (PDI) derivatives in highly strained environments
abstract: Remarkable polymorphic diversity of PDI-C n H 2 n +1 under high pressure and high temperature involve the same space-group type transitions and the odd–even effect depending on the length of alkyl chain.

title: Synthesis and characterization of Cs 2 TeCl 6 powder for scintillators
abstract: Luminescence and scintillation of Cs 2 TeCl 6 powder were studied in the 77–500 K temperature range and interpreted in terms of self-trapped exciton and two-excited state level models.

title: Circularly polarised luminescent inks with low crosstalk and high efficiency for 3D information encoding from liquid crystal microcapsules
abstract: R6G-doped cholesteric liquid crystal microcapsules show low crosstalk, strong CPL ( g lum = −1), and optical encryption. Precise control of dye concentration, shell, and film thickness enables flexible photonic coatings and anti-counterfeiting.

title: Design and application of organic electron transport layers for efficient and stable perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Organic electron transport layers (ETLs) promote the development of perovskite solar cells. This review summarizes the research progress of five organic ETLs, analyzes structure-performance relationships, and existing challenges.

title: Research progress on polycrystalline SnSe-based thermoelectric materials
abstract: Polycrystalline SnSe thermoelectrics gain high performance via structural and microstructural optimization. The improved power factor and ZT endow them bright prospects for waste heat recovery in aviation and civilian fields.

title: Berry curvature dipole-induced non-linear Hall effects in oxide superlattices
abstract: This study establishes non-centrosymmetric oxide perovskite superlattices as a versatile platform for exploring and manipulating BCD-driven non-linear transport phenomenon.

title: Deconvoluting the electronic landscape of ZnO using 2D excitation–emission spectroscopy: effects of microstructuring, doping and restructuring
abstract: Understanding the electronic properties and energy relaxation pathways of semiconductors, is essential for developing next-generation materials for applications in areas as diverse as medicine, flat panel-displays and sensing.

title: Synergy of intramolecular hydrogen bonding and π-bridge locking enables high-efficiency red/NIR electroluminescence
abstract: A novel thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF) emitter with a linker-locked π-bridge exhibits enhanced oscillator strength and rigidity, and suppresses intermolecular quenching, enabling high efficiencies in both doped and nondoped devices.

title: Impact of Electron-Withdrawing Groups on the Performance of Triplet-Triplet Annihilation Upconversion Annihilators
abstract: Triplet-triplet annihilation (TTA) upconversion (UC) is a process converting two low-energy photons into one of higher energy and has a wide range of promising technological applications. Typically, TTA-UC systems are...

title: Transient degradable electronics enabled by systems of conducting polymers and natural biopolymers
abstract: Transient degradable electronics enabled by systems of conducting polymers and natural biopolymers.

title: Unravelling the Interfacial Charge Dynamics of Atomically Dispersed Nickel on Hematite {001} Photoelectrodes
abstract: The efficiency of hematite photoanodes is fundamentally constrained by sluggish kinetics and severe surface recombination, particularly on the {001} basal plane. Here, we report a defect-remediation strategy to anchor atomically...

title: First-Principles-Informed Unified Transport Model for Resolving Parameter Non-Identifiability in Sub-10 nm Ruthenium Interconnects
abstract: As backend interconnect dimensions approach the sub-10 nm regime, ruthenium (Ru) is increasingly attractive as a barrier-lean conductor, yet linking process conditions to transport performance remains difficult because transport parameters...

title: Potentiation and depression phosphorescent plasticity of multi-confined carbon dots for all-optical neuromorphic imaging
abstract: A bidirectional all-optical synapse based on multi-confined carbon dots is realized, enabling direct visualization of synaptic plasticity for neuromorphic imaging.

title: Tunable electrochemical doping and charge transport in non-OMIEC:OMIEC blends by microstructure design
abstract: Microstructure control in non-OMIEC:OMIEC blends enables tunable electrochemical doping and charge transport, transforming a conventional fullerene into a functional n-type mixed conductor.

title: Exploring π-conjugated triphenylamine–cyano derivatives as cost-effective hole-transporting materials in perovskite solar cell
abstract: The rapid progress in the power conversion efficiency of perovskite solar cells, along with the limitations of the benchmark hole-transporting material spiro-OMeTAD has driven the search for alternative hole-transporting materials.

title: Rapid and mild acid-induced recovery of high-yield carbon quantum dots from commercial black liquor
abstract: A rapid and mild acid-induced strategy enables high-yield recovery of carbon quantum dots from industrial black liquor. The CQDs show favorable photoluminescence and promising performance in photocatalysis and electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution.

title: MXene-enhanced quantum dots ultraviolet photodetector performance towards flexible photoelectric detection
abstract: A ZnMgO/Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnMgO 0D/2D/0D sandwich-like heterostructure composed of ZnMgO quantum dots and ultrathin Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene nanosheets is designed to enhance charge separation and migration of high-performance flexible ultraviolet photodetectors.

title: Ultra-long WSe 2 nanotubes and related hybrid nanostructures
abstract: Ultra-long WSe 2 nanotubes, along with related core–shell and hierarchical structures with lengths exceeding hundreds of microns, were synthesized via high-temperature selenidation and tested for their optoelectronic and electronic properties.

title: Hydrogen functionalization and strain tuning of superconductivity and charge density waves in MXene-derived chalcogenide monolayers
abstract: Hydrogenation and strain engineering synergistically induce high-temperature superconductivity and suppress charge density waves in M 2 S-type MXene monolayers, establishing them as a promising platform for tunable 2D quantum materials.

title: Structural ordering driven enhancement of electrical conductivity and mechanical performance in PEDOT:PSS via sustainable additives for flexible electronics
abstract: Enhanced electrical and mechanical performance of Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene): poly(styrene sulfonate) (PEDOT:PSS) through sustainable additive modification for flexible electronic device applications

title: Developing a solid state electrochromic-supercapacitor prototype through DFT-guided charge transfer engineering in 2D carbon doped NiO
abstract: Tailored carbon doping in NiO reveals distinct charge storage mechanisms, enabling multifunctional devices that seamlessly integrate energy storage with real-world electrochromism to fabricate color-indicative electrochromic supercapacitors.

title: Insights into ligand passivation strategies for multiple types of surface defects in high-performance perovskite quantum dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: The multi-step ligand exchange method applied enables the quantum dots to achieve complete passivation and the binding mechanisms of the ligand was provided. The photoelectronics performance of the synthesized devices have significantly improved.

title: A columnar molecular glass at high temperature
abstract: We prepared two triphenyltriazines decorated with three phenyltriazolyl and three tolanyl systems; only the phenyltriazolyl derivative formed a blue-emissive columnar glass mesophase, promising for anisotropic optoelectronic layers.

title: Indirect-to-direct bandgap transition in CsRESiS 4 (RE = Sc, Y, and Lanthanides) through intrinsic charge-transfer transition (CTT) behaviour
abstract: Replacement of La by Sm in CsRESiS 4 induces an indirect-to-direct bandgap and charge-transfer transition behaviour. CsSmSiS 4 exhibits the most potential for high-efficiency optoelectronics.

title: Fabrication of 3D PEDOT:PSS composite microstructures via two-photon polymerisation
abstract: Two-photon polymerisation of PEDOT:PSS composite microstructures characterised by conductive-AFM.

title: Tailoring near-field thermal radiation via coupled plasmon-phonon polaritons in n-InAs/hBN stacks
abstract: A schematic of two planar stacks held at T 1 and T 2 with gate bias V (and optional magnetic field B ) controlling the carrier density of n-InAs. A photonic transmission coefficient map of angular frequency ω and normalized in-plane wavevector β / k 0 .

title: Alloying Au into a Cu/Cu 2 O/nickel foam photoanode for solar-enhanced hydrogen production coupled with glucose oxidation
abstract: The Cu/Cu 2 O photoelectrocatalyst, possessing strong active centers and a narrow bandgap, has been considered a potential photoanode for photoelectrocatalytic hydrogen production coupled with glucose oxidation.

title: Exploring lateral and vertical phase separation in SEBS and dibenzochrysene derivative polymer blends
abstract: A vertical phase separation has been observed in spin-coated films prepared using blends of SEBS and a dibenzochrysene derivative π-conjugated polymer.

title: Probing structural and optical modulations in metal-ion co-doped gadolinium vanadate: a combined spectroscopic and diffraction study
abstract: Upconversion in GdVO 4 :Yb 3+ /Er 3+ was tuned by co-doping with Sc 3+ , Cd 2+ , and Zn 2+ . Enhanced PLQY was achieved through Zn 2+ -induced suppression of large vacancy clusters, while Cd 2+ introduced extended defects that quenched luminescence.

title: Two-dimensional hexagonal boron nitride-ferrofluid hybrids enable efficient magnetic cooling
abstract: Overheating remains a critical limitation in high-performance electronic and computing systems, necessitating the development of efficient thermal management fluids.

title: Emerging two-dimensional group IV chalcogenides as building blocks for advanced infrared photodetectors
abstract: The promising IR photoelectric properties of the 2D group IV chalcogenides (Te–Si–Pb–Te, Te–Si–Si–Se, and Te–Si–Ge–Se monolayers) highlight their potential as high-performance IRPDs.

title: Emulating frog's behavior: humidity-driven paired-pulse facilitation and metaplasticity realised in a 1D supramolecular nanofibre based neuromorphic device
abstract: Inspired by moisture-sensitive frogs, a supramolecular nanofibre device is developed as a humidity-responsive neuromorphic sensor. It shows humidity-dependent plasticity, light-enhanced response, and basic logic functions.

title: Dual Z-scheme g-C 3 N 4 /In 2 S 3 /In 2 O 3 for enhanced visible-light H 2 evolution and pollutant degradation
abstract: The design of a double Z-type heterojunction represents a promising strategy in photocatalysis to achieve superior charge carrier separation, increased light-harvesting capability, and consequently, a higher photocatalytic reaction rate.

title: Omnidirectional brightness and UV-shielding bifunctional pigments via CeO 2 photonic crystal microbeads
abstract: CeO 2 @SiO 2 photonic microbeads with bright angle-independent color and UV-shielding function.

title: Thermally activated delayed fluorescence polymers with well-defined structures to explore structure–performance correlation
abstract: Structurally well-defined TADF polymers are constructed via alternating copolymerization, enabling precise spatial separation of TADF units. This design delivers non-doped, solution-processed OLEDs with an EQE of 19.7%.

title: Nanocavity-confined Cu 2 O in a porphyrin-based MOF for steering CO 2 electroreduction toward hydrocarbons
abstract: The in situ encapsulation of Cu 2 O into PCN-223 leads to intermediate confinement and enhanced hydrocarbon selectivity.

title: Dual-mode ratiometric optical thermometry in NaCaY(MoO 4 ) 3 :Ho 3+ /Nd 3+ /Yb 3+ phosphor via simultaneous downshifting and upconversion
abstract: Dual-mode down-/up-conversion (DC/UC) phosphors are promising substances for non-contact optical thermometry.

title: Moiré superlattice in relatively rotated and stacked BiMnO 3 nanoflakes
abstract: Moiré superlattice formation by stacking relatively rotated BiMnO 3 nanoflakes by a fixed in-plane angular rotation of ∼7°.

title: Harsh-environment-stable organic heterojunction memristor enabling reliable neuromorphic computing and hardware-aware neural network implementation
abstract: An F 16 CuPc/MDMO-PPV organic heterojunction memristor exhibits robust memristive switching and synaptic plasticity under 70% RH and up to 300 °C, enabling reliable hardware-aware neuromorphic computing.

title: Metal-free Carbazole-Based D-π-A Systems: Substituent-Modulated Acceptor Effects in Deep-Blue HLCT Emitters
abstract: The development of efficient deep-blue emitters is crucial for next-generation optoelectronics materials. In this work, three donor-engineered hybridized local and charge-transfer (HLCT) emitters were rationally designed and synthesized by integrating...

title: The crystal structure, and magnetic and resonant properties of decorated spin kagome system (CsCl)Cu 5 As 2 O 10
abstract: As a structural and magnetic bridge between V- and P-based averievites, (CsCl)Cu 5 As 2 O 10 reveals a 310 K trigonal–monoclinic transition followed by canted antiferromagnetic order at 21 K.

title: Gate-reconfigurable metal-oxide/polymer heterojunction phototransistors for broadband photodetection and multifunctional opto-synaptic computing
abstract: A gate-reconfigurable ZTO/P3HT heterojunction phototransistor enables reversible switching between positive and negative photoconductance, integrating broadband photodetection and opto-synaptic computing in a single device.

title: Manipulating the phase transitions and the appearance of a “black phase” in cyclically compressed CH 3 NH 3 PbBr 3 perovskite
abstract: Using high-pressure treatment, one can modify inorganic–organic hybrid perovskites and manipulate their phase stability ranges. In the example of CH 3 NH 3 PbBr 3 , this treatment also resulted in the appearance of a “black phase”.

title: Stable PbI 2 -terminated (001) facets drive low-defect anisotropy for high-performance charge transport in MAPbI 3 single crystals
abstract: Our study demonstrates improved Hall mobility in the PbI 2 -terminated MAPbI 3 -(001) facet compared to its MAI-terminated (100) counterpart, establishing crystallographic facet engineering as a cornerstone for high-performance optoelectronics.

title: An acid–base responsive supramolecular drawstring with switchable fluorescence
abstract: An acid–base responsive supramolecular drawstring complex, when incorporated into polymer networks, could establish novel stimuli-responsive fluorescent materials.

title: Orbital coupling–anion synergy in AB 2 Ch 4 semiconductors: band engineering for high-performance p-type transparent conductive materials
abstract: Here, we propose an “orbital coupling–anion synergy” strategy to design ternary AB₂Ch₄ semiconductors, addressing the scarcity of high-performance p-type transparent conductive materials (p-TCMs).

title: Synergistic N-doped carbon encapsulation in porous CoSe 2 nanospheres for enhancing the anode performance of sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Transition metal selenides (TMSes) are considered one of the most promising anode materials for advancing sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) due to their high specific capacity and energy density.

title: Irreversible thermochromic Pb 2 Sn 2 O 6 · x H 2 O: competition between weak ligand H 2 O and lone-pair electrons
abstract: Irreversible thermochromism in Pb 2 SnO 6 · x H 2 O demonstrates the conduction band gap narrowing effect in hybridizing lone-pair electrons in 6s–6p orbitals with the competition between the ligand of crystalline water and oxygen vacancies.

title: Magneto-dielectric synergy: one-pot synthesis of Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticle-decorated graphene oxide for high performance electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: With the rapid development of electronic devices and radar detection technology, there is an urgent need to develop highly efficient electromagnetic wave (EMW) absorption materials with a wide absorption bandwidth.

title: Prediction of group-IV multiferroic films with giant mid-infrared responsivity
abstract: Group-IV multiferroic films couple ferroic switching with polarization-selective absorption, enabling non-volatile, high-responsivity MWIR photodetection across the 3–5 μm window.

title: Salt complexation drives liquid crystalline self-assembly in crown ether–amino acid hybrids
abstract: Metal-salt complexation of crown ether-amino acid hybrids switches mesomorphic properties on and tunes phase type: short chains and small anions favor SmA d , while the opposite drive Col h stacks with tunable ion channels for responsive ion-conductors.

title: Exploring structure–function synergy in multi-fused A–D–A systems for advanced organic electronics
abstract: This computational study designs five novel A–D–A type organic molecules by modifying the acceptor units of synthetically realistic reference structure for advanced optoelectronic applications.

title: Coexisting ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic interactions in Mott–Hubbard insulating-type FeCr 2 O 4 nanoparticles driven by localized d-electron states
abstract: The Mott–Hubbard insulating nature of FeCr 2 O 4 nanoparticles is demonstrated through resonant photoemission spectroscopy (RPES).

title: Tailoring photo- and radio-luminescence in metal–organic frameworks via coordination group modulation
abstract: A series of new isostructural TPPA-based MOFs with varied terminal coordination groups of metal zinc (ZJU-813, ZJU-814, ZJU-815 and ZJU-816) were developed, yielding enhanced photoluminescence and radiation-stimulated photon emission.

title: Excited-state dynamics of colloidal gold nanoshells functionalized with trimethoxysilylazachalcones
abstract: New trimethoxysilylazachalcones and their application in hybrid materials with plasmonic gold nanoshells, including experimental and theoretical studies of intermolecular charge transfer and femtosecond transient absorption.

title: Modulation of metal (oxy)sulfide photocatalysts towards artificial photosynthesis
abstract: This review summarizes the advances in visible-light-responsive metal (oxy)sulfides for artificial photosynthesis, highlighting key strategies that improve activity and durability in water splitting and CO 2 reduction towards scalable implementation.

title: Optimizing the Cu 2+ ion and carbon-related defect center ratio in g-C 3 N 4 –ZnO:Cu nanocomposites for supercapacitor applications
abstract: Nanocomposites based on g-C 3 N 4 as carbon structure and ZnO doped with different Cu ion concentrations (ZnO:Cu x , x = 0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, and 1%) were tested as electrode materials for supercapacitor application. The electrochemical performances were evaluated for all the tested designs.

title: Ultrastable partially etched Ti 3 AlC 2 with tunable nonlinear scattering and absorption properties
abstract: Partially etched Ti 3 Al 1− x C 2 exhibit good saturable absorption properties and enhanced oxidative stability. The material size affects the optical response, which provides insights into the preparation of nonlinear optical materials.

title: Gibbs–Curie–Wulff-directed 2D cocrystal evolution for tunable in-plane anisotropic and isotropic photon transmission
abstract: 2D crystals with different shapes were controllably evolved from the same cocrystal, validating the Gibbs–Curie–Wulff theory in molecular co-crystallization and exhibiting morphology-dependent anisotropic and isotropic photon transmission.

title: Pressure-induced structural transitions and metallization in two-dimensional semiconductor CuInP 2 S 6
abstract: High-pressure structural transitions and metallization of CuInP2S6.

title: Bithiazole-based A–π–A′–π–A-type completely non-fused small-molecule acceptors for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: Three A–π–A′–π–A CNF-SMAs with a 2,2′-bithiazole central unit (A′) were obtained to study how the A′ unit and its side chains and change of the terminal A units affect the molecular structure, optoelectronic properties and photovoltaic performance.

title: Inorganic hole transport layers for perovskite light-emitting diodes: from material design to interfacial kinetics regulation
abstract: Perovskite light-emitting diodes (PeLEDs) have emerged as a promising technology for next-generation displays.

title: Wurtzite boron arsenide polytypes for polarization-controlled optoelectronic devices: a first principles study
abstract: Polarization-resolved excitonic and vibrational fingerprints in wurtzite BAs (2H, 4H, 6H) enable phase identification. Indirect gaps (∼2.0–2.12 eV) and robust excitons highlight their potential for anisotropic optoelectronics.

title: Oxygen vacancy-induced correlation and enhanced magnetism in SrRuO 3 nanoparticles studied by X-ray spectroscopic techniques
abstract: Enhanced magnetism and correlation-driven electronic effects in SrRuO 3 nanoparticles demonstrated through X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS), X-ray magnetic circular dichroism (XMCD), and resonant photoemission spectroscopy (RPES).

abstract: Lightweight, thermally insulating, and electromagnetic wave-absorbing building materials were developed using a quaternary solid-waste-based cementitious system composed of steel slag, blast furnace slag, phosphogypsum, and copper slag. The effects of...
