abstract: The increasing integration and miniaturization of power electronic devices is making it difficult for traditional polymer dielectrics to meet the demands of hightemperature operation. This creates an urgent need to...

title: Pursuing red thermally activated delayed fluorescence upon increasing the degree of branching in phenothiazine-trithienyltriazine push–pull compounds
abstract: Our time resolved spectroscopic results demonstrate the positive impact of increasing the degree of branching on boosting the red delayed fluorescence of new push–pull phenothiazine-trithienyltriazine derivatives in solid-state thin films.

title: Unravelling the mechanism of phase fraction modulation via process parameter tuning and first-principles study for enhanced TCR in VO x -based uncooled microbolometers
abstract: Changing the Ar/O 2 flow ratio at the gas inlet leads to an increase in TCR at low-temperature deposition in a sputtering chamber.

title: Electroluminescence from carbon quantum dots with an ultra-simple structure of metal/insulator/semiconductor
abstract: An AC-driven carbon quantum dot light-emitting junction overcomes DC limitations, exhibiting dual EL peaks per cycle via polarization effects for simplified AC-powered lighting.

title: Intrinsic physical properties of flexible van der Waals semiconductor InSe
abstract: By a tailored floating zone method, we successfully overcome thermodynamic challenges to obtain pure-phase InSe crystals for the first time. Our InSe closely approaches the intrinsic limit, establishing it as a benchmark material for future studies.

title: Enhancing the adhesion between self-assembled molecules and perovskites enabled 22.6% efficiency for flexible solar cells
abstract: We demonstrate that an optimal annealing temperature facilitates the formation of a dense self-assembled molecular layer, thereby enhancing interfacial adhesion and yielding F-PSCs with a high efficiency of 22.6% and excellent stability.

title: Rewritable large-area structural color films based on Mie scattering of Cu 2 O@SiO 2 microspheres
abstract: Spraying Cu 2 O@SiO 2 microspheres into a disordered array and encapsulating with PDMS yields a structural color film via Mie scattering. A liquid layer controls the dielectric environment for rewritable color paper.

title: Tailored thermally stable functionalization of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals for polymer nanocomposite scintillator fabrication
abstract: We present a novel tailorable functionalization strategy of cesium lead halide nanocrystals for the preparation of their nanocomposites. The application potential focuses on novel radiation detection concepts and other light emitting applications.

title: Nitrogen-Doped Hollow Carbon Microcubes Modified by in-Situ Catalytic Growth CNTs for Broadband Microwave Absorption
abstract: 3D hollow structural design and component regulation have been considered to bean effective strategy to obtain lightweight and wideband microwave absorption materials. Herein, nitrogen-doped hollow carbon microcubes modified by in-situ...

title: High-performance solution-combustion synthesized V 2 O 5 /NiO nanocomposites for supercapacitor applications: comparative electrochemical evaluation
abstract: A synergistic V 2 O 5 /NiO nanocomposite electrode facilitates efficient redox reactions and charge transport, delivering enhanced electrochemical performance and stable energy storage behavior.

title: Correction: Low-voltage, long-term stable organic heterojunction transistors with a broadband bidirectional photoresponse for in-sensor vision computing
abstract: Correction for ‘Low-voltage, long-term stable organic heterojunction transistors with a broadband bidirectional photoresponse for in-sensor vision computing’ by Yue Tian et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D6TC00018E.

title: Utilizing polymerizable ammonium as A-site cation to improve the stability of Mn-based perovskite in humid environments
abstract: Mn-based metal halide scintillators have emerged as a promising candidate for scintillators in X-ray imaging with attractive photophysical properties, tunable structure, cost-efficient processability, and low toxicity. However, their poor stability...

title: Unveiling the synergistic mechanism of the NiSe 2 /CoSeO 3 heterointerface in enhancing the oxygen evolution reaction kinetics
abstract: Transition metal selenides, due to their excellent conductivity and low cost, have become promising alternative materials.

title: Acid-reagent-engineered tunable multicolor carbon dots via solid-phase pyrolysis for advanced information encryption and anti-counterfeiting
abstract: Acid-reagent-engineered tunable multicolor carbon dots via solid-phase pyrolysis.

title: Crystallographic orientation-dependent anisotropic doping in organic single-crystal microplates
abstract: Using pentacene-doped 1,4-bis(4-methylstyryl)-benzene (p-MSB) as the model system, organic semiconductor single crystals manifest orientation-mediated spatially dual-color emission domains.

title: Orthogonal optical encoding via excitation-modulated core–shell perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: Lanthanide-doped nanocrystals with color-tunable upconversion luminescence under 980 and 1550 nm excitaion.

title: PEI-based nanocomposites with dual-functional complementary fillers for synergistic enhancement of energy storage performance
abstract: The nanocomposite with dual-functional fillers achieves a synergistic enhancement of dielectric constant and breakdown strength.

title: Synergistic enhancement of nonlinear optical limiting via in situ confined carbon nanodots in metal–porphyrinic framework thin films
abstract: An effective optical limiting device was prepared by impregnating a porous PIZA-1 MOF thin film with an o -phenylenediamine precursor followed by in situ conversion of the precursor into carbon nanodots (CDs) via laser irradiation.

title: Linkage isomers and their enantiomeric siblings as CPL-TADF emitters: modulating chirality and enhancing the dissymmetry factor using N- and O-linked carbazoles
abstract: The change in linking position of the carbazole impacts the luminescence dissymmetry factor.

title: On the photo and back isomerization of phenylazothiazoles anchored onto peptoids: one step further toward visible-light photoswitches for solar energy harnessing
abstract: Embedding visible-light-absorbing phenylazothiazole chromophores into peptoid frameworks preserves their photoabsorption while enabling tunable metastable isomer lifetimes.

title: Metal-driven interface engineering enables multi-functionality in SrTiO 3 memristor devices
abstract: Metal-dependent interface engineering in SrTiO 3 controls interfacial oxide formation and oxygen vacancy distribution. This enables reproducible enhancement of both resistive switching and unipolar NDR performance.

title: Symmetric transport in sub-5-nm monolayer Sn 2 S 2 -based homogeneous CMOS devices
abstract: Sub-5 nm homogeneous CMOS devices with ultra-high on-state current and pronounced n/p symmetry are achieved using a Sn 2 S 2 monolayer.

title: Regulating the compactness of Cu nanoclusters with assembly-induced emission properties for highly sensitive optical strain sensing
abstract: Highly sensitive strain sensing based on delicate structure–fluorescence correlation is achieved by regulating the compactness of Cu nanocluster assemblies with AIE properties and ligand-dependent compatibility in a PDMS matrix.

title: A multi-responsive pattern switchable PDLC anti-counterfeiting platform with instant IR/UV readout enabled via upconversion–fluorophore synergy and electrode addressing
abstract: A multi-responsive PDLC platform is developed for advanced anti-counterfeiting, featuring AC/IR/UV-triggered pattern/color switching and high-contrast optical performance, offering a robust strategy for multi-level information security.

title: Superior energy storage performance of PbZrO 3 -based films via phase regulation and entropy engineering
abstract: Synchronous improvement of recoverable energy density and efficiency in PbZrO 3 -based films via phase regulation and entropy engineering.

title: Design and fabrication of a strong absorption gradient multilayer honeycomb with wideband absorption
abstract: Inspired by multi-scale impedance matching, this study presents a gradient-coated honeycomb metamaterial. With a honeycomb thickness of only 30 mm, it achieves a absorption bandwidth of 2.2–18 GHz.

title: Sequential p-Type Doping of Polytriphenylamine-SWCNT Composites by Tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane for High-Performance Flexible Thermoelectrics
abstract: Organic thermoelectric materials offer sustainable alternatives to conventional inorganic systems but are limited by low electrical conductivity and weak thermal stability. Here, we demonstrate the first thermoelectric application of polytriphenylamine-single-walled...

title: Multi-state emission of TICT luminophores
abstract: In this work, we address the challenge of designing versatile luminophores that maintain their emissive properties across various phases, including solutions, crystalline, aggregated states, mechanically treated solids, and solid polymer matrices.

title: Tailoring Yb 3+ energy levels by local configuration in the garnet structure: the case of Ca 3 (NbGa□) 5 O 12 laser single crystals as a model for Yb:YAG
abstract: An increase in the cationic charge on the tetrahedral garnet site shifts the ytterbium level energy towards higher values.

title: Ferroelectric memristors optimized in thickness for short-term memory-driven reservoir computing
abstract: Thickness-optimized Mo/HZO/Si ferroelectric memristors show strong STM, high polarization, large TER, and self-rectification. A 5-nm HZO enables multilevel synapses and reservoir computing with 98.71% Hand-MNIST accuracy for in-sensor hardware.

title: Sulfonyl-locking and steric hindrance engineering achieve efficient blue multi-resonance TADF emitters
abstract: New blue MR-TADF emitters are developed by combining sulfonyl-locking and steric hindrance engineering on a carbonyl/nitrogen framework, exhibiting blue electroluminescence at 458–468 nm, narrow FWHMs of 25–30 nm, and EQE max s of 9.2–15.8%.

title: Progress in flexible inorganic near-infrared photodetectors: materials and applications
abstract: Progress in flexible inorganic nanomaterials near-infrared photodetectors: work principles, materials, applications and challenges.

title: Tuning mesomorphic, spectral and nonlinear optical behavior in chalcogenophene triads: the role of oxygen, sulfur, and selenium
abstract: Changing the chalcogen atom from O to S to Se tunes core geometry, mesophase formation, emission and NLO response in bichalcogenophene mesogens, offering design guidelines for multifunctional soft materials.

title: Acid-resistant bilayer MXene/attapulgite-g-C 3 N 4 /BiOBr aerogel for high-efficiency solar-driven interfacial evaporation and photocatalytic water treatment
abstract: Bilayered MXene/PPAL-CN/BiOBr aerogel with type-II heterojunction photocatalysis & solar evaporation: 1.59 kg m −2 h −1 (97.1%), acid resistance 1.50 kg m −2 h −1 in 3% H 2 SO 4 , 91% TC degradation/80 min, for treating TC-contaminated acidic wastewater.

title: Automatic hydrogen clathrate recognition neural networks for high-pressure superconductor prediction
abstract: A neural network automatically recognizes hydrogen clathrate structures and predicts the critical temperature of hydrogen-containing superconductors across a broad pressure range.

title: Enhancement of high-temperature piezoelectric properties in V/Mn co-doped Sr 2 Nb 2 O 7 ceramics
abstract: V/Mn co-doping enhances the intrinsic and extrinsic piezoelectric responses of Sr 2 Nb 2 O 7 ceramics through increased B-site octahedral distortion, spontaneous polarization, and domain-wall activity, giving d 33 = 3.5 pC N −1 .

title: Rationally constructed triple interfaces in Rh/Cu 3 P/MnP heterostructured electrocatalysts for enhanced overall water splitting
abstract: Rationally designed electrocatalysts with excellent catalytic activity and robust stability are essential for enhancing the performance of water splitting toward a near-future hydrogen economy.

title: A multimodal artificial sensory neuron realized by a bio-voltage-level threshold switching memristor
abstract: We fabricated a threshold switching memristor (TSM) with a structure of Pt/Ag/Al 2 O 3 /HfO 2 /Ag NIs/Pt. Building upon this TSM, we further developed an artificial sensory neuron with spike amplitude encoding capabilities.

title: On-demand detachable hydrogel electrodes with robust adhesion for epidermal electrophysiological monitoring
abstract: A thermo-responsive hydrogel based on P(AA- co -NIPAAM) with reversible adhesion property and painless detachment achieves high-fidelity electrophysiological monitoring, offering a promising strategy for next-generation wearable bioelectronic devices.

title: Unveiling rare earth dopant configurations and crystal field analysis in tetragonal BaTiO 3 via machine learning and superposition modeling
abstract: Machine-learning structural optimization combined with crystal-field modeling reveals systematic site preference, lattice distortion, and stabilization trends across the full rare-earth series in BaTiO 3 .

title: Quantifying anisotropic mobility enhancement in uniaxially aligned polythiophene films via ion-exchange doping
abstract: Ion-exchange doping of aligned polythiophene films achieves a 2–3 orders of magnitude enhancement in hole mobility along both backbone and π–π stacking directions, without compromising uniaxial alignment.

title: Room-temperature ultra-sensitive trimethylamine and ethylenediamine assays for fish freshness detection based on an imine-covalent organic framework
abstract: Chemically synthesised COF materials enable highly sensitive detection of trimethylamine and ethylenediamine at room temperature. Furthermore, this sensor is successfully deployed in practical applications for monitoring fish freshness.

title: Voltage-driven engineering for concurrent enhancement of ferroelectricity and endurance in HZO-based ferroelectric devices
abstract: This study establishes a systematic framework that enables precise evaluation of P r variations in fluorite-structure ferroelectrics, thereby an optimal operating voltage range is proposed to ensure stable endurance characteristics.

title: Deep blue thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitters with a 9,9′-spirobifluorene-fused xanthone acceptor for efficient OLEDs
abstract: Efficient deep blue thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitters are developed based on a 9,9′-spirobifluorene-fused xanthone acceptor, which furnish deep blue electroluminescence and high external quantum efficiencies of up to 16%.

title: Low dark current and type-II band alignment in a double perovskite single crystal Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /Cs 3 Bi 2 I 9 nanocrystal heterojunction enables high-performance photodetection
abstract: We report a lead-free perovskite heterojunction photodetector using Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 single crystal and Cs 3 Bi 2 I 9 nanocrystals, showing improved charge separation and reduced dark current due to built-in electric field at the interface.

title: Green Synthesis of Manganese-Based Anti-Perovskite with Anti-Thermal Quenching Properties for LED
abstract: Due to their excellent properties, lead halide perovskites are promising candidates for future optical emitters. However, lead (Pb) toxicity in perovskite materials poses a critical concern for their commercial application...

title: An InGaSnO synaptic device array for in-sensor convolution and nondestructive defect detection
abstract: An optoelectronic synaptic array based on InGaSnO nanowire networks integrates hardware convolution with reservoir computing, enabling joint spatial-temporal processing for efficient neuromorphic perception and learning.

title: Two magnesium based metal-organic frameworks (Mg-MOFs): Structures, photochromism, and fluorescence sensing to Fe3+ ions and cysteine molecules
abstract: Here in this work, two magnesium based metal organic frameworks (Mg-MOFs) assembled from 1,4-naphthalinic acid (1,4-H2NDC) and pyridine-containing ligands of 1,2-di(4-pyridyl)ethylene (L1) or 2-di(pyridin-4-yl)diazene (L2) are presented and characterized. In...

title: Near Infrared Electroluminescence in Light-emitting Electrochemical Cells from Binuclear Copper(I) Complexes bearing π-extended Benzimidazole and Benzothiazole Ligands
abstract: The design of compounds that efficiently emits into the deep-red to near-infrared (NIR) region is still highly challenging, yet they can play pivotal role in optoelectronic devices for phototherapy, encryption...

title: Rational tuning of naphthalene diimide (NDI)-thienoisoindigo (TIIG) copolymer compositions for high-performance all-polymer photodetectors with broadband response from 300 nm to 1050 nm
abstract: Through the rational regulation of the content of thiophene isoindigo groups in NDI/TIIG copolymers, high-performance broadband responses of all-polymer photodetectors can be realized.

title: FRET-relayed magneto-photoluminescence driven by triplet-pair dynamics in organic films
abstract: We establish energy transfer as a general route to decouple magnetic-field responsiveness from the emitting chromophore, relaying triplet-pair-mediated magneto-photoluminescence into spin-silent organic materials.

title: Arbitrary-ordered pulsed programming achieving 11 well-separated programming levels via a multilevel transistor–memristor series configuration
abstract: A memristor device exhibits multilevel resistance states with excellent endurance and retention. Arbitrary-ordered programming enables precise and flexible access to 11 well-separated program levels, advancing high-density memory applications.

title: High-efficiency organic solar cells enabled by non-ionic small-molecule cathode interlayers with tunable alkoxy side chains
abstract: We developed two non-fused molecular cathode interlayers. With side-chain engineering, M-tOR achieved a superior PCE of 15.36% in PM6:Y6 OSCs, outperforming PDINO (15.12%), demonstrating the potential of non-fused materials for high-performance OSCs.

title: Achieving narrowband deep-blue electroluminescence (CIE y = 0.04) via a fluorinated and sterically modulated MR-TADF emitter
abstract: Strategic fluorination and terminal modification yield a Rec.2020 deep-blue MR-TADF emitter (CIE y = 0.040) with a high reverse intersystem crossing rate of 2.32 × 10 5 s −1 via lithium-free borylation.

title: Achieving high field-effect mobility in CuSCN thin-film transistors with thiocyanate-functionalized polymers as fluorine-free dielectrics
abstract: Fluorine-free polymers containing a thiocyanate group are synthesized, yielding a high dielectric constant of ∼18. When used as dielectrics in CuSCN transistors, a high mobility is obtained, highlighting the chemical compatibility at the interface.

title: Liquid crystal tetramers and spontaneous chirality: the heliconical twist-bend nematic phase
abstract: New liquid crystal tetramers exhibiting the twist-bend nematic phase are studied in terms of their structure–property relationships. The effects of changing their three flexible spacers’ length, parity and nature on phase behavior are analyzed.

title: Dual-functional Cr 3+ -doped far-red to NIR broadband phosphors for high-fidelity white lighting and non-destructive detection
abstract: A novel Cr 3+ -doped phosphor with UV-blue excitation and far-red/NIR emission ensures excellent compatibility with UV/blue chips, enabling the generation of high-fidelity white light and NIR radiation for non-destructive detection.

title: All-solution-processed transparent electrodes consisting of Ag NW/PEDOT:PSS composites on PVDF-HFP flexible substrates for 2.5 V-operating wearable heaters
abstract: Flexible transparent electrodes with mechanical flexibility have attracted increased interest as next-generation wearable heaters.

title: Significantly enhanced high-temperature power generation of BiScO 3 –PbTiO 3 ceramics by rationally regulating atmosphere sintering
abstract: Reasonable atmosphere sintering helps to enhance the piezoelectric and insulating properties of BS–PT ceramics, bringing excellent high-temperature power generation capacity.

title: Nonvolatile switching of intra- and interlayer magnetism in 2D CrSe 2 /Sc 2 CO 2 multiferroic heterostructures
abstract: We demonstrate a robust magnetoelectric coupling enabled by ferroelectric polarization switching in 1L- and 2L-CrSe 2 /Sc 2 CO 2 vdW heterostructures, which provides a viable route toward electrically controlled magnetism in low-dimensional systems.

title: Ferroelectric and hyper dielectric modes in ferronematic liquid crystals
abstract: Binary mixtures of ferroelectric nematic DIO and the superparaelectric compound WJ-16. Mixtures display the usual ferroelectric and new hyper-dielectric mode. Both phases exhibit giant dielectric permittivity.

title: Buried interface defect regulation enables efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: A 30 mV V OC improvement is achieved by incorporating p -ClPA at the perovskite/Al 2 O 3 buried interface, enabling stable and efficient inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: Enhancing the flexural strength of 96-alumina ceramics to improve the anti-burst capability of capacitive pressure sensors
abstract: This study proposes a feasible method to increase the flexural strength of 96-alumina ceramics by combining a Ce 4+ -doped ZrO 2 reinforcement phase and a kyanite coating, enhancing the burst resistance in ceramic-based capacitive pressure sensors.

title: One-step decomposition pathway for solution-derived metal oxide layers driven by hydroxyl radical atmospheres
abstract: Hydroxyl radicals (˙OH) atmospheres are employed to drive a one-step decomposition pathway in solution-derived oxide films, allowing film crystallization at low temperatures.

title: A disordered–ordered TiO 2 /TiO 2− x homostructure hosting a high-mobility interfacial conductive layer for robust symmetric rectification
abstract: Low-cost chemical vapor reduction (CVR) creates a disordered TiO 2 /ordered TiO 2− x homostructural interface that hosts a vacancy-driven interfacial conductive layer, enabling ultrahigh rectification in a strictly symmetric Au/TiO 2 /TiO 2− x /TiO 2 /Au device.

title: Precise site occupation of Zn2+ in Rb2CuBr3 to regulate exciton recombination for violet luminescence
abstract: With its tunable bandgap and 385 nm violet emission, Rb2CuBr3 is a promising candidate for violet light-emitting diodes (LEDs). This work provides how Zn2+ doping enhances self-trapped exciton (STE) emission...

title: 6-Bit multilevel and highly linear synaptic plasticity in sputtered AlScN ferroelectric capacitors on (100) Si
abstract: Fully CMOS-compatible sputtered AlScN ferroelectric capacitors deliver 64 conductance states with near-linear weight updates, achieving 94.72% MNIST accuracy and validating their potential for large-scale neuromorphic computing.

title: Mechanical stability of flexible perovskite solar cells: challenges, strategies, and prospects
abstract: Flexible perovskite solar cells (F-PSCs) have an efficiency exceeding 25%, featuring both light weight and the advantage of roll-to-roll production. However, their mechanical stability is insufficient, which hinders their commercial application.

title: Atmospheric pressure chemical vapor deposition growth of large-area monolayer ReS 2 films for high-performance photodetectors
abstract: Optimized APCVD yields continuous monolayer ReS 2 films. The annealed ReS 2 /GaSe heterojunction photodetector exhibits exceptional performance with an ultrahigh R λ of 48.22 A W −1 and an exceptional EQE of 1.33 × 10 4 %.

title: Compact and functional co-self-assembled monolayers for efficient inverted wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: A cyanation-functionalized self-assembled monolayer material, CN-4PACz, was developed to construct a compact and functional co-assembled monolayer material as the hole transport layer for efficient wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells.

title: Red-to-NIR luminescent trinuclear gold( i ) complexes with exceptionally wide liquid crystalline temperature range
abstract: Luminescent trinuclear Au( i ) complexes integrated into liquid crystalline (LC) frameworks provide a promising platform for developing anisotropic and stimuli-responsive soft materials.

title: Dual-amino nitrobenzene functionalization enables graphene quantum dots for selective UV and high-energy blue-light filtering
abstract: Dual-amino nitrobenzene-functionalized graphene quantum dots enable precise UV/HEBL filtering. The transparent composites block harmful light while preserving clarity and color accuracy, converting cool light to warm illumination.

title: Molecular origin of UV-induced irreversible phase changes in a chromonic liquid crystal
abstract: UV-induced photodegradation of disodium cromoglycate (DSCG) disrupts molecular aggregates, irreversibly expanding the isotropic and nematic–isotropic biphasic regions in its chromonic phase diagram.

title: Balancing charge transport and C–N bond strength in stability-oriented host design for blue TADF-OLEDs
abstract: A series of carbazole–biphenyl hosts with tuned charge transport and high triplet energies is developed for blue TADF-OLEDs. N -Phenylation shifts host LUMO from a weak C–N bond, raising anionic-state BDE and boosting device lifetime up to 12×.

title: Phthalocyanine and single-walled carbon nanotube-decorated hybrid material: design, photophysical properties and high-performance supercapacitor applications
abstract: Phthalocyanine-decorated carbon nanotubes are promising materials for supercapacitor applications due to their high capacitance, excellent power performance, and exceptional cycling stability.

title: Halogen functionalization-induced modulation of ferromagnetism and electronic phases in CrXY monolayers
abstract: Halogen functionalization enables effective tuning of electronic and magnetic properties in Janus CrXY monolayers, enhancing ferromagnetism and Curie temperature for advanced spintronic applications.

title: Shape memory-assisted anticounterfeiting polymer platforms
abstract: In advanced shape memory-assisted anticounterfeiting polymeric materials, stimuli-induced shape change resulting from the shape memory effect is used as one security mode, while other smart phenomena serve as additional alarm modes. Image of cubic structure generated with AI.

title: Soft-mode-mediated coherent semiconductor–metal transition in ReS 2 induced by charge doping
abstract: ReS 2 undergoes a semiconductor–metal phase transition from the 1T″ to the 1T′ phase under hole doping. The underlying mechanism involves the co-evolution of the potential energy surface and coherent phonons with doping.

title: The magic of semi-perfluorinated mixtures: engineering MR-TADF emission from host–guest mixtures of liquid crystals
abstract: Strong MR-TADF emission at room temperature was achieved by perfect integration of a liquid crystalline (LC) emitter in a columnar hexagonal LC host mesophase, resulting in efficient energy transfer from host to emitter via FRET.

title: Partially self-healing flexible light-emitting diodes based on InGaN/GaN microwires
abstract: A self-healing LED architecture that integrates three main components as blue-emitting InGaN/GaN core–shell microwires, self-healing polydimethylsiloxane, and single-walled carbon nanotube electrodes positioned on the silicone matrix was developed.

title: Biphenylene molecules with iron atoms: new half-metallic ferromagnetic MOF for advanced spintronic devices
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) represent a versatile class of materials with tunable physical properties, including magnetism.

title: Reducing the cell-to-module performance gap of inorganic perovskites using anisotropically structured hole transport materials
abstract: Compared with conventional Spiro-OMeTAD, the anisotropic SF-MPA-MCz hole transport material reduces the cell-to-module performance gap when scaling all-inorganic perovskite solar cells from lab-scale devices to large-area modules.

title: AuPd heterostructured nanoplates: in vitro near-infrared photothermal agents for combating multidrug-resistant bacteria
abstract: AuPd heterostructured nanoplates exhibit high photothermal conversion efficiency (up to 64.7%) for combating drug-resistant bacteria in vitro , a result of optimized electronic synergy and strain at the Au/Pd interface.

title: Highly sensitive triethylamine gas sensors enabled by hamburger-like GO/ZnO heterostructures
abstract: The rationally designed, hierarchical hamburger-like ZnO/GO heterojunction results in greatly enhanced TEA sensing, delivering outstanding response, excellent selectivity, and an ultra-low detection limit.

title: Trehalose-enhanced anti-freezing conductive hydrogels with high stretchability and low hysteresis for flexible human–machine interaction
abstract: Conductive hydrogels exhibit excellent stretchability, low hysteresis, anti-freezing properties, and environmental stability, making them suitable for applications in flexible strain sensors and human–machine interaction.

title: Spray-printing piezoresistive sensors based on poly( d , l -lactide- co -glycolide) copolymer composites for urinary catheter applications
abstract: This work demonstrates the implementation of biodegradable and biocompatible natural polymer based piezoresistive sensors in urinary catheters.

title: Persistent photoconductivity enables in-sensor binarization and reservoir computing in a single organic phototransistor
abstract: Time-dependent binarization (TDB) transforms non-uniform spectral responses from defects to computational resources, enabling a single organic phototransistor to perform both in-sensor preprocessing and reservoir computing (RC) with 96.54% accuracy.

title: Designing adaptive materials for 3D-printing via dynamic covalent imine bonds
abstract: 3D-printable acrylates bearing amine groups are reported allowing the dynamic tuning of the properties of the 3D-printed objects (surface polarity, emission behavior) via imine condensation and exchange reaction on the surface of the samples.

title: Additive-free low-temperature densification and dielectric behavior of combustion-derived nanocrystalline CaTiO 3 for LTCC applications
abstract: Additive-free combustion synthesis yields nanocrystalline CaTiO 3 enabling dense microstructures at 894 °C with enhanced dielectric properties for LTCC applications.

title: Ligand-directed growth control for high-performance short-wave infrared quantum dot photodetectors
abstract: 1-Octanethiol (OT)-modified PbS quantum dots (QDs) exhibit enhanced monodispersity, reduced defects, and octahedral-like morphologies, enabling high-performance SWIR photodetectors with high EQE and specific detectivity.

title: Synergistic modulation of thermoelectric performance in SnTe via Ge alloying and Sb doping
abstract: This work demonstrates a novel synergistic strategy combining Ge alloying with Sb doping to achieve both low thermal conductivity and a high power factor in SnTe, thereby rendering it a promising candidate for advanced thermoelectric applications.

title: An interfacial electric field strategy for improving the performance of perovskite X-ray detectors
abstract: An interfacial electric field strategy effectively enhances the performance of perovskite X-ray detectors.

title: Synergistic bimetallic PtPd catalysts supported on Zn 2 SnO 4 /SnO 2 nanocubes for improved formaldehyde gas-sensing performance
abstract: PtPd-decorated Zn 2 SnO 4 /SnO 2 nanocubes were synthesized to improve formaldehyde gas-sensing performance through the synergistic catalytic effect of bimetallic PtPd catalysts.

title: Molecular dual-functionalization unlocks room-temperature phosphorescence of graphitic carbon nitride for environmentally robust encryption
abstract: Naphthyl–phenyl dual functionalization unlocks room-temperature phosphorescence of graphitic carbon nitride, which shows great application potential in complex information encryption scenarios.

title: Multimodal mechanoluminescence regulation and mechanism study of CaYAl 3 O 7 :Eu 2+ ,Eu 3+ under light irradiation
abstract: It realizes stress-induced transient luminescence, stress-induced color-changing luminescence, and stress-induced afterglow luminescence within a single host.

title: Thiol-functionalized carbon dot-enabled self-catalytic reduction-dynamic assembly for constructing three-dimensional porous reduced graphene oxide aerogels toward high-performance terahertz modulation
abstract: SH–CDs are conducive to the formation of rGO aerogels with a three-dimensional porous network structure, which achieving a maximum reflection loss of −37 dB and shielding effectiveness of −44 dB, with average values of −26 and −37 dB.

title: A superhydrophobic bilayer anisotropic conductive film with improved reliability for electronic interconnects
abstract: A superhydrophobic bilayer anisotropic conductive film (S-ACF) achieves 150° water contact angle ( vs. 64° for the pristine ACF) and maintains stable Z -axis resistance under humid-heat reliability (85 °C/85% RH, extending from ∼150 to ∼300 h).

title: Construction of 3D and 2D perovskites with N -methylpropane-1,3-diammonium as the organic cation and superior interlayer charge transport in the 3D perovskite single crystal photodetector
abstract: The planar-type photodetector achieves superior responsivity and detectivity along the [001] crystal orientation.

title: Seed-mediated tuning of branch size in vertically aligned branched silver nanoplates for large-area and highly sensitive SERS substrates
abstract: The controllable fabrication of vertically aligned branched Ag nanoplates enables dense plasmonic hotspots and large-area SERS substrates with high sensitivity and reproducibility.

title: Electronic structure engineering of NiFe layered double hydroxide via first-row transition-metal doping for efficient electrochemical oxidation of sulfamethazine in food samples
abstract: Layered double hydroxides (LDHs) are promising electrocatalysts due to their excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Incorporation of Al 3+ into turbostratic graphite: an efficient route toward improved thermal and electrical conductivity
abstract: Creation of carbide crystals in turbostratic graphite sheets greatly improves thermal and electrical conductivity and imrpovement is due to the lengthened mean free path of phonons.

title: Anisotropy in bulk-like lead halide perovskite nanorods triggers circularly polarized photoluminescence
abstract: The degree of polarization of the bulk-like S-NRs is higher at room temperature, and S-NRs embedded on Light-emitting diode chips demonstrate promising potential as circularly polarized luminescence emitters.

title: Neodymium chloride doping and homogeneous passivation in CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals for efficient blue light-emitting diodes
abstract: A significant advancement in blue PeLED performance (15.74% EQE) was achieved by employing NdCl 3 doping and surface passivation to improve crystal quality and reduce defects, paving the way for practical applications in displays and lighting.

title: Factors affecting the resistivity and colossal magnetoresistance (CMR) of manganite materials
abstract: Perovskite manganites exhibit unique phenomena such as phase diversity and colossal magnetoresistance (CMR), originating from the strong interplay between charge, spin, orbital, and lattice degrees of freedom.

title: (C 4 H 8 N 5 )(PhPHO 2 )·H 2 O: a polar ionic cocrystal with strong second-harmonic generation and high birefringence
abstract: A polar ionic cocrystal, (C 4 H 8 N 5 )(PhPHO 2 )·H 2 O, exhibits strong second-harmonic generation, a high laser damage threshold, a wide band gap, and high birefringence, highlighting phosphinate salts as promising ultraviolet materials.

title: Preparation of a 2D WS 2 /MoS 2 heterostructure via S-vacancy doping and its application in ultrafast laser modulation
abstract: WS 2 /MoS 2 -based SAs are prepared using the S-vacancy regulation method. The nonlinear optical properties of WS 2 /MoS 2 -based SAs are investigated through first-principles calculations and Z-scan experiments.

title: Structural effects of the SnO 2 electron transport layer on the reliability of perovskite solar cells
abstract: The fundamental difference between sputtered SnO 2 and solution processed colloidal SnO 2 as an electron transport layer is systematically investigated, and the critical roles of their interfacial properties in device performance are revealed.

title: Lanthanide upconverting luminescence in molecular complexes and metal–organic frameworks
abstract: The review summarizes advances in lanthanide upconverting luminescence from complexes and MOFs, addressing mechanisms, structure–property relationships, strategies, challenges, and opportunities for improving efficiency, stability, and applications.

title: Surface transfer n-type doping of graphene using WO 3 nanoparticles prepared without the use of hazardous chemicals
abstract: Chemically free-prepared WO 3 nanoparticles induce strong doping in single-layer graphene, enabling tunable electronic properties for advanced optoelectronic and energy applications.

title: Molecularly engineered nitrogen-enriched and solid-state emissive carbon dots for visual zeptomole explosive tracing
abstract: A straightforward method yields intrinsically solid-state fluorescent N-Cdots for zeptomole level detection of nitroaromatic explosives, with excellent stability and high selectivity.

title: Benzothiadiazole-based donor–acceptor-type covalent organic frameworks for effective heterogeneous photocatalytic aerobic cycloaddition reaction
abstract: Benzothiadiazole-based covalent organic frameworks exhibit exceptional crystallinity and superior photoelectric properties, enabling them to serve as highly efficient heterogeneous photocatalysts for the synthesis of tetrahydroquinolines.

title: Shifting shades: a polymorph-driven luminescence modulation in a dual-state emissive organic luminophore
abstract: Herein, we report a dual-state emissive (DSE) luminophore, DCZHPI, bearing a phenanthroimidazole (PI) core with carbazole substitution.

title: A self-powered artificial optoelectronic synapse based on lead-free organic–inorganic hybrid molecular ferroelectric [C 4 N 2 H 14 ][BiI 5 ]
abstract: A self-powered optoelectronic synapse based on lead-free ferroelectric BDA-BiI 5 emulates biological synaptic functions and associative learning, positioning BDA-BiI 5 as a promising material for energy-efficient neuromorphic vision systems.

title: Twist-angle programmable magnetism in graphene/CrI 3 bilayers
abstract: Graphene shows moiré spin densities, a spin-polarization ratio, spin-polarized minigaps, and a Zeeman splitting by proximity effects with the CrI 3 monolayer; conversely, graphene induces a magnetic exchange enhancement in the CrI 3 monolayer.

title: Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction engineering for field-free ultra-small skyrmions in alkali-functionalized monolayer Cr 2 Te 3
abstract: Alkali-metal adsorption on monolayer Cr 2 Te 3 induces a highly tunable, layer-dependent DMI. This unique mechanism successfully stabilizes field-free, ultra-small Néel skyrmions with a diameter of 4 nm.

title: Chemical and physical pressure meet: deciphering the polymorphism and morphology of α- and δ-KY 3 F 10 induced by Eu 3+ doping
abstract: HRSEM images of KY 3 F 10 polymorphs and the morphological connection.

title: Electrical double-layer based wearable tensile and pressure sensors: optimizing materials and architecture for improved sensing response
abstract: A materials-to-architecture design rule for iontronic textiles optimizes dense films for strain and porous scaffolds for pressure, enabling motion discrimination.

title: Ultrasensitive detection of hazardous substances using a SERS platform based on gold-nanostar–silica aerogel nanocomposites
abstract: A 3D gold nanostar–silica aerogel (AuNS–SA) SERS substrate was successfully fabricated via suspension self-assembly, and its detection performance for various trace organic pollutants was systematically evaluated.

title: Hydrothermal synthesis and comprehensive DFT evaluation of Ba 2 PrMnO 6 double perovskite nanocrystals for next-generation spintronic and optoelectronic technologies
abstract: An Integrated experimental–DFT analysis of Ba 2 PrMnO 6 , having an energy bandgap of ∼1.98 eV with spin-polarized electronic properties. Structural distortion enhances absorption and dielectric response for spintronic and optoelectronic uses.

title: n-Type and p-type co-doping for optimizing turn-on and threshold fields of SiC towards enhanced field emission properties
abstract: Turn-on field ( E to ) and threshold electric field ( E thr ) are regarded as typical indexes for fabricating better field emission (FE) devices.

title: Dimensionality-regulated Hofmann MOF-derived 3D core–shell CoNi/C composites for high-performance broadband microwave absorption
abstract: 3D CoNi@C composites derived from dimensionality-regulated Hofmann MOFs exhibit synergistic microwave attenuation via conduction, magnetic, and interfacial losses, with an RL min of −59.4 dB and a broad EAB of 8.04 GHz.

title: Adhesion enhancement between colorless biphenyl polyimide copolymerized film and magnetron sputtering Cu for FPC circuit board
abstract: Polyimide (PI) film is one of the world's leading thin film insulation materials, which can be used in electronic applications in the aerospace, medical, and automotive industries.

title: Magnetic structure of layered Co 2 B 2 O 5 and the role of the bridging B 2 O 5 anions in three-dimensional magnetic ordering
abstract: The 3D magnetic structure of layered Co 2 B 2 O 5 consists of the ferromagnetic ribbons network antiferromagnetically coupled via pyroborate anions (B 2 O 5 ) 4− . A small magnetic anisotropy enables magnetic-field control of the magnetic structure.

title: Neodymium-engineered relaxor bismuth ferrite nanoparticles: structural tuning and dielectric enhancement for efficient sensor applications
abstract: This paper investigates the synthesis and properties of neodymium-doped bismuth ferrite (BiFeO 3 ) nanoparticles, highlighting their enhanced functionality for advanced applications.

title: High-resolution direct laser writing nanolithography enabled by oxidation-mediated corrosion selectivity in N-doped Sb heat-mode resists
abstract: The NSb thin film is proposed as a positive-tone resist suitable for direct laser writing and dry etching, offering a developing selectivity of 6.1 and a Si-to-NSb selectivity of 34.0. Features of 60 nm are patterned with a period of 160 nm.

title: A supramolecular strategy for the control of thermal expansion in molecular crystals
abstract: Wheel-axle-type supramolecular cations assemble into two-dimensional layers via C–H⋯π interchain interactions, suppressing crown ether rotation and delivering uniaxial zero thermal expansion between 110 and 190 K.

title: Entropy-mediated Pb doping in tungsten bronze (Sr 0.5 Ba 0.3 La 0.2 ) 1− x Pb x Nb 2 O 6− δ oxides for synergistic electron–phonon transport optimization
abstract: Entropy-mediated Pb doping in tungsten bronze oxides achieves a record ZT of 0.42 at 1073 K through synergistic electron–phonon transport optimization, offering a scalable strategy for high-performance oxide thermoelectric materials.

title: Single-atom catalysts as Frontier materials for advanced gas sensors
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have attracted wide interest in the field of gas sensing, owing to their high atomic utilization efficiency, distinctive electronic structures, and exceptional catalytic performance.

title: The key role of soft corona in the superlattice formation of CeO 2 nanoparticles
abstract: The fabrication of superlattices is nontrivial because nanoparticles are notoriously difficult to employ due to the complex nanoscale forces among them.

title: Advances in fluorescence-based point-of-care diagnostics: probes, nanostructures and integrated systems
abstract: Fluorescent probes, nanomaterials, and integrated platforms drive next-generation point-of-care diagnostics with enhanced brightness, stability, multiplexing, and portable sensing performance.

title: Synergistic mesoporous silica confinement and PMMA encapsulation enabling highly stable CsPbX 3 composite films for wide-color-gamut backlighting
abstract: SBA-15 confinement and PMMA sealing synergistically suppress anion exchange and improve water and spectral stability in CsPbX 3 tricolor systems. The fabricated white LEDs achieve 131% NTSC gamut, showing promising potential for backlight displays.

title: Stabilization of Twisted-Rope Like Domains in Cholesteric Mesophase formed by Novel Cholesterol based Liquid Crystal Molecule

title: Enhancing proton irradiation tolerance of a-IGZO thin-film transistors through hydrogen doping
abstract: Hydrogen doping enhances the radiation tolerance of a-IGZO TFTs by suppressing oxygen vacancies and compensating excess carriers, enabling stable operation under high-energy proton irradiation.

title: Modulating polymorphic phase boundaries and defect chemistry in KNN-based piezoceramics through multi-element doping strategies
abstract: Lead-free potassium–sodium niobate ceramic represents a promising alternative to replace lead-based piezoelectrics in energy harvesting and actuator applications due to their high Curie temperature and favorable ferroelectric properties.

title: Hierarchically porous carbon derived from waste watermelon rind for enhanced microwave absorption
abstract: Waste watermelon rind-derived porous carbon achieves broadband and strong wave absorption at low filling ratios and demonstrates high adaptability and functional diversity in the design of multilayer impedance matching structures.

title: Improved green synthesis of manganese-based halide perovskites via high-energy ball milling and their multiple applications
abstract: Manganese-based halide perovskite hosts were synthesized via an improved high-energy ball-milling method using low-cost MnBr 2 ·4H 2 O as the raw material, exhibiting excellent optical properties, thermal stability and photostability, and response to multiple stimuli.

title: Crystal structure and microwave dielectric properties of CaCuGe 2 O 6 pyroxene-type ceramics
abstract: Pure-phase CCG ceramics were obtained, and their structure (XRD/SEM) and dielectric properties were assessed versus sintering temperature. Optimal performance ( ε r = 8.86, Q × f = 21 301 GHz, τ f = −90.26 ppm °C −1 ) was at 1020 °C.

title: Structure–properties correlation in (1 − x )Bi 0.90 Pr 0.10 FeO 3 –( x )Ca 0.5 Sr 0.5 TiO 3 : phase evolution, optical, and electrical properties
abstract: Structural analysis of BPFO–CSTO ceramics revealed the coexistence of the R 3 c–Pnma phases with increasing orthorhombic fraction. Electrical studies indicate hopping/polaron conduction, with a reduced bandgap, suggesting photovoltaic potential.

title: Ultralow detection limit X-ray detectors based on Rb 4 Li 2 TiOGe 4 O 12 single crystals with anisotropic responses
abstract: The anisotropic X-ray responses of RLTG were systematically investigated based on X - and Z -cut RLTG detectors. The ultralow detection limit of 0.927 nGy s −1 for the Z -cut RLTG detector was a record value among all reported X-ray detectors.

title: TiO 2 -driven enhancement of electrochromic performance of polymer electrolytes: insights and mechanisms

title: Efficient carbon dot-based phosphorescent materials with time-dependent color-changing through surface modification

title: Effects of altermagnetic order, strain, and doping in RuO 2
abstract: We compare how altermagnetic and nonmagnetic order, together with epitaxial strain, influences the electronic, vibrational and optical properties of RuO 2 , using a combination of experiment and theory.

title: Reconfigurable ambipolar non-volatile transistors for fuzzy logic-enabled edge computing

title: Tuning from nanoparticles to nanorods: experimental and theoretical investigation of La-doped CdTe for enhanced photoresponse

title: Recent advances in perovskite nanocrystal light-emitting diodes: synthesis, device engineering, and film processing

title: Light, spin, and flow in a soft nanocomposite: coupling between magnetic, mechanical, and optical properties via hysteretic spin crossover at room temperature
abstract: A soft nanocomposite with spin-crossover, fluorescence, and viscoelastic properties shows reversible changes in optical and mechanical behavior driven by room-temperature magnetic switching.

abstract: Differences in solvent selection for small molecules and polymers used in inkjet printing and the compatible binary ink formulations.

abstract: Bismuth ferrite–graphene (BFO–Gr) nanocomposites with controlled graphene layers (3–5 and 5–8 layers) were synthesized by sonication method and comprehensively investigated for their structural, magnetic, and piezo-photocatalytic properties.

abstract: Circular dichroism and circularly polarized luminescence were used to characterize DJ-phase 2D perovskites based on 4-AMP. Spin-OLEDs and perovskite LEDs were also realized.

title: Iron–cobalt/carbon nanocomposites with adjustable impedance matching and a wide effective absorption bandwidth as outstanding microwave absorbers
abstract: The FeCo/CNF composite exhibits outstanding comprehensive absorption performance with low filler loadings of 20 wt%.

title: The role of the donor in the light-induced degradation of Y6 non-fullerene acceptors in PM6:Y6 blend films
abstract: Light-induced degradation in PM6:Y6 blend films through electron and energy transfer.

abstract: We carried out a systematic investigation on the magnetic properties, microstructure, and corrosion behavior of grain boundary diffused magnets using Dy 100− x Al x , Dy 100− x Ni x , and Dy 100− x Cu x ( x = 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, at%) alloys as diffusion sources.

title: A wearable and flexible sensor based on laser-induced graphene/Kevlar as a smart glove for table tennis gesture recognition and training

title: Wide-spectrum self-powered photoelectric detection based on the type-II heterostructure of MnPSe 3 /MoS 2

title: Boosting SERS performance of ZnO via Er 3+ doping: enhanced carrier density and charge transfer

abstract: As the demand for extreme-temperature-pressure exploration grows, the pressure stability of relaxor ferroelectrics, the backbone of piezoelectric sensors, emerges as a decisive factor.

title: A terahertz quadband metasurface absorber with enhanced sensitivity for biosensing applications
abstract: A compact quadband THz metasurface biosensor is proposed for biomedical sensing. Its multi-resonant design provides high absorption, enhanced sensitivity, angular stability, and effective discrimination of healthy, malignant, and infected samples.

title: Lattice engineering mediated fast decay in Li 2 HfF 6 :Mn 4+ with dual-excitation-band responsive differential emission for fingerprinting, anti-counterfeiting, and optical signal transmission
abstract: A Mn 4+ -activated Li 2 HfF 6 phosphor integrates shortened decay kinetics with dual-band excitation intensity differentiation, enabling red-channel optical encoding for fingerprinting, time-domain anti-counterfeiting, and LED-based signal transmission.

title: Development of visible-to-NIR light absorbing and emitting polymers incorporating hypervalent germafluorene-fused π-conjugated systems
abstract: NIR light absorbing and emitting hypervalent germafluorene-fused π-conjugated polymers featuring hypervalent germanium spiro centers were developed.

title: Spin-correlation driven ferroelectric quantum criticality in a perovskite quantum spin-liquid system, Ba 3 CuSb 2 O 9
abstract: Here, we report the first experimental demonstration of spin correlation driven ferroelectric quantum criticality in a quantum spin liquid, Ba 3 CuSb 2 O 9 , which is analogous to Multiferroics-II in the quantum critical regime.

title: “Colorless to other colors” electrochromic polymer materials: structural design strategies and current development status
abstract: Electrochromic materials have attracted extensive attention due to their ability to achieve rapid and reversible regulation of optical properties under low-voltage driving.

title: The effect of Ag + on the thermoelectric properties of Sm 3+ - and Ti 4+ - co-doped KSr 2 Nb 5 O 15 ceramics: a combined first-principles and experimental study on synergistic optimization of thermal and electrical transport
abstract: Ag + doping in KSNST induces lattice anharmonicity and Umklapp scattering, and the point defects and oxygen vacancies act as effective phonon scatter centres, significantly reducing thermal conductivity, especially at high doping levels.

abstract: An organic–inorganic hybrid ionic dielectric layer was developed. Under pressure, these effects trigger ion state transitions and rapid redistribution, enhancing electrical double layer formation.
